records.title: General Warehousing and Storage
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators of general merchandise warehouses and distribution facilities that repeatedly provide storage, handling, inventory control, pick-pack, and related fulfillment services to external customers. Business customers buy recurring storage capacity and transaction-based receiving, handling, picking, packing, shipping, clerical, and accessorial services; contracts commonly combine monthly minimums with per-unit and hourly charges.

records.title: Other Accounting Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market non-CPA bookkeeping, outsourced accounting, billing, controller, financial reporting, and related repeat services supplied to external customers, including mixed practices that also provide tax return preparation or payroll alongside accounting services. Primarily small and midsize business clients purchasing monthly or otherwise repeat bookkeeping, close, reporting, billing, and controller support under hourly, fixed-fee, subscription, or bundled arrangements; project cleanup and advisory work may supplement recurring fees.

records.title: Other Management Consulting Services
records.search_text: US other-management-consulting firms in the $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly provide outsourced advice, analysis, recommendations, facilitation, and implementation support to external organizations, including telecommunications and utilities management consulting. Business, nonprofit, and government clients buy scoped advisory projects, fixed-fee deliverables, time-and-materials work, value-priced engagements, and recurring retainers; repeat revenue arises from continuing client relationships, successive initiatives, and implementation support.

records.title: Executive Search Services
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market executive-search firms providing recurring or repeat retained, exclusive, or contingency recruitment and placement services for executive and senior-management roles to external business, nonprofit, and public-sector clients. Customers engage firms for senior-leadership searches. Revenue is commonly a fixed retainer linked to estimated first-year candidate compensation, sometimes supplemented by completion-related adjustments, reimbursed expenses, or contingency fees; repeat work depends on client relationships, sector specialization, and successful placements.

records.title: Custom Computer Programming Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced custom software design, development, modification, testing, integration, maintenance, and support to external customers. Business and government customers purchase custom projects, agile development capacity, dedicated teams, enhancements, maintenance, and support under time-and-materials, labor-hour, fixed-price, milestone, retainer, or managed-capacity arrangements.

records.title: Offices of Certified Public Accountants
records.search_text: US CPA firms in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that provide recurring or repeat audit and assurance, tax compliance and advisory, financial-statement preparation, client accounting, and related accounting advice to external customers. Recurring annual tax and assurance engagements and repeat advisory projects for businesses, nonprofits, and individuals, with a mix of hourly or time-based, flat-fee, project, value-based, and retainer billing.

records.title: Insurance Agencies and Brokerages
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market insurance agencies and brokerages that repeatedly place and service insurance policies for external commercial or personal-lines customers. External policyholders and commercial accounts buy placement, advice, and ongoing policy service; revenue is primarily carrier-paid commission on new and renewal premium, sometimes supplemented by fees.

records.title: Advertising Agencies
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market full-service advertising agencies with $1-10 million of normalized EBITDA and recurring or repeat outsourced client work across strategy, creative, account management, content production, and media planning or buying. Outsourced service for business, nonprofit, and public-sector advertisers, monetized through recurring retainers, project or scope fees, media commissions, and performance incentives; the qualifying lens requires recurring or repeat revenue and normalizes pass-through billings.

records.title: Payroll Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market payroll service bureaus that repeatedly collect client hours, pay rates, deductions, and related data and produce payroll, reports, payments, and tax filings for external employer clients. Recurring outsourced business-to-business service, generally billed per payroll period and often per employee, with implementation fees and optional compliance, HR, timekeeping, and reporting services.

records.title: Office Administrative Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing a recurring or repeat outsourced range of day-to-day office administration, including billing and recordkeeping, personnel administration, financial planning support, and logistics coordination, to external customers on a contract or fee basis. Business and institutional customers purchase ongoing multi-function administrative support through monthly or annual service agreements, fixed or per-unit fees, project fees, and time-and-materials or labor-hour billing.

records.title: Research and Development in Biotechnology (except Nanobiotechnology)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly provide biotechnology discovery, assay development, bioanalysis, preclinical research, computational biology, and related contract R&D services to external customers. Pharmaceutical, biotechnology, academic, and other life-sciences customers buy projects, studies, assays, expert capacity, or continuing research programs under fixed-fee, capped fee-for-service, rate-per-unit, and time-based contracts, often with modifications or follow-on work.

records.title: Farm Management Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring farm management on a contract or fee basis, including production oversight, tenant and lease management, budgeting and accounting, crop marketing coordination, compliance, reporting, and arrangement of operating services for client farms. Landowners, heirs, trusts, estates, and agricultural investors retain an external manager through renewable management contracts; fees may be fixed, per acre, or linked to rent or adjusted farm income, with ongoing reporting and local oversight.

records.title: Computer Systems Design Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms principally providing outsourced computer systems planning, architecture, configuration, network design, systems integration, implementation, installation, user training, and related support to external customers through recurring engagements or repeat project work. Customers are businesses, nonprofits, and government agencies buying expertise to assess needs and integrate hardware, software, networks, security, and data. Revenue commonly combines time-and-materials professional services, fixed-fee projects, milestone or unit pricing, hardware and software pass-through, and smaller support or maintenance streams; repeat work follows platform upgrades, integrations, migrations, security changes, and continuing customer roadmaps.

records.title: Process, Physical Distribution, and Logistics Consulting Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly provide outsourced operations and supply-chain advice or implementation covering manufacturing-process improvement, productivity, production planning, quality, inventory, warehousing, distribution, transportation, and materials handling. Business and public-sector clients buy scoped consulting projects, implementation bundles, and repeat improvement engagements; fees are commonly hourly or project-based and may be competitively bid.

records.title: Other Computer Related Services
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms in NAICS 541519 that deliver recurring or repeat outsourced computer-related services to external customers, including computer disaster recovery, software or hardware installation, technical support, monitoring, backup, cybersecurity, domain-related services, and residual computer consulting that does not belong in programming, systems design, facilities management, hosting, publishing, training, repair, or distribution codes. Customers include small and midsize businesses, larger enterprises, and public-sector organizations. Revenue can combine monthly managed-service subscriptions, per-user or per-device charges, fixed-fee bundles, disaster-recovery and backup retainers, incident-response fees, hourly consulting, installation projects, and software or hardware pass-throughs.

records.title: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms in NAICS 541715 that provide recurring or repeat outsourced research and experimental-development services to external commercial or government customers in physical, engineering, agricultural, environmental, computer, chemical, food, geological, health, medical, pharmaceutical, robotics, aerospace, and other covered sciences. Customers include manufacturers, pharmaceutical and medical-device companies, aerospace and defense organizations, technology companies, agriculture and food businesses, and federal or other public agencies. Revenue may come from cost-reimbursement and cost-plus contracts, fixed-price or milestone projects, fee-for-service and per-unit work, time and materials, full-time-equivalent arrangements, multi-year programs, and reimbursed pass-through costs.

records.title: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing repeat outsourced advice and implementation assistance on strategy, organization, financial planning and budgeting, records and office management, site selection, new-business formation, operational improvement, and related administrative or general management issues. External clients buy expert capacity, independent judgment, analysis, recommendations, and change support through projects, task orders, retainers, or recurring managed advisory. Pricing may be hourly or time-and-materials, fixed price, cost reimbursement, performance based, or a hybrid; repeat revenue usually depends on relationships, specialization, and continuing transformation needs rather than mandatory subscriptions.

records.title: Public Relations Agencies
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market public relations agencies providing recurring or repeat outsourced communications campaigns, media relations, reputation and crisis counsel, public affairs, lobbying, political consulting, executive communications, stakeholder engagement, and related PR consulting to external clients. Businesses, institutions, nonprofits, public bodies, political interests, and individuals buy ongoing counsel and campaign execution through monthly or annual retainers, hourly fees against budgets, fixed project fees, and hybrids. Repeatability is strongest in corporate reputation, media relations, public affairs, crisis preparedness, executive positioning, and integrated communications.

records.title: All Other Insurance Related Activities
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market providers of insurance advisory, insurance actuarial, and insurance ratemaking services performed on a contract or fee basis for insurers, employers, public entities, and other risk-bearing organizations. Customers purchase project work, retainers, recurring valuation or reporting engagements, and specialist analyses. Revenue is generated from professional fees, time-based billing, fixed deliverables, or recurring contracted service scope.

records.title: All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced physical-asset inspection and technical verification, including visual pipeline and power-line inspection, commodity inspection, and fire-suppression equipment testing or inspection without repair or installation. Infrastructure owners, industrial operators, utilities, commodity owners, insurers, and other businesses purchase documented inspections or verification under recurring service agreements or repeat work orders, commonly priced per visit, asset, route, project, or labor period.

records.title: Marketing Consulting Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced marketing operating advice and assistance, including marketing objectives and policy, market and sales forecasting, product and pricing strategy, customer-service and sales-management consulting, licensing or franchise planning, and marketing planning and strategy. External businesses and organizations buy project-based strategy engagements, ongoing retainers, time-based advisory work, and sometimes implementation support. Revenue is primarily professional fees under fixed-scope, hourly, daily, weekly, team-rate, retainer, or hybrid arrangements; repeatability comes from continuing planning, measurement, product, pricing, customer, and go-to-market needs.

records.title: Claims Adjusting
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent adjusting firms that repeatedly investigate, appraise, document, negotiate, and settle claims for insurance carriers, self-insured organizations, or their administrators. Carriers, self-insured organizations, and administrators outsource daily, specialty, field, desk, or catastrophe adjusting under per-claim, fee-schedule, daily-rate, time-and-expense, or managed-service arrangements.

records.title: Offices of Mental Health Practitioners (except Physicians)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market private and group practices of nonphysician psychologists, counselors, marriage and family therapists, social workers, and related licensed mental health practitioners providing recurring or repeat diagnosis, psychotherapy, assessment, and behavioral treatment to external patients and payors. Patients receive scheduled in-person or virtual clinical visits; practices collect negotiated fee-for-service reimbursement from commercial and government payors and patient payments, with each counseling session generally constituting a separate service obligation.

records.title: Computer Facilities Management Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms primarily providing recurring outsourced management, operation, monitoring, administration, maintenance, and user or network support for external customers' computer systems or data-processing facilities, including on-site and hybrid delivery consistent with NAICS 541513. Customers are businesses and institutions that outsource some or all day-to-day IT operations. Revenue commonly recurs under managed-services agreements priced per user, per device, as tiered bundles, or as fixed monthly subscriptions, with project, incident, and resale revenue as supplements. Service levels, security duties, supported assets, user counts, and response obligations shape price and delivery cost.

records.title: Human Resources Consulting Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly advising external clients on human-resource policies and procedures, employee benefits, compensation systems, wage and salary administration, personnel management, assessments, and related implementation support. Businesses and other organizations buy specialist advice through hourly or daily billing, fixed-fee projects, recurring retainers, repeat policy and compensation reviews, and implementation engagements; delivery is primarily expert labor supported by data, benchmarks, documents, and client workshops.

records.title: Testing Laboratories and Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market laboratories and field-testing firms that repeatedly provide physical, chemical, biological, calibration, electrical, geotechnical, mechanical, nondestructive, thermal, or related analytical testing to external customers. External commercial, industrial, and public-sector customers buy tests through per-sample or per-test fees, project scopes, recurring monitoring programs, master service agreements, and related reporting or certification work.

records.title: Computer Terminal and Other Computer Peripheral Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. firms repeatedly designing, assembling, manufacturing, testing, and selling computer terminals and non-storage peripherals such as printers, monitors, scanners, input devices, point-of-sale terminals, and specialized interface equipment to external customers. Repeat product and configured-system sales to enterprises, retailers, hospitality operators, financial institutions, governments, distributors, integrators, gamers, creators, and consumers, priced per device or configuration with channel discounts, warranty, support, consumables, software, and service terms.

records.title: Graphic Design Services
records.search_text: US graphic-design firms in the $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly provide outsourced visual-communication planning, brand identity, layout, illustration, production design, packaging, digital content, and related design management to external customers. Business and institutional customers buy discrete projects, fixed-scope campaigns and brand systems, hourly production, or ongoing retainers for recurring creative needs; repeat demand is generated by new campaigns, channels, products, variants, and brand maintenance rather than a mandatory subscription.

records.title: Electronic Computer Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. firms repeatedly designing, configuring, assembling, integrating, testing, and selling electronic computers, including servers, workstations, rugged or specialized computers, to external customers. Repeat hardware sales and configured system projects to enterprises, governments, data centers, distributors, and specialist users, priced per unit or configuration with component, warranty, support, volume, and channel terms.

records.title: Environmental Consulting Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced environmental assessment, compliance, permitting, risk analysis, waste and pollution-control advice, and remediation planning to external commercial or public customers. Project-based and recurring advisory work sold to businesses and public entities under time-and-materials, cost-plus, fixed-price, unit-price, and task-order arrangements; labor hours are the main economic input and reimbursable field or subcontract costs may be passed through.

records.title: Industrial Design Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms supplying repeat outsourced industrial design and product-development work to external customers, including research, concept development, sketching and rendering, CAD and virtual modeling, material and manufacturing analysis, physical prototyping, usability and safety evaluation, and design-for-manufacture coordination. Manufacturers, product companies, and entrepreneurs purchase scoped product-development projects, milestone phases, hourly design capacity, or retainers; repeat revenue comes from successive products, variants, redesigns, and movement from concept through production rather than a continuously delivered monthly service.

records.title: All Other Legal Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced paralegal and legal-document preparation, legal research and filing, patent-agent filing and search support, process serving, and networked notary services to external law firms, businesses, institutions, or repeat consumer channels. Customers are principally law firms, corporate legal departments, financial or government institutions, and repeat referral channels. Revenue commonly comes from per-document or per-service fees, project or matter fees, retainers, hourly work, and contracted high-volume workflows.

records.title: Geophysical Surveying and Mapping Services
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced geophysical acquisition, processing, interpretation, mapping, monitoring, data management, and related consulting, including seismic, magnetic, gravity, electrical, and electromagnetic methods. Customers include oil and gas, mining and minerals, utilities, infrastructure and civil engineering, environmental users, and government. Revenue is earned through project or fixed-fee work, time-and-materials or day rates, per-unit surveys, monitoring contracts, data licenses, and consulting.

records.title: Mortgage and Nonmortgage Loan Brokers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly arrange mortgage or nonmortgage loans for external borrowers by matching them with lenders and earning commissions or fees. Transaction-based commissions or fees paid by borrowers or lenders for loan sourcing, advice, application assembly, lender matching, and closing coordination; repeat volume is driven by referrals, borrower reuse, and continuing lender relationships.

records.title: Employment Placement Agencies
records.search_text: Recurring outsourced vacancy intake, candidate sourcing, resume review, matching, screening, interviewing, referral, placement coordination, and client/candidate communication for external employers. The lens emphasizes established lower-middle-market agencies with repeat employer relationships and approximately $1 million to $10 million of normalized EBITDA. Employers are the principal economic customers. Revenue is generally earned through successful-placement, contingent, fixed, retained-project, or other recruiting-service fees; repeat hiring clients may generate multiple assignments even when each placement is episodic. Candidates are service participants rather than employees of the placement agency under the NAICS definition.

records.title: Other Nonhazardous Waste Treatment and Disposal
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators repeatedly providing external customers with nonhazardous waste treatment or disposal other than landfill, combustion, incineration, or sewer treatment, including commercial composting and other physical, chemical, or biological treatment facilities and combined local collection-and-treatment operations. Recurring or repeat gate and tipping fees, service agreements based on waste type, frequency, weight or volume, municipal or commercial processing contracts, combined collection fees, and secondary revenue from compost, recovered products, environmental attributes, or related management services.

records.title: Educational Support Services
records.search_text: US educational consulting, curriculum and assessment support, testing and evaluation, guidance and counseling, student-support administration, and related non-instructional services sold repeatedly or under recurring contracts to schools, institutions, families, or other external customers by firms in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Customers include public and private school systems, colleges, education agencies, foundations, employers, and families. Revenue commonly combines school-year service contracts, retained or shared specialist capacity, fixed-scope packages, testing volumes, and hourly or daily consulting fees.

records.title: Residential Property Managers
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market firms that manage residential houses, apartments, multifamily communities, and other residential rental property for external owners under recurring or repeat service agreements. Property owners purchase recurring management, leasing, rent administration, reporting, maintenance coordination, compliance, inspection, and resident-service work. Revenue commonly combines recurring management fees with leasing, maintenance coordination, application, or other service charges.

records.title: Outpatient Mental Health and Substance Abuse Centers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market outpatient centers providing recurring or repeat diagnosis, counseling, psychotherapy, medication-supported care, crisis stabilization, case management, and related treatment for mental health and substance use conditions to external patients. Episode-based and recurring outpatient revenue paid by Medicaid, Medicare, commercial insurers, self-pay patients, grants, and public programs for assessments, individual and group therapy, medication-related services, intensive outpatient care, case management, crisis services, and care coordination.

records.title: Professional and Management Development Training
records.search_text: Privately owned firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced professional, leadership, management, compliance, communication, sales, and career-development training to external employers, including customized classroom, virtual, blended, simulation, cohort, and managed-program delivery. Employer-funded repeat cohorts, annual programs, retainers, managed curricula, subscriptions with facilitation, or fixed-fee customized engagements; providers are paid for needs assessment, content, instruction, facilitation, administration, and outcome reporting.

records.title: Marketing Research and Public Opinion Polling
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms repeatedly supplying external customers with custom market research, brand or customer tracking, survey and polling programs, statistical sampling, qualitative research, data collection, analysis, and research reporting. Business, nonprofit, media, campaign, and government customers buy recurring tracking programs, annual or multi-period subscriptions, retainers, and repeat fixed-fee custom projects. Revenue can combine design, fieldwork or panels, analysis, reporting, and advisory interpretation.

records.title: Collection Agencies
records.search_text: Independent third-party agencies collecting consumer or commercial claims for clients and remitting recoveries, using letters, telephone, email, text, portals, payment processing, skip tracing, dispute handling, and account servicing. Recurring or repeat creditor placements must be supported by transferable client relationships, systems, procedures, licenses, and compliance controls. Creditors and debt owners outsource account placements to agencies, commonly paying a contingent percentage of amounts recovered; some arrangements may use fixed, placement, or servicing fees. Revenue is recurring or repeat through portfolios and creditor relationships but varies with placements and recoveries.

records.title: Bare Printed Circuit Board Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of rigid, flex, rigid-flex, high-density interconnect, RF/microwave, and other bare printed circuit boards, emphasizing quick-turn prototypes, high-mix low-volume production, domestic or trusted manufacturing, engineering and CAM support, qualification, and repeat customer part numbers. Customers include aerospace and defense contractors, data-center and networking OEMs, medical and industrial equipment makers, automotive suppliers, electronics manufacturing services providers, and hardware developers. Revenue is earned per custom board or production lot, with engineering review, nonrecurring setup, expedited turnaround, qualification, repeat releases, and time-to-market support creating service-like program economics.

records.title: Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market specialty semiconductor and related-device manufacturers, including mature-node and trusted foundries, analog, power, sensor, optoelectronic, photonic, discrete-device, and custom-wafer businesses with high-mix production, customer-funded development, qualification, and repeat manufacturing programs. Customers include industrial, automotive, aerospace and defense, medical, communications, data-center, IoT, and research organizations. Revenue is earned from wafers, dice, devices, engineering runs, prototypes, qualification, masks and nonrecurring engineering, then recurring production releases over a customer's product life cycle.

records.title: Agents and Managers for Artists, Athletes, Entertainers, and Other Public Figures
records.search_text: US agencies and management firms in the $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly represent and manage artists, performers, athletes, creators, and other public figures for commissions or recurring management fees. The represented person is the client; revenue is predominantly a contractual percentage of client earnings, bookings, endorsements, or other commissionable income, with some recurring management or business-management fees.

records.title: Nonresidential Property Managers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly manage office, industrial, retail, and other nonresidential real estate for external owners and investors, including lease administration, property accounting, tenant coordination, vendor oversight, inspections, maintenance coordination, and owner reporting. Commercial property owners and investors pay recurring monthly fees based on a fixed amount or a percentage of rental income or receipts; contracts may also reimburse property-dedicated administrative and payroll costs and may include separately priced leasing, engineering, or project services.

records.title: Exam Preparation and Tutoring
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market tutoring and exam-preparation firms that repeatedly sell academic remediation, enrichment, standardized-test preparation, professional-exam review, or related online or center-based instruction to students, families, schools, or employers. Families and students commonly buy per-session, monthly, prepaid-package, membership, or fixed-course instruction; schools and organizations may buy cohorts or contracted tutoring. Revenue repeats through weekly sessions, multi-month programs, successive subjects, exam cycles, referrals, and institutional renewals.

records.title: Tax Preparation Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms whose primary business is preparing and filing tax returns for external individual or small-business customers, including related return review and client explanation within the engagement. Annual or repeat outsourced tax-return engagements, generally priced per return and by form or complexity, with some add-on representation or advisory fees; revenue and staffing are highly concentrated in filing season.

records.title: Offices of Physicians (except Mental Health Specialists)
records.search_text: US physician-owned, sponsor-owned, and other transferable physician practices that repeatedly provide non-mental-health professional medical services to patients and payers, including office-based and facility-based professional services when the practice is the billing operating entity. Practices serve patients as the clinical customers while collecting chiefly from commercial insurers and public payers through fee-for-service schedules, supplemented by capitation, shared savings, bundled payments, performance payments, and patient cost sharing.

records.title: Engineering Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market engineering-services firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced design, analysis, project delivery, technical documentation, inspection, testing coordination, and engineering advisory services to external customers. External private- and public-sector customers purchase projects, task orders, master-service-agreement work, and repeat advisory or compliance support under lump-sum, cost-plus, time-and-materials, and hourly billing structures.

records.title: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market providers of recurring or repeat outsourced computing infrastructure, data processing, managed and web hosting, cloud storage, backup, platform or application hosting, and technical streaming-support services for external customers. Business customers pay recurring subscriptions, reserved-capacity charges, usage-based compute, storage, or bandwidth fees, and managed-service fees, commonly with service-level commitments. Revenue can combine contracted recurring fees with variable consumption.

records.title: Other Scientific and Technical Consulting Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced scientific and technical advice to external customers, including economic, energy, agricultural, biological, geological, workplace-safety, and public-safety consulting. Business and government customers buy specialist analysis, compliance support, audits, studies, expert advice, and project deliverables through hourly, time-and-materials, fixed-fee, retainer, and government-contract arrangements.

records.title: Offices of Physical, Occupational and Speech Therapists, and Audiologists
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market independent and group outpatient practices providing recurring or repeat physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech-language pathology, audiology, and closely related rehabilitation services to external patients and payors. Patients receive evaluated plans and repeated treatment visits, commonly following referral or medical need; practices earn per-visit or per-service reimbursement from commercial insurers, Medicare, workers' compensation, and patients, with some audiology device revenue and contract services.

records.title: Commercial Banking
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market commercial banks providing recurring deposit, transaction-account, payment, cash-management, commercial and consumer lending, and related relationship-banking services to external households and businesses under a bank charter. Banks earn net interest income from the spread between loans and other earning assets and deposits or other funding, plus account, payment, card, treasury-management, origination, servicing, and other fees; customer relationships are recurring and control transfers require regulatory approval.

records.title: Architectural Services
records.search_text: US architectural-services firms in the $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that provide building planning, architectural design, documentation, specification, permitting, and construction-phase services to external owners and repeat or recurring clients. Project-based outsourced professional services for private and public owners, commonly billed by stipulated sum, professional fee plus reimbursables, hourly rates, or a percentage of construction cost; repeat demand comes from continuing owner relationships and successive projects rather than subscriptions.

records.title: Offices of Lawyers
records.search_text: US law firms in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that provide recurring or repeat legal advice, representation, drafting, transactions, litigation, compliance, and related client service to external individuals or organizations. External individuals, businesses, insurers, and institutions buy matter-based or continuing legal services through hourly, flat, contingent, subscription, retainer, and blended fee arrangements.

records.title: Fitness and Recreational Sports Centers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating gyms, fitness centers, fitness studios, swimming facilities, skating rinks, and racquet or other recreational sports centers for external members and repeat users. Consumers and organizations pay recurring monthly or annual memberships plus enrollment, class, training, court, pool, program, day-pass, event, and ancillary retail fees; some facilities operate through franchises or nonprofit ownership.

records.title: Sheet Metal Work Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market sheet-metal job shops and contract manufacturers repeatedly providing outsourced detailing, cutting, punching, bending, forming, welding, assembly, finishing, and delivery for external HVAC, construction, equipment, enclosure, industrial, and OEM customers. Job, project, or repeat-production quotes for custom sheet-metal parts and assemblies, often under recurring contractor and OEM accounts with material, engineering support, programming, fabrication, finishing, inspection, and delivery bundled.

records.title: Motion Picture and Video Distribution
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market distributors that repeatedly acquire, license, represent, market, deliver, monetize, report, and settle rights to motion pictures and video with theaters, broadcasters, networks, streaming services, digital platforms, and other exhibitors or licensees. Distributors earn recurring or repeat distribution commissions, agency fees, platform and media-service fees, fixed license fees, minimum guarantees with overages, and shares of box-office, rental, advertising, subscription, or other title receipts across territories and release windows.

records.title: Diagnostic Imaging Centers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market diagnostic imaging firms repeatedly producing patient images on practitioner referral through MRI, CT, X-ray, mammography, ultrasound, PET, and nuclear-medicine services, including patient scheduling and preparation, image acquisition, technical operations, interpretation coordination, reporting, billing, collections, and referral support. Patients are generally referred by physicians, while commercial insurers, managed-care organizations, Medicare, Medicaid, hospitals, physician groups, and patients fund services. Revenue is mainly per procedure under negotiated commercial rates or government fee schedules, with some capitation and management-fee arrangements; repeat referrals and recurring payer or physician relationships support volume.

records.title: General Rental Centers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market general rental centers that repeatedly rent a broad inventory of consumer, commercial, and industrial equipment to external customers for short periods, including tools, contractor equipment, lawn and garden items, moving equipment, audiovisual gear, and party or banquet equipment, together with associated reservation, delivery, pickup, inspection, and maintenance services. Customers reserve equipment by branch, phone, or digital channel and pay fixed daily, weekly, monthly, or event rental rates plus delivery, pickup, fuel, damage-waiver, consumable, and ancillary service charges; operators also realize used-equipment proceeds and must manage utilization, maintenance, logistics, loss, and residual value.

records.title: Special Die and Tool, Die Set, Jig, and Fixture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market tool and die shops that repeatedly design, engineer, manufacture, modify, repair, validate, and support special dies, die sets, jigs, fixtures, gauges, and related production tooling for external manufacturers. Repeat B2B project and program revenue from custom tooling design and build, engineering changes, repair, refurbishment, spares, tryout, and support, priced through quotations, milestones, time and materials, or purchase orders, often with customer-owned tools.

records.title: Electroplating, Plating, Polishing, Anodizing, and Coloring
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market job shops repeatedly electroplating, plating, anodizing, coloring, buffing, polishing, cleaning, pickling, tumbling, or blasting customer-owned metal and other products for the trade. Recurring job-shop and production-program revenue billed per piece, square foot, pound, rack, barrel, batch, or quoted program, with charges for masking, pretreatment, plating metal, thickness, testing, certificates, packaging, and expedite service.

records.title: Security Systems Services (except Locksmiths)
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms that design, install, inspect, test, repair, maintain, or monitor electronic security, intrusion, video-surveillance, access-control, and fire-alarm systems for external customers on recurring contracts or through repeat service relationships. Residential and commercial customers pay installation and equipment charges, time-and-material service fees, and contractual recurring monitoring or maintenance fees. Monitoring can be bundled with installation, maintenance, repair, response notification, and equipment rental.

records.title: Home Health Care Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market home health agencies providing recurring or repeat skilled nursing, therapy, home health aide, home infusion, in-home hospice, medical social, and related clinical services in patients' homes to external patients and payers. Patients receive episodic or ongoing home-based clinical care ordered under plans of care; agencies are paid by Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, commercial insurers, health systems, or private pay through prospective 30-day bundles, per-visit rates, case rates, or contracted fee schedules.

records.title: Golf Courses and Country Clubs
records.search_text: Independently controlled, for-profit golf-course and country-club operators serving repeat golfers or members through green fees, memberships, dues, instruction, events, food and beverage, and related facility services. Direct-to-consumer repeat play and recurring membership revenue, supplemented by cart rental, instruction, events, food and beverage, and pro-shop sales; pricing is posted, seasonal, membership-based, or dynamically adjusted by tee-time demand.

records.title: Residential Mental Health and Substance Abuse Facilities
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market operators providing recurring or episode-based residential care and treatment for mental-health and substance-use conditions, including nonhospital rehabilitation facilities, mental-health halfway houses, psychiatric convalescent settings, and residential group homes, with room, board, supervision, counseling, rehabilitation, and support services. Patients are service recipients, while commercial insurers, Medicaid, Medicare and other public programs, and self-pay clients fund care. Revenue is generally episode, day, service, or negotiated-rate based and reduced by payer contractual adjustments, discounts, and collection concessions.

records.title: General Automotive Repair
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market general automotive repair firms providing repeat mechanical and electrical diagnosis, maintenance, engine repair, and replacement services to external vehicle owners and fleet customers. Repeat repair orders paid by consumers, commercial fleets, warranty administrators, or insurers, with revenue from labor, parts markup, diagnostic fees, and related shop charges.

records.title: Linen Supply
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly rent, collect, launder, inspect, repair, replace, inventory, and deliver table and bed linens, towels, diapers, and nonindustrial uniforms, gowns, or coats to external business customers. Healthcare, hospitality, food service, personal-care, and other businesses pay recurring rental and service charges, commonly under multi-year agreements with scheduled pickup and replacement routes.

records.title: Indoor and Outdoor Display Advertising
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat public-display advertising services to external customers, including campaign design, inventory sales, billboard and place-based display placement, digital-display management, printing, installation, and related measurement and account service. Advertisers and agencies buy or lease access to location-specific billboard, transit, airport, street-furniture, retail, and other display inventory, commonly in campaign periods or network packages. Rates are driven primarily by location, audience, demand, competition, format, and occupancy; operators may also charge for design, printing, installation, and measurement.

records.title: Industrial Mold Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market industrial mold makers that repeatedly design, engineer, manufacture, modify, repair, validate, and support molds for external plastics, rubber, glass, die-casting, foundry, and other manufacturing customers. Repeat B2B project and program revenue from custom mold design and build, engineering changes, repair, refurbishment, spares, validation, and support, priced through quotations, milestones, time and materials, or purchase orders with customer-owned tooling common.

records.title: Offices of Physicians, Mental Health Specialists
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market private and group practices of MD and DO mental health specialists providing recurring or repeat psychiatry and psychoanalysis services to patients and payers, including evaluation, medication management, psychotherapy, and related clinical administration. Patients and third-party payers purchase recurring evaluations, psychotherapy, medication management, and care coordination through per-visit fee schedules, negotiated commercial rates, Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement, value-based arrangements, and some cash-pay packages.

records.title: Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly provide outsourced refrigerated and frozen storage, handling, inventory control, blast freezing, case picking, inspection, and related cold-chain warehouse services to external customers. Food producers, processors, distributors, retailers, and selected life-sciences customers buy recurring temperature-controlled space plus transactional handling and value-added services under warehouse agreements, rate letters, tariffs, and occasionally dedicated leases, often with minimum storage commitments and price-adjustment clauses.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Waste Management Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms in the code that repeatedly provide outsourced sewer, storm-basin, catch-basin, commercial or industrial tank, beach, and other miscellaneous waste-management cleaning, inspection, and maintenance services to municipal, utility, industrial, commercial, institutional, or property customers. Customers buy recurring maintenance programs, competitively bid municipal or utility work, scheduled inspections and cleanouts, and project or emergency services under unit-price, time-and-materials, lump-sum, or term contracts, often with mobilization, equipment, disposal, and traffic-control components.

records.title: All Other Consumer Goods Rental
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators that own and repeatedly rent miscellaneous consumer goods not assigned to more specific rental codes, principally party and event supplies and short-duration residential furniture or specialty household goods, together with quoting, reservation, warehouse, preparation, delivery, setup, pickup, inspection, cleaning, and maintenance work. Households, event hosts, venues, and planners pay time-based fees for access to operator-owned goods, with additional delivery, setup, damage-waiver, cleaning, or service revenue. Revenue depends on inventory turns, route and warehouse execution, return condition, and local event or household demand.

records.title: Relay and Industrial Control Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying relays, motor starters and controllers, drives, configured control products, industrial control accessories, and related repeat engineering or lifecycle support to external OEM, industrial, infrastructure, and automation customers. Products are sold through repeat OEM and distributor channels, configured-to-order purchases, and automation projects, with recurring replacement, expansion, spare, software, engineering, and lifecycle-service demand around installed controls.

records.title: Medical Laboratories
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market medical laboratories repeatedly providing human diagnostic testing to physicians, health systems, employers, payers, and patients, including clinical chemistry, hematology, microbiology, molecular and genetic testing, toxicology, blood analysis, anatomic pathology, specimen logistics, result reporting, billing, and related customer service. Customers and ordering parties include physicians, hospitals, health systems, managed-care organizations, employers, government programs, and patients. Revenue is primarily per test under government and commercial fee schedules or negotiated fee-for-service contracts, with smaller capitated, bundled, risk-sharing, and direct-pay components; repeat testing and recurring referral relationships drive volume.

records.title: Convention and Trade Show Organizers
records.search_text: US convention, conference, and trade-show organizers in the lower middle market that repeatedly plan, promote, manage, or own business events for external customers, exhibitors, sponsors, or attendees. Customers include associations, corporations, exhibitors, sponsors, and attendees. Revenue can combine management or production fees with exhibit-space, sponsorship, registration, ancillary-event, media, and digital-service revenue; contracts and advance bookings commonly precede the event.

records.title: Other Measuring and Controlling Device Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers whose principal activity is making measuring and controlling devices classified in NAICS 334519, including physical-properties testing and inspection equipment, surveying and drafting instruments, nonflight aircraft engine instruments, mechanical counters and meters, balances and scales, and related residual measuring devices. Primarily product and aftermarket revenue from external industrial, infrastructure, aerospace, laboratory, and government customers, with some recurring calibration, maintenance, consumables, software, and replacement demand depending on the product family.

records.title: Packaging and Labeling Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly packaging client-owned products, including kit assembly, blister packaging, shrink wrapping, folding, labeling, imprinting, inspection, and related handling for external customers. Brand owners and manufacturers outsource runs under per-unit, per-batch, project, minimum-volume, setup, materials-pass-through, and recurring program arrangements; the provider supplies labor, packaging machinery, floor space, quality control, labeling data, and changeover capability around client-owned goods.

records.title: Computer Training
records.search_text: Privately owned computer-training firms that repeatedly provide external employers with programming, software application, business-system, computer operations, cybersecurity, cloud, data, and network training, including instructor-led, virtual, blended, lab, and managed-learning delivery. Recurring employer contracts, annual training programs, subscriptions with services, managed cohorts, or repeat fixed-fee and per-seat engagements; revenue combines instruction, labs, curriculum adaptation, assessment, and program administration.

records.title: Dental Laboratories
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market dental laboratories that repeatedly manufacture patient-specific crowns, bridges, dentures, orthodontic appliances, implant restorations, and related custom dental devices for external dental practices. Dental practices and specialists send prescriptions, impressions, or digital scans on a case-by-case basis. Laboratories charge per restoration or appliance, often with remake and turnaround commitments; recurring revenue comes from repeated case flow and dentist relationships rather than subscriptions.

records.title: Veterinary Services
records.search_text: Veterinary clinics, animal hospitals, specialty and emergency practices, mobile and farm-animal practices, and veterinary diagnostic laboratories that serve external customers, generate recurring or repeat service revenue, and fall within the dataset-defined lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Customers are primarily pet owners, farms and other animal owners, with some referrals from other veterinary practices. Revenue is predominantly fee-for-service by examination, procedure, diagnostic test, treatment, hospitalization, and dispensed product; preventive, chronic-care, and follow-up needs create repeat demand, while emergency and surgical work is episodic.

records.title: Exterminating and Pest Control Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced inspection, prevention, extermination, integrated pest management, termite, mosquito, rodent, wildlife, and structural fumigation services to external residential, commercial, institutional, and industrial customers. Customers commonly buy scheduled residential or commercial prevention plans, periodic inspections and treatments, renewals, and one-time or ancillary responses for specific infestations, termite, wildlife, exclusion, or remediation. Revenue is predominantly recurring under service agreements, supplemented by initial treatments and one-time specialty work; route density, technician productivity, retention, callbacks, materials, and pricing determine unit economics.

records.title: All Other Amusement and Recreation Industries
records.search_text: US operators of fixed-site participatory recreation venues in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band, including miniature golf and driving ranges, archery and shooting ranges, billiard parlors, escape rooms, go-kart or similar single-attraction venues, and comparable facilities selling repeat admission, reservations, memberships, parties, or events to external customers. Direct consumer and group-event revenue from admissions, timed reservations, activity fees, memberships or passes, parties, food and beverage, and ancillary merchandise; repeat use is common but most revenue is not protected by long-term contracts.

records.title: Instruments and Related Products Manufacturing for Measuring, Displaying, and Controlling Industrial Process Variables
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of instruments and related devices that measure, display, record, transmit, or control industrial process variables and repeatedly serve external industrial customers, including associated calibration, configuration, engineering, software, and support. Instrument and system sales to process manufacturers, OEMs, distributors, engineering contractors, and industrial facilities, with repeat demand from production programs, installed-base replacement, calibration, consumables, software, configuration, and technical support.

records.title: Other Services Related to Advertising
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market distributors and coordinators that repeatedly provide branded-merchandise and advertising-specialty programs to external customers, including product sourcing, decoration coordination, creative support, online stores, order management, fulfillment coordination, and account service. Business and institutional customers buy decorated merchandise and continuing program support. Providers primarily purchase products and branding from third parties and resell finished goods, while some also charge for technology, creative, loyalty, event, warehousing, fulfillment, and related services. Revenue ranges from repeat orders to multi-year, auto-renewing programs, often without minimum purchase guarantees.

records.title: Apprenticeship Training
records.search_text: Privately controlled firms that repeatedly provide apprenticeship-program design, related technical instruction, curriculum, apprentice administration, compliance, and multi-employer coordination to external employers, sponsors, or apprentices. Recurring employer, sponsor, association, or workforce-system contracts and per-apprentice or per-cohort instruction fees, sometimes supplemented or reimbursed by federal or state workforce funds and tuition support.

records.title: Business Associations
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market business associations that repeatedly serve external member businesses through industry or local-business advocacy, research, market statistics, standards, certification, publications, events, education, directories, and member communications, including trade associations, chambers of commerce, manufacturers' associations, farm bureaus, and real estate boards. Members commonly pay annual dues; associations also earn event registration, sponsorship, advertising, publication, education, certification, data, and service revenue. Boards and members govern mission and budgets, and many organizations are nonprofit business leagues rather than investor-owned firms.

records.title: Home Health Equipment Rental
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market businesses that repeatedly rent durable home health equipment to external patients or caregivers and provide the associated intake, delivery, setup, instruction, maintenance, billing, and follow-up service. Recurring or episode-based rental payments from Medicare, other insurers, institutions, or patients, often accompanied by reimbursed supplies and service obligations.

records.title: Instrument Manufacturing for Measuring and Testing Electricity and Electrical Signals
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of instruments for measuring and testing electricity and electrical signals that repeatedly serve external customers, including circuit testers, multimeters, wattmeters, signal and semiconductor test equipment, related software, calibration, configuration, and support. Instrument and system sales to electronics and semiconductor manufacturers, utilities, laboratories, OEMs, distributors, field technicians, and industrial users, with repeat demand from technology upgrades, fleet replacement, calibration, accessories, software, service contracts, and support.

records.title: Professional Organizations
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market professional organizations that repeatedly serve external individual or institutional members by advancing a profession through research, statistics, standards, certification, continuing education, publications, conferences, peer review, ethics, advocacy, and professional communities, including bar, health-professional, engineering, scientific, and learned-society organizations. Members commonly pay annual dues and fees for conferences, continuing education, certification, publications, data, and programs; institutions and vendors may pay sponsorship and advertising fees. Boards and members govern mission and standards, and many organizations are nonprofit business leagues or educational nonprofits rather than investor-owned firms.

records.title: Research and Development in the Social Sciences and Humanities
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing external social-science or humanities research, survey research, behavioral and economic research, program and policy evaluation, qualitative research, and related study design, data collection, analysis, and reporting. Customers include government agencies, foundations, nonprofits, and businesses. Revenue commonly comes from task orders, multiyear studies, cost-reimbursement contracts, time-based work, fixed-fee deliverables, and recurring evaluation or evidence-building programs.

records.title: Other Activities Related to Real Estate
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market real estate escrow agencies, listing-service operators, real estate fiduciaries, landman and right-of-way service firms, real estate consultants other than agents or appraisers, and real estate asset-management service firms other than property managers, when they repeatedly serve unaffiliated customers for transaction, project, retainer, data-access, or administration fees. Customers include lenders, buyers and sellers, investors, developers, energy and infrastructure companies, and property owners. Revenue is typically earned through per-transaction escrow or administration charges, project fees, recurring listing or data access, retainers, or asset-management fees rather than ownership of the underlying property.

records.title: Support Activities for Forestry
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced forestry support to external customers, including timber cruising and estimation, forest management and consulting, reforestation and stand improvement, pest and vegetation control, wildfire prevention and suppression support, and related field implementation. Customers include federal, state, Tribal, and local land managers, industrial and family forest owners, timber businesses, utilities, conservation organizations, and developers. Revenue commonly comes from competitively bid fixed-price or unit-price projects, time-and-materials consulting, multi-year or preseason blanket agreements, and repeat seasonal field programs.

records.title: Doll, Toy, and Game Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly supply dolls, toys, physical games, electronic game devices, parts, accessories, or private-label products to external retailers, distributors, licensors, institutions, and brand owners, including firms that retain accountable product-development, sourcing, compliance, and customer-delivery responsibility while outsourcing some fabrication. Revenue is principally per-unit wholesale or contract-manufacturing sales, often through seasonal line reviews and repeat purchase orders. Economics may also include tooling or development charges, minimum-order commitments, private-label programs, and royalties on licensed characters or brands.

records.title: Other Communications Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of fire and intrusion alarm communicators, intercom and paging systems, traffic and railway signals, institutional communications devices, stadium displays, and other specialized communications equipment, emphasizing installed-base monitoring, cloud connectivity, software, maintenance, and repeat dealer or integrator relationships. Customers include security dealers and integrators, fire-alarm installers, municipalities, transit and railway agencies, schools, hospitals, industrial facilities, commercial buildings, and venue operators. Revenue combines upfront devices and systems with replacement parts, support, firmware, hosted access, cellular or cloud communications, monitoring-enablement, and maintenance subscriptions tied to an installed base.

records.title: Other Residential Care Facilities
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators of staffed residential group homes that provide recurring supervision, personal care, habilitation, case support, and daily living assistance to children, disabled people without nursing care, or justice-involved residents under government, Medicaid, managed-care, or agency placement arrangements. Recurring occupancy- or service-linked payments, commonly per-diem reimbursement, public-agency placement contracts, Medicaid or managed-care reimbursement, and block or capacity contracts, with residents receiving continuous supervision and personal care.

records.title: Title Abstract and Settlement Offices
records.search_text: Independent and affiliated US title abstract, title search, title production, escrow, and real-estate settlement offices serving external lenders, real-estate professionals, buyers, and sellers, restricted to transferable firms in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Revenue is predominantly transaction-based: fixed or scheduled fees for search, examination, escrow, document preparation, recording, and closing, plus commissions retained when acting as an agent for a title insurer. Demand is repeatedly referred by lenders, brokers, builders, and attorneys even though the end borrower or property party often pays.

records.title: Other Activities Related to Credit Intermediation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent third-party mortgage and non-mortgage loan servicers that repeatedly administer loans for unaffiliated lenders, investors, or government programs, including payment and escrow administration, statements, account records, borrower support, delinquency workflows, and servicing transfers. Recurring servicing-fee, per-loan, or portfolio administration contracts paid by loan owners, lenders, investors, or public programs, sometimes supplemented by permitted activity fees; revenue generally continues while accounts remain under administration.

records.title: Recreational Goods Rental
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators that own and rent recreational equipment such as bicycles, canoes, motorcycles, skis, sailboats, beach chairs, and umbrellas, including the reservations, fitting or handoff, inspection, maintenance, and retrieval work needed to support repeated rentals. Consumers and visitor groups pay time-based rental fees, often by hour or day and with strong seasonal and location effects; operators may also earn delivery, damage-waiver, instruction, or accessory revenue. The model requires local inventory utilization, safe handoff, condition control, and turnaround between users.

records.title: Postharvest Crop Activities (except Cotton Ginning)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market independent firms repeatedly providing crop cleaning, drying, shelling, fumigating, curing, sorting, grading, packing, waxing, cooling, and associated lot-handling services after harvest for external growers, marketers, wholesalers, and processors. Operators typically earn repeat seasonal or year-round service revenue based on units handled, weight, bins, cartons, storage time, processing steps, or contracted capacity, with customers paying to prepare crops for market or further processing while preserving quality, traceability, and food safety.

records.title: Industrial Process Furnace and Oven Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market industrial furnace, oven, kiln, induction-heating, and dielectric-heating OEMs with repeat external revenue from installed-base parts, preventive maintenance, calibration, field service, technical support, controls upgrades, retrofits, rebuilds, and repeat equipment orders. Engineered-to-order and standard capital equipment sold through quotations and projects, followed by recurring parts, field service, calibration, maintenance, modernization, software and controls work across a long-lived installed base.

records.title: Colleges, Universities, and Professional Schools
records.search_text: Privately controlled four-year colleges, universities, and professional schools providing recurring degree or professional education to external students and operating in the approximately $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. Students purchase term- or program-based education funded by tuition, institutional grants, federal or state student aid, employer support, and private payment; some institutions also earn recurring auxiliary, research, clinical, or contract revenue tied to the operating institution.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Schools and Instruction
records.search_text: Private firms repeatedly providing outsourced health, safety, emergency-response, and compliance-oriented instruction to employers and other organizations, including first-aid, CPR/AED, lifeguard, and closely related skills courses when classified in 611699. Employer and institutional customers buy scheduled public seats or on-site group classes, commonly priced per participant or per cohort, with repeat demand driven by employee turnover, credential expiration, and regulatory or workplace policy requirements.

records.title: Navigational Services to Shipping
records.search_text: Recurring outsourced harbor tug and ship-assist work, docking and undocking, compulsory or contracted pilotage, and externally sold vessel-traffic reporting and navigation support performed by lower-middle-market firms. Shipowners, operators, agents, terminals, and ports buy per-movement, tariff-based, hourly, or contracted navigation and harbor-assist services; repeat traffic and regulatory pilotage requirements create recurring relationships, while rates may be regulated or periodically reset.

records.title: Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market providers of cadastral, boundary, topographic, geodetic, construction, hydrographic, aerial, photogrammetric, LiDAR, GIS, and cartographic surveying or mapping services supplied to external customers on repeat project, task-order, or continuing-service relationships. Professional project and task-order services for public agencies, engineering and construction firms, utilities, real-estate owners, and resource clients. Engagements are commonly billed hourly or per diem, as cost plus a fixed fee, as a lump sum for defined scope, or by a fee related to construction cost; repeat business comes from continuing capital programs, land transactions, compliance, asset records, and map updates.

records.title: Soil Preparation, Planting, and Cultivating
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors that repeatedly provide soil preparation, plowing, seedbed preparation, fertilizing, seeding, planting, transplanting, thinning, pollinating, cultivating, crop spraying, and weed or pest-control field services to external farms while retaining accountable responsibility for equipment, operators, scheduling, application accuracy, safety, compliance, and job completion. Customers are crop farms, landowners, farm managers, cooperatives, and agribusinesses that outsource equipment-intensive field passes. Revenue is commonly charged per acre, per hour, per job, or by application volume, with materials sometimes billed separately; repeat demand follows planting, tillage, fertilizer, spraying, and cultivation calendars.

records.title: Solar Electric Power Generation
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market independent operating companies whose primary activity is operating solar electric generation facilities and repeatedly selling generated electricity to transmission or distribution systems under power purchase agreements, tariffs, or merchant arrangements. Operators sell metered electricity and sometimes renewable attributes to utilities, public agencies, corporate offtakers, community-solar subscribers, or wholesale markets, commonly under long-term fixed or escalating power purchase agreements and sometimes at merchant prices.

records.title: Landscape Architectural Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market landscape architecture firms providing outsourced land planning, site design, landscape design, documentation, permitting support, and construction-phase observation for commercial, institutional, public, infrastructure, recreational, and residential clients, with recurring relationships or repeat project work. Project-based professional services sold to developers, public agencies, institutions, architects and engineers, businesses, and property owners. Revenue is commonly earned through fixed or stipulated project fees, hourly fees, supplemental-service fees, and sometimes fees linked to construction cost; repeat clients are an important source of new projects.

records.title: Offices of Optometrists
records.search_text: Independent group and multisite optometry practices in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly provide eye examinations, refraction, disease detection and management, corrective-lens prescribing and fitting, contact-lens care, vision therapy, pre- and postoperative care, and integrated optical dispensing to external patients and payers. Patients receive the service and may pay directly, while medical or vision insurers fund covered examinations and procedures. Practices may also earn product revenue from eyeglasses, contact lenses, and related optical goods. Clinical revenue is generally visit- or procedure-based, whereas optical revenue combines product margin and fitting service; repeat examinations, disease monitoring, and prescription renewals create repeat demand without guaranteed contracts.

records.title: Interior Design Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market interior design and interior decorating firms providing repeat outsourced planning, design, documentation, specification, procurement coordination, and project-administration services for commercial, hospitality, healthcare, institutional, and residential clients. External property owners, developers, operators, institutions, and homeowners buy project-based services. Revenue may be fixed fee, hourly, a percentage of project cost, procurement markup, or a hybrid; repeatability is strongest with commercial, hospitality, institutional, developer, and multi-site clients.

records.title: Teleproduction and Other Postproduction Services
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms repeatedly supplying external studios, producers, agencies, brands, broadcasters, and digital publishers with video editing, conforming, color, visual effects, animation, graphics, captioning, subtitling, localization, media transfer, restoration, mastering, and delivery services. Customers buy project bids, work orders under master service agreements, episodic or campaign packages, retainers, and capacity blocks. Revenue is mainly fee-for-service and often fixed by project or deliverable, with some time-and-materials, usage, storage, revision, and rush charges.

records.title: Couriers and Express Delivery Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat intercity and express pickup, linehaul, sorting, tracking, and parcel or document delivery services to external business and consumer customers. Per-package, zone-and-weight, time-definite, route, or negotiated account pricing, often with accessorial and indexed fuel surcharges; revenue is repeat but volumes and customer concentration can vary materially.

records.title: Semiconductor Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of wafer-processing equipment, semiconductor assembly and packaging equipment, and other semiconductor-making machinery, including manufacturer-attributable spare parts, upgrades, service agreements, software, installation, training, and extended warranty work. Recurring or repeat outsourced revenue from external semiconductor manufacturers through equipment programs, technology-node upgrades, spare parts, field service, maintenance agreements, factory-automation software, installation, training, and warranties.

records.title: Bowling Centers
records.search_text: US operators of commercial bowling centers selling repeat lane use, league play, group events, food and beverage, and related on-site amusements to external consumers and organizations, within the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. Primarily direct-to-consumer, per-game or time-based lane sales, recurring league play, prepaid or contracted group events, and on-site food, beverage, and amusement spend; revenue is repeat but generally not long-term contracted.

records.title: Other Personal Care Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms in NAICS 812199 providing repeat external-customer personal-care services, including nonmedical hair-removal, tattoo and piercing, permanent-makeup, tanning, sauna or steam-bath, hair-replacement, and related day-spa services. Primarily consumer-paid appointments, sessions, packages, memberships, and product add-ons; revenue is local, transaction-heavy, and often driven by repeat visits, referrals, practitioner reputation, and capacity utilization.

records.title: Telemarketing Bureaus and Other Contact Centers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms repeatedly operating outsourced inbound or outbound contact centers for external customers, including customer information and assistance, order taking, complaint handling, retention, lead qualification, product promotion, telesales, and contribution solicitation across telephone, email, chat, and related communication modes. Customers outsource campaigns or ongoing service queues and commonly pay per agent hour, productive hour, contact, seat, transaction, sale, qualified lead, outcome, dedicated team, or fixed monthly capacity, often under service-level and quality commitments.

records.title: Building Inspection Services
records.search_text: Private lower-middle-market firms whose primary service is outsourced inspection of residential or commercial buildings, including home inspections, property-condition inspections, and repeat inspection programs for external customers. Customers include homebuyers and sellers, property owners and managers, lenders, insurers, developers, and public or institutional clients. Residential work is generally sold as a fixed inspection fee adjusted for property size and add-on tests; larger commercial and programmatic work may use project fees, unit-price schedules, or recurring service contracts. Repeat business often comes through referral channels and portfolio or construction relationships even when each property produces a separate report.

records.title: Automatic Environmental Control Manufacturing for Residential, Commercial, and Appliance Use
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of automatic controls and regulators for heating, air-conditioning, refrigeration, buildings, residences, and appliances that repeatedly supply external customers, including associated engineering, configuration, and support. Product and system sales to OEMs, distributors, contractors, building owners, and appliance makers, often supplemented by engineering, software, configuration, replacement, and support revenue; repeat demand comes from production programs, replacement cycles, retrofits, and installed-base support.

records.title: All Other Automotive Repair and Maintenance
records.search_text: Independent automotive air-conditioning service, tire repair, lubrication, safety and emissions inspection, diagnostic service without repair, rustproofing, undercoating, spray-on bed liners, and other automotive maintenance not classified elsewhere. Local vehicle owners and fleets buy episodic inspections, diagnostics, tire repairs, fluid services, and protective treatments through per-job, menu-priced, or labor-plus-materials charges.

records.title: Travel Agencies
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market travel agencies repeatedly arranging and selling business or leisure transportation, accommodation, cruises, excursions, and related travel services for external customers, including agencies serving repeat leisure clients and contracted corporate accounts. Leisure travelers and business accounts buy advice, itinerary planning, booking, ticketing, changes, disruption support, and travel-program services.
