records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers in the band that repeatedly replenish non-living, nondurable supplies for external retailers, commercial users, and manufacturers, including pet supplies excluding food and live pets, art supplies, textile bags and canvas goods, industrial yarns, brushes, plant food, packaging-like goods, and similar catalog products. Repeat product distribution through quoted, catalog, contract, or account prices, supported by inventory ownership, sourcing, credit, order processing, customer service, and delivery coordination.

records.title: Other Electronic Component Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying engineered electronic component families—such as RF and microwave components, crystals and frequency devices, electronic filters and switches, transducers, specialty displays, piezoelectric devices, laminates, and electronic harness assemblies—to external OEMs, contract manufacturers, and distributors. Revenue is primarily unit or lot sales under purchase orders, blanket orders, production releases, or platform programs. Custom engineering, qualification, reliability, and application support are generally embedded in component pricing; repeat revenue follows customer product lifecycles, replacement demand, and recurring distributor replenishment.

records.title: Plumbing and Heating Equipment and Supplies (Hydronics) Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers repeatedly supplying plumbing equipment and supplies, hydronic heating equipment, household water heaters, pipe, valves, fittings, fixtures, and related products to contractors, builders, industrial and institutional users, municipalities, and other external customers, including specification, ordering, sourcing, stocking, logistics, credit, returns, warranty, and product-support work. Recurring and repeat B2B product sales earned mainly through merchandise gross margin, sometimes supplemented by freight, fabrication, kitting, delivery, project staging, inventory-management, specification, training, warranty, or other value-added services.

records.title: Automotive Body, Paint, and Interior Repair and Maintenance
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing repeat outsourced collision, body, paint, trailer-body, interior repair, restoration, and vehicle customization services to external consumers, fleets, and insurers. Repair orders funded by vehicle owners, insurers, fleets, or warranty administrators, with revenue from labor, paint and materials, parts markup, supplements, storage, and related shop charges.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Chemical Product and Preparation Manufacturing
records.search_text: US LMM manufacturers that repeatedly formulate, blend, activate, package, or custom-produce miscellaneous chemical preparations for external customers, including activated carbon, water-treatment compounds, industrial process aids, lubricants and maintenance chemicals, and other recurring specialty formulations captured by 325998. Repeat B2B or channel sales of consumable formulations and materials, often supported by technical service, qualification, application know-how, private-label arrangements, or recurring custom production; some branded maintenance products also sell through distribution.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Supplies and New Parts Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US warehouse distributors and jobbers that repeatedly source, stock, pick, sell, and deliver new motor-vehicle parts, supplies, accessories, tools, equipment, batteries, and automotive glass to other wholesalers, retailers, repair shops, fleets, and business users. Repeat B2B customers place replenishment and repair-driven orders from stocked assortments; distributors earn product gross margin and may provide cataloging, fitment support, availability, fulfillment, delivery, returns, warranty handling, and credit.

records.title: Special Needs Transportation
records.search_text: Private lower-middle-market operators repeatedly transporting elderly, disabled, infirm, or otherwise special-needs passengers, including nonemergency medical transportation and contracted paratransit, using accessible or conventional passenger vehicles. Recurring and repeat trips paid by Medicaid agencies, managed-care plans, transportation brokers, transit authorities, care institutions, and private-pay passengers; revenue commonly follows awarded contracts or authorized fee-for-service trips, mileage, and vehicle/service categories.

records.title: Metal Crown, Closure, and Other Metal Stamping (except Automotive)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly stamping metal crowns, bottle caps, canning lids and rings, other unfinished non-automotive metal stampings, and spun unfinished metal products for external packaging, appliance, electrical, industrial, consumer-product, and equipment customers. Recurring releases and production programs priced per part, thousand units, pound, lot, or program, commonly supported by customer-specific dies, specifications, quality documentation, and metal-price adjustments.

records.title: Metal Tank (Heavy Gauge) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying heavy-gauge metal storage tanks, pressure tanks and vessels, gas cylinders, custom-fabricated and field-erected tanks, and related repeat contract fabrication or field services to external customers. Configured or custom equipment is sold under quoted purchase orders and project contracts to industrial, energy, chemical, water, agricultural, and infrastructure customers; repeat revenue can include replacement vessels, expansions, field erection, repairs, and related fabrication.

records.title: Child Care Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators providing recurring center-based infant, toddler, preschool, before-school, after-school, or employer-sponsored child care and early-learning services to external families, employers, or subsidy programs. Revenue is commonly recurring tuition paid by families in advance, supplemented in some centers by employer management fees or operating subsidies, public child-care subsidies, Head Start or similar program funding, food-program support, and enrollment-related fees. Tuition varies by child age, schedule, location, and program quality.

records.title: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events with Facilities
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly manage and staff arenas, stadiums, theaters, music halls, fairgrounds, and similar facilities for external promoters, event organizers, sponsors, renters, and attendees, including recurring venue booking, ticketing, marketing, event settlement, and facility operations. Customers include promoters, artists, corporate and private renters, sponsors, municipalities, and event attendees. Revenue commonly combines management or rental fees with tickets and ticketing fees, sponsorship, premium space, parking, merchandise commissions, food and beverage, and event-profit shares.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Body Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing truck, bus, and automobile bodies or cabs, including final-stage assembly of vocational and special-purpose vehicles on purchased chassis, for external commercial, fleet, dealer, and public customers. Configured product sales and repeat fleet, dealer, OEM, or municipal orders, with revenue earned per body or completed vehicle and often supplemented by options, parts, and warranty support.

records.title: Oil and Gas Field Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacture of oil and gas field drilling and production machinery, derricks, and water-well drilling machinery, including related aftermarket activity attributable to the manufacturer. Recurring or repeat revenue from external customers through equipment programs, replacement units, spare parts, repair, technical support, field service, training, or manufacturer-linked monitoring.

records.title: Wood Container and Pallet Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market wood pallet, box, crate, and container manufacturers that repeatedly provide outsourced specification, production, inspection, repair-support, inventory, or managed-supply services to external industrial, retail, food, logistics, and distribution customers. Eligible firms earn repeat B2B revenue from customer-specific pallets, crates, boxes, and related managed supply, often priced per unit or program and supplemented by inspection, repair coordination, inventory, or service commitments.

records.title: Other Chemical and Allied Products Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers repeatedly sourcing, storing, documenting, selling, and delivering industrial chemicals and allied products to external business customers, in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. Repeat B2B product distribution, generally earning a gross spread and sometimes delivery, handling, blending, packaging, inventory-management, or technical-service fees; customer relationships range from negotiated accounts to spot orders.

records.title: Industrial Building Construction
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market industrial-building general contractors, design-build firms, and construction-management firms that repeatedly deliver new construction, additions, alterations, maintenance, or repairs for external industrial customers. Project-based revenue from industrial owners under competitively bid or negotiated fixed-price, guaranteed-maximum-price, cost-plus, design-build, and construction-management arrangements; repeat customers and follow-on projects create the repeat-service relationship even though individual projects are finite.

records.title: Meat and Meat Product Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US meat and meat-product merchant wholesalers in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly source, receive, hold, cut where incidental to wholesaling, sell, and distribute fresh, non-packaged frozen, cured, smoked, deli, and processed red-meat products to restaurants, retailers, institutions, and other food businesses. Repeat B2B product sales using product markup and commonly bundling procurement, cold storage, lot control, order cutting or assembly, delivery, and recall or shortage response.

records.title: Pharmaceutical Preparation Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of finished pharmaceutical preparations serving repeat external drug sponsors, brands, wholesalers, health-system channels, or private-label customers. Repeat batch, product, and contract-manufacturing revenue under supply agreements, purchase orders, private-label arrangements, and sponsor quality agreements; pricing is generally per batch, unit, or product rather than per labor hour.

records.title: Fish and Seafood Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US fish and seafood merchant wholesalers in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly source, receive, hold, sell, and distribute fresh, frozen, and prepared fish or shellfish to restaurants, retailers, institutions, and other food businesses. Repeat B2B product sales using product markup and often combining procurement, quality grading, cold storage, traceability, order assembly, delivery, and shortage or spoilage resolution.

records.title: Remediation Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms performing recurring or repeat outsourced cleanup of contaminated buildings, soil, groundwater, and mine sites; asbestos, lead, mold, and other toxic-material abatement; environmental emergency response; and integrated reclamation for external public- and private-sector customers. Government agencies, industrial owners, property owners, developers, utilities, mines, and responsible parties purchase project-based cleanup, abatement, emergency response, monitoring, and long-term remedial operations through fixed-price, cost-reimbursement, time-and-materials, unit-price, and indefinite-delivery task-order contracts.

records.title: Other Grocery and Related Products Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent merchant wholesalers repeatedly buying and reselling the grocery and related products classified in 424490, including bakery products, canned foods, dried or canned dairy and meat products, soft drinks, bottled water processed by others, and other grocery specialties, to external retail, foodservice, institutional, and wholesale customers. Repeat B2B product resale earning a merchant gross margin, with varying combinations of sourcing, assortment, warehousing, route delivery, merchandising, inventory availability, and customer service.

records.title: Recyclable Material Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market recyclable-material merchants with repeat industrial, commercial, municipal, or dealer counterparties that source, grade, process, aggregate, broker while taking title to, and sell ferrous metal, nonferrous metal, paper, plastics, electronics, automotive scrap, or other secondary materials. Repeat purchases of scrap and sales of graded secondary commodities to mills, foundries, processors, exporters, and manufacturers; economics combine commodity spread, processing and upgrading yield, logistics, scale aggregation, and sometimes recurring container, pickup, destruction, or compliance services.

records.title: Aircraft Engine and Engine Parts Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers of certified aircraft-engine components and replacement parts under recurring OEM, Tier-1, defense, or FAA-PMA programs, including precision machining, forming, casting, additive production, coatings, welding, brazing, inspection, and assembly of hot-section, rotating, structural, fuel, and accessory components. Suppliers earn per-part or lot revenue under long-term, requirements-based engine-platform agreements, purchase orders, defense contracts, and qualified-source programs, with separate nonrecurring engineering, tooling, qualification, and sometimes aftermarket or repair revenue. Volumes are normally released rather than guaranteed; quality, traceability, delivery, cost-down, and airworthiness obligations are central.

records.title: Financial Transactions Processing, Reserve, and Clearinghouse Activities
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms providing recurring external financial transaction processing, authorization, clearing, settlement, electronic funds transfer, payment gateway, reserve, liquidity, and non-central-bank clearinghouse services. Financial institutions, merchants, platforms, governments, and other enterprises pay account-based, transaction-based, volume-based, network-access, gateway, maintenance, support, and value-added service fees, often under multi-year contracts and net of client incentives or partner revenue shares.

records.title: All Other Outpatient Care Centers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market outpatient centers with medical staff repeatedly delivering general or specialized care to external patients and payors, including multispecialty community clinics, pain therapy centers, sleep disorder centers, and biofeedback centers. Centers provide recurring visits, diagnostics, treatment, care coordination, and monitoring to patients. Revenue can combine Medicare or Medicaid prospective or fee-schedule payments, commercial insurer contracts, patient cost sharing, grants, and sliding-fee payments; the mix varies sharply between community health centers and commercial specialty clinics.

records.title: Gasket, Packing, and Sealing Device Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market gasket, packing, and sealing-device manufacturers that repeatedly provide external OEM and maintenance customers with application engineering, material selection, prototyping, custom conversion or molding, qualification, production, replenishment, and technical support. Eligible firms earn repeat B2B revenue from engineered-to-specification production, replacement and maintenance demand, scheduled replenishment, and customer-specific sealing programs across industrial, transport, energy, food, pharmaceutical, aerospace, and other critical applications.

records.title: Other Miscellaneous Durable Goods Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers in the EBITDA band that repeatedly source, hold, sell, and coordinate delivery of miscellaneous durable goods for external business customers, including recurring account service and order administration. Revenue is principally product resale to business accounts; the recurring outsourced service is supplier aggregation, availability, ordering, inventory coordination, and delivery support, monetized through gross margin rather than a separately billed fee.

records.title: Corporate, Subsidiary, and Regional Managing Offices
records.search_text: Only the rare edge case in which an establishment recorded in 551114 provides recurring, paid management or administrative services to unaffiliated external customers with meaningful operational accountability. Because the official definition requires management of affiliated establishments within the same enterprise, this included population is expected to consist mainly of classification leakage or short-lived transition arrangements rather than the normal industry population. The standard 551114 establishment is funded by its parent or affiliates through budgets and intercompany cost allocations, not by arm's-length external customers. A qualifying edge case would need recurring third-party management agreements and externally paid fees, with the provider accountable for delivery rather than merely owning or financing the client.

records.title: Motion Picture and Video Production
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market companies that repeatedly develop, finance, organize, produce, or co-produce motion pictures, television programs, commercials, music videos, and other video for external buyers, platforms, advertisers, or distribution partners, including producers that also distribute their own productions. Revenue is earned through repeated project fees, production and executive-producer fees, fixed or variable platform licenses, cost reimbursements, ownership participations, and exploitation of completed titles across release windows; cash flows are slate- and project-based rather than subscription-like.

records.title: Car Washes
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market fixed-site professional car-wash operators providing conveyorized express-exterior or staffed exterior-and-interior cleaning, washing, waxing, vacuuming, and membership services to retail and fleet customers. Retail and corporate customers buy single washes, prepaid packages, or recurring monthly memberships. Fixed sites combine automated payment and conveyor equipment with traffic management, customer assistance, chemistry control, equipment maintenance, site safety, and, for full-service formats, manual vacuuming and interior cleaning.

records.title: Rolling Mill and Other Metalworking Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying rolling-mill equipment, roll machines, wire drawing and fabricating machinery, coil winding and cutting machinery, wire assembly machines, and other metalworking machinery outside the specified machine-tool categories to external metal producers and fabricators. Engineered capital-equipment and line sales, retrofits, replacement parts, controls and automation upgrades, field service, and recurring maintenance support, generally sold through negotiated projects, bids, and installed-base relationships.

records.title: Dairy Product (except Dried or Canned) Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, take title to, hold, sell, and distribute raw milk, fluid milk, cheese, butter, cream, yogurt, ice cream, and other dairy products except dried or canned products to external business customers. Recurring and repeat business-to-business product sales with a wholesale gross margin compensating the distributor for sourcing, temperature control, inventory, delivery, trade credit, account service, product availability, and handling of short-dated goods.

records.title: Brick, Stone, and Related Construction Material Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, inventory, quote, sell, dispatch, and support stone, cement, lime, sand, gravel, brick, asphalt and concrete mixtures, and concrete, stone, or structural-clay products for external construction customers. Repeat product sales to masonry, concrete, paving, civil, landscape, residential, and commercial contractors and dealers; the wholesaler earns merchandise margin while providing local availability, credit, estimating or product support, load coordination, and jobsite delivery.

records.title: Food Product Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of food and beverage processing machinery, including dairy, bakery, meat and poultry, and other commercial food-product processing equipment, plus manufacturer-attributable replacement, rebuild, parts, service, and monitoring revenue. Recurring or repeat outsourced revenue from external food and beverage processors through equipment programs, replacement units, aftermarket parts, rebuilds, maintenance, service agreements, leases, and manufacturer-linked software.

records.title: Analytical Laboratory Instrument Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of analytical laboratory instruments and systems that repeatedly serve external laboratories, including chromatography, spectroscopy, mass spectrometry, chemical and physical analyzers, related software, accessories, consumables, calibration, maintenance, and support. Instrument and system sales to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, chemical, environmental, food, academic, government, and industrial laboratories, with repeat revenue from columns and other consumables, accessories, service contracts, qualification, calibration, software, upgrades, training, and replacement.

records.title: Skiing Facilities
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market ski-area operators providing repeat lift access and related rentals, lessons, food service, snowmaking, grooming and guest services to external customers. Direct consumer revenue from day tickets and advance season passes, with repeat ancillary spending on lessons, rentals, dining and other mountain services; operations are highly seasonal and weather-dependent.

records.title: Jewelry, Watch, Precious Stone, and Precious Metal Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers with repeat B2B accounts that source, authenticate or quality-control, finance, merchandise, and distribute finished jewelry, watches, clocks, precious or semiprecious stones, precious metals, costume jewelry, silverware, or jewelers' findings to independent retailers and other business buyers. Repeat product sales to independent jewelers, chains, designers, repair shops, online sellers, and specialty retailers; revenue is product resale spread, supported in stronger firms by sourcing access, credit, memo or consignment programs, authentication, assortment curation, compliance, merchandising, and fulfillment.

records.title: Recreational Vehicle Dealers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market recreational-vehicle dealers that sell new or used RVs and provide repeat external-customer service through repair, warranty work, parts and accessories, inspections, storage, consignment support, or related ownership services. Households purchase or consign RVs and may return for customer-pay repairs, warranty work, parts, accessories, inspections, winterization, storage, and ownership support; revenue mixes high-ticket transactional unit sales and F&I with recurring labor, parts, and service work.

records.title: School and Employee Bus Transportation
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market private operators providing recurring outsourced school-pupil or employee bus transportation to school districts, schools, employers, and institutions under route, bus-day, unit-rate, or fixed-price service arrangements. Multi-route recurring service paid primarily by public school districts, private schools, employers, or institutions, commonly under competitively awarded fixed-price or per-bus, per-pupil, per-mile, route, or bus-day contracts with defined renewal and escalation terms.

records.title: Other Building Equipment Contractors
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market contractors in NAICS 238290 that repeatedly install, inspect, maintain, repair, modernize, or rig building and industrial equipment for external customers, including elevators and escalators, commercial doors, conveyors, millwright work, and related equipment services. Commercial, institutional, industrial, government, and multifamily customers buy project installation or modernization, recurring preventive-maintenance agreements, inspections, emergency callouts, and time-and-materials repairs. Fixed monthly service fees and contract escalators coexist with competitively bid projects and separately billed extra work.

records.title: Painting and Wall Covering Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors providing outsourced interior or exterior painting, coatings, surface preparation, and wall-covering installation or removal for residential, commercial, institutional, industrial, or infrastructure customers, including repaint, maintenance, repair, alteration, and new-work jobs. Contractors sell labor-and-material project delivery, usually through a quoted job, bid, unit-price, or subcontract arrangement; repeat work comes from property managers, general contractors, institutional and industrial accounts, and recurring repaint or turnover needs, while homeowner work is more episodic.

records.title: General Medical and Surgical Hospitals
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market hospital operating firms repeatedly providing inpatient diagnostic, medical, surgical, nursing, accommodation, and related outpatient services to external patients and payers through a transferable operating business. Recurring patient episodes reimbursed by Medicare, Medicaid, commercial insurers, employers, and patients through case-based prospective payments, negotiated rates, fee schedules, bundled arrangements, and limited cost-based or supplemental payments.

records.title: Transportation Equipment and Supplies (except Motor Vehicle) Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers supplying recurring aftermarket parts, components, consumables, spares, exchange units, and related sourcing, inventory, documentation, and fulfillment services for aircraft, marine vessels, rail equipment, and other non-motor-vehicle transportation assets. Product and exchange gross margin from repeat parts procurement by airlines, MROs, operators, government agencies, lessors, and transportation fleets, often supplemented by AOG response, pooling, consignment, inventory management, documentation, sourcing, repair coordination, and logistics fees.

records.title: Radio and Television Broadcasting and Wireless Communications Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. firms repeatedly designing, assembling, manufacturing, testing, and selling wireless communications, antenna, GPS, mobile radio, cable television, and broadcast or studio transmission equipment to external customers. Repeat equipment, platform, accessory, license, support, and service sales to carriers, broadcasters, cable operators, enterprises, governments, public-safety agencies, defense customers, integrators, distributors, and consumers, priced by performance, frequency, capacity, configuration, qualification, volume, support, and procurement terms.

records.title: Drinking Places (Alcoholic Beverages)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating bars, taverns, pubs, nightclubs, and other drinking places whose primary business is preparing and serving alcoholic beverages for immediate on-premises consumption as a repeat hospitality service to external customers. Walk-in and repeat consumers generally pay posted menu prices for beverages, tabs, cover charges, and occasional event minimums; revenue is predominantly transactional rather than governed by long-term customer contracts.

records.title: Other Construction Material Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers repeatedly stocking, quoting, selling, and delivering miscellaneous construction inputs such as flat and plate glass, nonwood fencing and accessories, ceiling and gypsum products, building paper, mastics, culvert pipe, storage bins, and architectural metalwork. Repeat B2B resale to contractors, fabricators, installers, builders, dealers, and facilities customers, with product gross margin paying for local availability, technical selection, quoting, trade credit, and delivery.

records.title: Glass Product Manufacturing Made of Purchased Glass
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly coat, laminate, temper, cut, bend, or otherwise shape purchased glass for external architectural, residential, transportation, appliance, furniture, or industrial customers. Repeat business-to-business fabrication orders, often job-, unit-, square-foot-, or program-priced to drawings and performance specifications, with revenue from processed glass and associated fabrication rather than a separately metered labor service.

records.title: Cut Stone and Stone Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly template, cut, finish, and fabricate stone products to customer specifications, including countertop, architectural, monument, and commercial stone work sold to external customers. Project and repeat-order revenue from builders, remodelers, designers, institutions, and end customers, commonly quoted per square foot or as a fixed project price covering material, fabrication, options, and sometimes installation.

records.title: Rubber Product Manufacturing for Mechanical Use
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly molding, extruding, or lathe-cutting rubber goods other than tubing for mechanical applications in motor vehicles, machinery, and equipment, sold to external OEM, tier, industrial, and replacement customers. Recurring purchase orders and multi-period OEM or tier supply programs for custom or catalog molded mechanical parts, typically priced per part with tooling and engineering charges sometimes separate.

records.title: Other Warehousing and Storage
records.search_text: Independent U.S. LMM operators providing recurring outsourced nonrefrigerated specialized physical-goods storage and handling, including bulk petroleum, chemicals, automobiles, lumber, whiskey, and other goods requiring dedicated tanks, yards, buildings, security, equipment, or regulatory controls. Industrial, commercial, and institutional customers outsource custody and handling of specialized goods. Revenue generally combines recurring time- or capacity-based storage charges with receiving, movement, loadout, transaction, compliance, and other value-added activity fees; dedicated arrangements may use negotiated all-inclusive or cost-plus contracts.

records.title: Flower, Nursery Stock, and Florists' Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent merchant wholesalers repeatedly sourcing, receiving, conditioning, merchandising, holding, selling, and delivering cut flowers, nursery stock, plants, and florist supplies to external florists, garden centers, landscapers, event professionals, institutions, and retailers. Repeat B2B product sales, generally earning a wholesale spread and sometimes delivery or service fees; fresh-product value also depends on availability, grading, freshness, conditioning, cold-chain execution, credit, and rapid exception resolution.

records.title: Computer and Computer Peripheral Equipment and Software Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent value-added resellers and IT distributors that repeatedly supply external customers with technology procurement plus managed services, configuration, deployment, lifecycle support, cloud, cybersecurity, or related accountable services, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Business, public-sector, and institutional customers buy hardware and software at product margins and may also buy project, maintenance, managed-service, cloud, security, and lifecycle work through recurring contracts or repeat statements of work.

records.title: Medical, Dental, and Hospital Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market medical, dental, and hospital equipment or supply distributors that repeatedly provide external providers with outsourced inventory, fulfillment, equipment installation and repair, technical support, formulary or sourcing support, recall and traceability coordination, or home-delivery services. Physician and dental offices, laboratories, ambulatory sites, hospitals, health systems, government, and home-care patients buy recurring consumables and episodic equipment at distribution margins, often with repeat logistics, inventory, technical, installation, maintenance, traceability, or support services.

records.title: Pharmacy Benefit Management and Other Third Party Administration of Insurance and Pension Funds
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market, non-risk-bearing third-party administrators of employer health, welfare, insurance, self-insurance, and pension plans. Included work covers eligibility and enrollment, claims and reimbursement administration, account and recordkeeping operations, participant service, compliance support, reconciliation, and reporting. Employers, health plans, insurers, financial institutions, payroll firms, and benefit intermediaries buy recurring software-enabled administration. Revenue is commonly based on participants, accounts, transactions, or contracted service scope, with implementation and ancillary service fees.

records.title: Industrial and Personal Service Paper Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly procure, hold inventory, quote, deliver, and replenish industrial paper, paperboard, sanitary paper, shipping supplies, foodservice disposables, and related disposable plastics for external commercial and institutional customers. Recurring product resale margin plus service embedded in sourcing, inventory availability, product selection, order handling, breaking bulk, local delivery, and exception resolution; customers generally pay per order under negotiated account pricing.

records.title: Corrugated and Solid Fiber Box Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US corrugated and solid-fiber box manufacturers in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly design, convert, print, and supply boxes to external customers. Recurring business-to-business sales of custom or standard corrugated boxes under quotes, purchase orders, supply agreements, and recurring replenishment programs, commonly priced per thousand square feet or per unit with board and freight adjustments.

records.title: Caterers
records.search_text: US catering firms in the lower-middle-market band that prepare, deliver, and often serve food for customer events, with repeat demand from corporate clients, institutions, venues, event planners, or recurring social-event referral channels. External corporate, nonprofit, public-sector, venue, planner, and household customers usually buy a defined event package or per-guest service. Revenue is project-like at the event level; repeatability comes from recurring customer events, venue and planner referrals, and framework or preferred-vendor relationships.

records.title: Industrial and Commercial Fan and Blower and Air Purification Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US firms in NAICS 333413 that supply recurring or repeat outsourced work to external customers through engineered fan, blower, dust-collection, filtration, or stationary air-purification equipment programs and associated parts, filters, controls, upgrades, maintenance, diagnostics, or technical support, within the lower-middle-market band. Customers include industrial plants, commercial buildings, institutions, contractors, and equipment integrators. Revenue can combine engineered equipment orders with repeat replacement projects, filters and wear parts, controls, upgrades, field service, inspection, commissioning, and technical support tied to an installed base.

records.title: Real Estate Credit
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operating nondepository lenders primarily lending funds secured by real estate to external borrowers, including mortgage banking and home-equity, residential, and commercial real-estate credit operations with transferable origination, underwriting, funding, compliance, and closing capabilities. Operators earn interest spread, origination points and fees, warehouse or gain-on-sale economics, and sometimes ancillary closing revenue; results depend on funded volume, rates, funding lines, secondary-market execution, collateral values, credit losses, and product mix.

records.title: Farm Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers of farm, agricultural, turf, and grounds-care machinery, implements, attachments, replacement assemblies, and repeat engineered equipment sold to external dealers, farms, landscapers, or OEMs. Repeat B2B equipment and parts sales through dealers, OEM programs, direct farm accounts, and replacement cycles, with engineering, configuration, warranty, parts, and technical support.

records.title: Switchgear and Switchboard Apparatus Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying switchgear, switchboards, distribution panels, power circuit breakers, fuses, bus duct, and related engineered assemblies, replacement equipment, commissioning, or repeat support to external utility, data-center, industrial, commercial, and infrastructure customers. Configured and custom electrical equipment is sold through purchase orders and project contracts, often fixed-price and long-cycle, with repeat revenue from expansions, spare parts, replacement breakers, upgrades, commissioning, and installed-base support.

records.title: Medicinal and Botanical Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of medicinal chemicals, active ingredients, botanical extracts, and related bulk preparations supplied repeatedly to external pharmaceutical, supplement, or formulation customers. Recurring or repeat batch and product sales under supply, contract-manufacturing, private-label, or purchase-order relationships, commonly with quality specifications and customer qualification requirements.

records.title: Toilet Preparation Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers and contract manufacturers that repeatedly formulate, blend, fill, package, and support perfumes, hair and skin preparations, lotions, sunscreens, and other cosmetics for external brands or channels. Repeat B2B contract-manufacturing and private-label revenue, plus repeat wholesale sales of owned formulations, with formulation, compliance, testing, filling, and packaging support.

records.title: Measuring, Dispensing, and Other Pumping Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of measuring and dispensing pumps, centrifugal, turbine, reciprocating, rotary, diaphragm, water-system, oilfield, sump, and related pumping equipment and parts sold repeatedly to external industrial, municipal, commercial, agricultural, energy, and distribution customers. Business-to-business and business-to-government sales of configured equipment, replacement pumps, and parts through direct sales, specifications, distributors, and projects; repeat revenue follows installed-base replacement, maintenance, efficiency upgrades, and customer capital programs.

records.title: Plastics Materials and Basic Forms and Shapes Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers of plastic resins, compounds, unsupported film and sheet, rod, tube, and other basic shapes that repeatedly serve external processors, fabricators, OEMs, contractors, and institutional customers through sourcing, inventory, technical selection, cutting or other light value-added preparation, and logistics. Distributors buy materials on their own account and earn a gross spread while providing repeat availability, supplier access, credit, material and regulatory guidance, inventory buffering, cut-to-size or light fabrication, and delivery coordination. Revenue is predominantly product sales rather than separately billed service fees.

records.title: General Freight Trucking, Local
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market employer firms providing recurring or repeat, for-hire local transportation of palletized general freight for external shippers, including local truckload and less-than-truckload operations and incidental terminal or storage activity, typically on same-day metropolitan-area routes. Business shippers and logistics intermediaries buy recurring lane, route, pickup-and-delivery, or dedicated-capacity service under negotiated contracts, with overflow and irregular loads priced transactionally; revenue is generally tied to loads, miles, stops, weight, time, or committed capacity.

records.title: Institutional Furniture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of stock or custom furniture for schools, libraries, theaters, churches, public buildings, and similar institutions, supplied repeatedly through dealers, architects, contractors, purchasing cooperatives, public bids, and direct institutional accounts. Repeat B2B and public-sector product revenue through catalog, dealer, specification, cooperative contract, bid, renovation, and project channels, priced per SKU or configured package with freight, installation coordination, warranties, discounts, and contract renewals.

records.title: Truck Trailer Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing truck trailers, semitrailers, truck-trailer chassis, cargo-container chassis, detachable trailer bodies, and detachable chassis for external fleets, dealers, leasing companies, and logistics customers. Product revenue from repeat fleet orders and dealer sales of standard or configured dry van, refrigerated, flatbed, dump, tank, specialty, and chassis equipment, with options and parts as ancillary revenue.

records.title: Power and Communication Line and Related Structures Construction
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors that repeatedly build, upgrade, maintain, or repair electric-power and communications lines, substations, towers, and related structures for utilities, telecommunications carriers, public agencies, and infrastructure owners. Project, task-order, master-service-agreement, storm-response, and maintenance revenue, commonly bid as lump-sum, unit-price, or negotiated work for utilities, carriers, governments, and infrastructure owners.

records.title: Crushed and Broken Limestone Mining and Quarrying
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market operators principally developing quarry sites, mining or quarrying crushed and broken limestone and related calcareous rocks, and mechanically beneficiating limestone through crushing, grinding, pulverizing, screening, or sizing for construction aggregate, cement and lime feedstock, agriculture, and industrial uses. High-volume, shipment-based B2B sales to road contractors, ready-mix and asphalt producers, cement and lime plants, agricultural distributors, industrial users, and public infrastructure projects. Economics depend on permitted reserves, plant throughput, product gradations, quality, utilization, energy and maintenance, and delivered freight radius.

records.title: Musical Groups and Artists
records.search_text: US ensemble operating companies in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly provide live musical performances to venues, presenters, event clients, ticket buyers, sponsors, and institutions, including transferable bands, orchestras, chamber groups, and multi-ensemble operators. Repeated fixed-fee engagements, guarantees, door or ticket-share arrangements, self-produced ticketed performances, residencies, subscriptions, sponsorships, merchandise, and, for some orchestras, grants and contributions; delivery is project- or season-based rather than continuously contracted.

records.title: Showcase, Partition, Shelving, and Locker Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying stock or custom wood and nonwood showcases, store and office fixtures, partitions, shelving, lockers, frames, laminated fixture tops, and furniture parts to external retail, warehouse, office, education, healthcare, industrial, and government customers. Project bids, dealer and distributor orders, roll-out programs, catalog replenishment, architect or contractor specifications, and repeat replacement or expansion purchases, generally priced per unit, bay, fixture package, lot, or project.

records.title: Tour Operators
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators repeatedly designing, assembling, selling, and delivering multi-day or packaged tours for external leisure, affinity, educational, or group customers, including operators selling directly and through travel advisors or online distributors. Travelers, groups, institutions, affinity organizations, and travel-advisor channels buy packaged itineraries that combine planning, lodging, transportation, activities, guides, logistics, and on-trip support. Operators commonly set a package price, collect deposits before departure, pay suppliers and distribution commissions, and recognize revenue while the tour is delivered.

records.title: Wineries
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. winery operators that repeatedly produce, age, blend, bottle, or supply wine for external wholesale, retail, wine-club, private-label, or custom-crush customers, including estate wineries with a material production business. Recurring case, bottle, bulk, private-label, and custom-production sales to distributors, retailers, restaurants, club members, and brand owners, with wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels often coexisting.

records.title: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of non-vehicle air-conditioning, heat pumps, warm-air furnaces, humidification and dehumidification equipment, and commercial or industrial refrigeration and freezer equipment supplied repeatedly to distributors, contractors, OEMs, institutions, food and retail customers, and industrial users. Repeat B2B equipment, component, replacement-part, and support revenue through distributors, contractors, OEM programs, specifications, private-label relationships, and projects, with unit or configured-system pricing, rebates, bids, warranties, and refrigerant or material transition costs.

records.title: Hardware Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers repeatedly supplying hardware, knives, handtools, fasteners, and related maintenance, repair, construction, industrial, and dealer products to contractors, industrial and institutional users, retailers, and other external business customers, including ordering, sourcing, stocking, kitting, logistics, credit, returns, and product-support work. Recurring and repeat B2B product sales earned mainly through merchandise gross margin, sometimes supplemented by freight, vending or inventory-management programs, kitting, cutting, labeling, delivery, technical assistance, or other value-added services.

records.title: Truck, Utility Trailer, and RV (Recreational Vehicle) Rental and Leasing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms renting or leasing trucks, truck tractors, buses, semi-trailers, utility trailers, or recreational vehicles without drivers to external commercial or consumer customers, including repeat short-term rental and recurring full-service lease relationships. The code mixes short-duration transaction revenue from consumer and commercial rentals with multi-year commercial leases and bundled maintenance, fuel, compliance, and digital fleet services. Customers pay time- and usage-based rental charges or recurring lease and service fees.

records.title: Other Professional Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US merchant wholesalers of nonmedical laboratory and scientific instruments, surveying and drafting equipment, school and church supplies, veterinary equipment, and similar professional supplies, where the firm repeatedly sources, stocks, advises on, and delivers products for external customers. Predominantly B2B and institutional product resale margins, often supplemented by repeat consumables, technical product selection, installation, calibration, repair, logistics, and account support.

records.title: Urethane and Other Foam Product (except Polystyrene) Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying polyurethane and other non-polystyrene foam bunstock, molded and fabricated components, cushioning, bedding and furniture foam, automotive and appliance foam, insulation, seals, medical and protective packaging products to external customers. Repeat B2B sales under purchase orders, blanket agreements, OEM programs, project quotes, and distributor relationships, priced by unit, volume, weight, board, fabricated part, or run; the code spans commodity slabstock and differentiated molded, medical, insulation, transportation, and sealing applications.

records.title: Roofing Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing, replacing, repairing, coating, inspecting, and maintaining residential, commercial, and institutional roof systems for external customers, including firms whose recurring activity is a sequence of projects, storm-restoration jobs, maintenance calls, and reroofing work. Revenue comes from fixed-price bids, negotiated commercial contracts, unit-priced work, insurance-funded residential restoration, service and maintenance agreements, and time-and-material repairs for homeowners, property managers, general contractors, institutions, and commercial building owners.

records.title: Motor and Generator Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly engineering, winding, assembling, testing, rebuilding on a factory basis, and supplying electric motors, power generators, and motor-generator sets to external OEM, industrial, commercial, utility, contractor, and distributor customers. Quoted per-unit, program, or project sales, often configured or engineered to OEM and industrial specifications, with design, materials, testing, documentation, warranty, repair, and lifecycle support bundled under repeat accounts.

records.title: All Other Transit and Ground Passenger Transportation
records.search_text: Private lower-middle-market operators of recurring or repeat shuttle, airport-transfer, hotel-transfer, carpool, vanpool, and other scheduled ground-passenger services for external customers or contracting organizations. Repeat passenger fares, monthly vanpool payments, route or service contracts with airports, hotels, parking operators, employers, campuses, and transit sponsors, and in some cases public operating or capital support.

records.title: Confectionery Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US confectionery merchant wholesalers in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly source, hold, sell, and distribute candy, chocolate, chewing gum, and related confectionery to retailers, foodservice customers, and institutions. Repeat B2B product sales, commonly combining product markup with account service, order processing, inventory availability, delivery, merchandising support, and seasonal promotion execution.

records.title: Other Direct Insurance (except Life, Health, and Medical) Carriers
records.search_text: Privately controlled direct carriers underwriting recurring product-warranty and other non-life, non-health insurance, with emphasis on policy administration, underwriting, claims, fraud review, customer service, compliance, and partner-account management. The carrier earns recurring premiums or economically similar underwriting revenue from consumers, manufacturers, retailers, dealers, or affinity partners, assumes insured risk, pays covered claims, and retains underwriting and investment economics after claims, commissions, and operating expenses.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Electrical and Electronic Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers of vehicle electrical, electronic, power-control, lighting, sensing, wiring, and related equipment for repeat external OEM and aftermarket customers. Repeat per-unit supply under OEM programs, qualified contracts, distributor replenishment, and aftermarket orders.

records.title: Construction Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers of construction, surface-mining, and logging machinery, attachments, wear parts, portable processing equipment, and repeat engineered assemblies sold to external dealers, contractors, mines, rental fleets, or OEMs. Repeat equipment, attachment, wear-part, replacement, and OEM-program sales, supported by configuration, engineering, warranty, parts, field service, and dealer or fleet relationships.

records.title: Travel Trailer and Camper Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing standardized or configured travel trailers, campers, pickup coaches and caps, automobile trailers, utility trailers, light-truck trailers, and single-car transporters for external dealers, distributors, fleets, and consumers. Repeat-model product sales through dealers and distributors or direct fleet/customer orders, with model options, parts, warranty, and periodic product refreshes; end demand spans discretionary recreation and functional utility/light-trailer uses.

records.title: Drywall and Insulation Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing, replacing, and finishing drywall, ceiling tile, plaster, building insulation, mechanical insulation, and closely related firestopping for external general contractors, property owners, and facility customers on recurring or repeat outsourced engagements. Primarily project-based subcontract revenue from competitively bid or negotiated scopes, commonly fixed-price with change orders and occasional price-sharing or escalation clauses; repeat revenue comes from recurring general-contractor and owner relationships rather than subscription contracts.

records.title: Oil and Gas Pipeline and Related Structures Construction
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market external contractors repeatedly performing oil and natural-gas gathering, transmission, distribution, and service-line construction, replacement, rehabilitation, integrity digs, corrosion work, and directly related pumping, compression, metering, and storage-tank field work. Project, work-order, and master-service-agreement revenue from gas utilities, pipeline and midstream operators, producers, industrial customers, and large primes, commonly under fixed-price, unit-price, or schedule-of-rates structures. Repeat revenue arises from successive expansions, replacements, integrity programs, and maintenance capital rather than subscriptions.

records.title: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of fluid-power valves, hose fittings, couplings, and related engineered components supplied repeatedly to external OEM, distributor, repair, and industrial customers. Repeat component sales through OEM supply agreements, distributor replenishment, standing purchase orders, and replacement demand, typically priced per part, assembly, or lot rather than per labor hour.

records.title: Folding Paperboard Box Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US folding-paperboard-box manufacturers in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly design, print, cut, fold, glue, and supply cartons to external customers. Recurring business-to-business carton programs for food, beverage, pharmaceutical, personal-care, and other consumer goods, sold through quotes, purchase orders, releases, and supply agreements with charges influenced by board, printing complexity, tooling, run length, and freight.

records.title: Food Service Contractors
records.search_text: Independent firms providing recurring contracted food preparation, cafeteria, institutional dining, concession, workplace dining, healthcare dining, education dining, correctional food service, and related on-site food operations for external organizations. Business, education, healthcare, government, industrial, transportation, and venue customers buy multi-period outsourced food operations under cost-plus, operator-risk, or fixed-price arrangements; consumer sales and client subsidies can coexist within an account.

records.title: Saw Blade and Handtool Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying saw blades for hand and powered saws, nonpowered handtools, edge tools, and related professional or industrial tools to external distributors, retailers, OEMs, construction suppliers, industrial users, and private-label customers. Repeat product and replenishment sales through distributors, retailers, OEM/private-label programs, and industrial accounts, priced per tool, blade, pack, lot, or program; saw blades and edge tools have more consumable replacement than durable handtools.

records.title: Residential Intellectual and Developmental Disability Facilities
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market operators providing recurring residential care to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities through group homes, intermediate care facilities, and related staffed residential settings, with room, board, protective supervision, habilitation, counseling, and incidental health care. Residents and families are the service recipients, while Medicaid agencies and managed-care plans are major payers. Revenue is recurring and commonly paid through fixed per-diem, per-unit, tiered, bundled, or negotiated rates tied to assessed need, with separate resident funds or other payers sometimes covering room and food.

records.title: Hardwood Veneer and Plywood Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers repeatedly supplying hardwood veneer, veneer-core or composite-core hardwood plywood, and closely related prefinished panels to external customers. Repeat product sales to cabinet, furniture, fixture, architectural millwork, flooring, recreational-vehicle, manufactured-housing, distributor, and specialty industrial customers, commonly priced by panel, area, grade, species, thickness, and finish.

records.title: Direct Title Insurance Carriers
records.search_text: Privately controllable direct title insurance carriers in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that repeatedly underwrite owner or lender title policies for external real-estate customers and institutional referral channels, including their carrier-operated title examination, commitment, policy, escrow, closing, and claims workflows. A carrier generally earns a one-time premium when a property is purchased or refinanced, often through affiliated or non-affiliated title agents; related search, examination, escrow, and closing fees may be bundled or separately charged. Repeat volume comes from lenders, agents, attorneys, builders, and transaction channels rather than recurring premiums on the same policy.

records.title: Roofing, Siding, and Insulation Material Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly source, stock, quote, sell, and deliver nonwood roofing, siding, and insulation materials to contractors and other trade customers. Repeat B2B product sales to roofing and exterior contractors, builders, and dealers, with value delivered through local inventory, product knowledge, credit, order accuracy, and job-site delivery; revenue is mainly product resale margin rather than a separately billed service fee.

records.title: Prefabricated Metal Building and Component Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of pre-engineered and prefabricated metal buildings and custom building components that repeatedly design, engineer, detail, fabricate, and supply systems for external contractors, developers, dealers, and owners. Project-based fixed-price or quoted revenue for engineered building systems and components, often through repeat dealer, contractor, and developer relationships with design, detailing, fabrication, and delivery bundled.

records.title: Dimension Stone Mining and Quarrying
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market operators whose principal activity is developing quarry sites and extracting dimension stone as rough blocks or slabs, including granite, limestone, marble, sandstone, slate, quartzite, dolomite, and specialty natural stone sold for architectural, building, landscape, monument, and related uses. B2B sales of rough blocks, slabs, and irregular stone to fabricators, distributors, monument companies, landscape suppliers, architects' specified supply chains, and construction customers. Revenue is shipment- and project-driven, with economics shaped by deposit quality, permitted reserves, extraction yield, freight radius, stone aesthetics, and construction cycles.

records.title: Finish Carpentry Contractors
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market contractors performing onsite finish carpentry, including trim and molding, millwork, built-in cabinets, paneling, stairs, and prefabricated residential door, window, garage-door, cabinet, and countertop installation, where work is repeatedly outsourced by homebuilders, general contractors, remodelers, property operators, or recurring institutional customers. Primarily project-based subcontract or direct-owner revenue, commonly bid as a fixed price for a defined scope, with some time-and-materials work for uncertain conditions. Recurrence comes from repeat builder, general-contractor, remodeler, property-manager, and institutional relationships rather than subscription contracts.

records.title: Paper Bag and Coated and Treated Paper Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US manufacturers in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band repeatedly supplying paper carryout, merchandise, food, pharmacy, and multiwall industrial bags and sacks to external customers. Recurring business-to-business supply of standard or custom printed bags and sacks under quotes, purchase orders, releases, and supply agreements, with pricing driven by paper, recycled content, coatings or liners, printing, dimensions, run length, compliance, and freight.

records.title: Furniture Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market furniture merchant wholesalers that take title to furniture and repeatedly source, stock, quote, sell, coordinate delivery, and support products for external business customers. Repeat B2B product sales to furniture retailers, offices, hospitality and institutional buyers; the wholesaler earns a merchandise gross margin and may add design, installation coordination, credit, and account support.

records.title: Other Motion Picture and Video Industries
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market motion-picture film laboratories and film-preservation service firms that repeatedly provide external archives, studios, libraries, museums, government bodies, rights owners, and producers with inspection, stabilization, cleaning, repair, scanning, laboratory processing, digitization, restoration, preservation masters, access copies, and exhibition or distribution derivatives. Customers buy project-based preservation and laboratory work, collection batches, multi-phase digitization programs, restoration engagements, repeat access or exhibition derivatives, and occasional custody or managed-file services. Fees are commonly based on footage, format, condition, capture specification, labor, deliverables, storage, and special handling.

records.title: Other Millwork (including Flooring)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing outsourced, specification-driven architectural millwork production, including custom molding, trim, ornamental woodwork, stairwork, shutters, and closely related made-to-order components for external builders, dealers, architects, and contractors. Eligible firms earn repeat B2B revenue by engineering and fabricating customer- or project-specific millwork, commonly through quotes, purchase orders, builder programs, and project releases; commodity or branded flooring sales are excluded for coherence.

records.title: Other Aircraft Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers and approved suppliers of aircraft parts, subassemblies, auxiliary equipment, modification and replacement articles, and prototypes sold repeatedly to external OEMs, airlines, maintenance organizations, defense customers, or distributors. Repeat qualified B2B program, spares, replacement, and prototype-to-production revenue supported by engineering, configuration control, inspection, traceability, certification, and customer quality systems.

records.title: Jewelry and Silverware Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying jewelry, precious-metal personal goods, silverware, lapidary work, unassembled jewelry parts, findings, and related finished products to external wholesale, retail, brand, or direct customers. Manufacturers sell finished collections, custom pieces, components, findings, stones, and silverware through wholesale accounts, brands, retailers, and sometimes direct channels. Revenue is unit and order based, with repeat seasonal collections and replenishment rather than contractual subscriptions.

records.title: Overhead Traveling Crane, Hoist, and Monorail System Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of overhead traveling cranes, hoists, and monorail systems for external customers, including engineered equipment, controls and attachments, replacement parts, modernization, and manufacturer-attached inspection, repair, or preventive-maintenance support. Industrial plants, warehouses, utilities, maintenance facilities, and other material-handling users buy configured lifting equipment and modernization projects, then purchase parts, inspections, repairs, and preventive maintenance over a long installed-base life. Revenue combines project and equipment sales with repeat aftermarket work rather than subscription billing.

records.title: Paint, Varnish, and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers that take title to paints, varnishes, coatings, pigments, wallpaper, brushes, rollers, and related supplies and repeatedly supply contractors, retailers, industrial users, and other external business customers. Recurring and repeat product distribution revenue earned through quoted or contracted product prices and associated delivery, inventory availability, credit, technical product selection, color/coating support, and account service.

records.title: Power Boiler and Heat Exchanger Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying custom or repeat power boilers, heat exchangers, condensers, related components, contract fabrication, installation, repair, and service to external industrial customers. Primarily engineered-to-order or configured equipment and components sold under project or purchase-order contracts, with repeat parts, retrofit, maintenance, repair, and installation revenue for industrial, energy, defense, data-center, and process customers.

records.title: Research and Development in Nanotechnology
records.search_text: Independent US firms performing repeat outsourced nanotechnology research and experimental development for external customers, including nanoscale materials and process research, experimental design, synthesis, characterization, prototyping, data analysis, and method development. Customers are industrial, life-science, semiconductor, energy, government, and other organizations outsourcing defined R&D programs. Revenue is earned through project statements of work, fixed-fee or capped studies, milestone arrangements, fee-for-service or unit-rate work, and sometimes cost-reimbursable contracts; repeat business depends on specialized facilities, scientific credibility, confidentiality, and reproducible delivery.

records.title: Natural Gas Extraction
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operating businesses repeatedly exploring for, developing, producing, treating, measuring, and selling natural gas and field-recovered hydrocarbon liquids to external purchasers, including transferable operators working wells for others. Recurring sales by volume and energy content to marketers, pipelines, utilities, processors, industrial buyers, power generators, and LNG-linked channels, generally priced from Henry Hub or regional indexes adjusted for basis, energy content, gathering, processing, transport, hedges, and contract terms; some operators also earn contract operating fees.

records.title: Electromedical and Electrotherapeutic Apparatus Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying FDA-regulated electromedical diagnostic, monitoring, therapeutic, imaging, hearing, endoscopic, stimulation, and related apparatus or accessories to external healthcare providers, distributors, and medical-device OEM channels. Revenue comes from device and accessory sales to healthcare systems, clinics, providers, distributors, and government or group-purchasing channels. Depending on the product, the installed base can also generate consumables, software, maintenance, support, and upgrade revenue; provider contracts may include volume tiers, tenders, warranties, and service obligations.

records.title: Materials Recovery Facilities
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators of facilities that repeatedly receive, separate, sort, bale, and market recyclable materials for external municipal, hauler, commercial, or institutional customers. Revenue commonly combines contracted per-ton processing or tipping fees with commodity-sale proceeds and negotiated revenue sharing. Municipal and hauler agreements may also specify contamination, residue, audit, reporting, and performance terms; some operators may receive producer-responsibility funding.

records.title: Plastics Bag and Pouch Manufacturing
records.search_text: US LMM firms that repeatedly convert plastics resin or film into bags and pouches for external food, healthcare, personal-care, retail, institutional, industrial, and other customers, including forming, coating, plastics-to-plastics laminating, printing, and pouch converting performed within the establishment. Repeat B2B sale of specified physical packaging, typically driven by customer production volumes, purchase orders, qualification requirements, artwork and format changes, and recurring supply agreements; resin cost changes may be passed through contractually or at renewal.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Parts (Used) Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US wholesale automotive recyclers and salvage-yard operators that acquire end-of-life or total-loss vehicles, depollute and dismantle them, identify and inventory reusable parts, and repeatedly sell and deliver used collision, mechanical, body, electrical, and major components to repair shops, dealers, rebuilders, fleets, and other business customers. Repair and collision customers buy specific used parts as lower-cost alternatives to new OEM or aftermarket parts; the operator earns part margin plus residual scrap and core value and may provide search, interchange matching, condition grading, removal, warehousing, delivery, returns, warranty, and estimating-system integration.

records.title: Toy and Hobby Goods and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, hold or coordinate inventory, sell, and fulfill toys, games, hobby goods, collectibles, and related supplies for independent retailers and other business customers. Recurring and repeat B2B product sales to specialty retailers, mass merchants, e-commerce sellers, hobby shops, and institutional buyers; revenue is principally product resale margin, sometimes reinforced by assortment curation, preorder allocation, merchandising support, and fulfillment.

records.title: Unlaminated Plastics Profile Shape Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of unlaminated plastic rods, tubes, and other profile shapes that repeatedly supply external OEM, distributor, and construction customers. Repeat business-to-business product sales, commonly quoted per unit, weight, or production run under purchase orders and supply agreements; customer-specific tooling and qualification can support recurring revenue.

records.title: Refrigeration Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent and owner-controlled merchant wholesalers repeatedly supplying commercial and industrial refrigeration equipment, compressors, condensing units, cold-storage machinery, display cases, ice machines, and related parts to external contractors, foodservice, food retail, institutions, and industrial customers. Repeat transaction-based resale supported by stocked branches or warehouses, technical product selection, contractor and facility relationships, credit, fulfillment, returns, and warranty support; recurring need arises from replacement, repair parts, expansion, and regulatory equipment transitions rather than subscription contracts.

records.title: Lessors of Residential Buildings and Dwellings
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market residential owner-lessors and master lessors that repeatedly lease homes, apartments, townhomes, or similar dwellings to external residents and retain an accountable operating role in leasing, tenant service, compliance, maintenance, or vendor oversight. External residents pay recurring rent under leases for occupancy; the lessor retains rental revenue and bears or coordinates property operating, maintenance, compliance, and tenant-service obligations, sometimes through third-party managers.

records.title: Boat Dealers
records.search_text: US full-service boat dealerships in the lower-middle-market band that sell new or pre-owned boats and also provide repeat customer services such as maintenance and repair, parts support, brokerage or consignment, storage, marina services, financing, insurance, or extended-service administration. Predominantly consumer-facing boat and accessory sales, with recurring or repeat revenue from service labor, parts, storage, brokerage, finance and insurance commissions, warranties, and other ancillary services. Boat sales are transactional and cyclical; service and ownership-lifecycle revenue is the recurring component relevant to the lens.

records.title: All Other Specialty Trade Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms in NAICS 238990 that repeatedly provide outsourced specialty site and structure work to external customers, including private-area paving and sealing, fence and playground installation or repair, crane rental with operator, rigging, scaffolding and shoring, and similar physical specialty services. Project, work-order, or recurring maintenance revenue from property owners, facilities operators, general contractors, businesses, and government customers; pricing is commonly lump-sum or unit-price, with some multi-year task-order and maintenance arrangements.

records.title: Cafeterias, Grill Buffets, and Buffets
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators of cafeterias, grill buffets, and buffets that repeatedly prepare and sell meals to external patrons using cafeteria lines, buffet stations, display grills, and related self-service equipment. Mostly repeat but noncontractual sales of prepared meals to individual patrons at posted prices, with some group, institutional-location, or event-related traffic; revenue depends on visits, check size, throughput, and local competitive positioning.

records.title: Used Car Dealers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market used-car dealers with a material recurring or repeat external-customer service, such as dealer-held installment servicing, repair and warranty administration, or an integrated customer-pay service operation, alongside vehicle retail. Consumers buy used vehicles and may also purchase financing, vehicle-service contracts, warranties, or repair services; eligible dealers earn repeat revenue through loan servicing and collections or through ongoing service and warranty relationships, while vehicle gross profit remains transactional.

records.title: Sawmill, Woodworking, and Paper Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of sawmill, woodworking, pulp, paper, and paper-converting machinery that repeatedly supply external mills and factories through equipment, replacement parts, upgrades, controls, and technical support. Engineered capital-equipment and system sales supplemented by recurring replacement parts, wear components, controls upgrades, rebuilds, and support; revenue is commonly milestone- or unit-priced under quotes and purchase orders rather than hourly billing.

records.title: All Other Support Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced route-, crew-, or field-data support, including inventory taking, contract meter reading, traffic flagging, utility locating, bottle exchange, and comparable day-to-day operational support delivered under customer contracts. Utilities, retailers, manufacturers, contractors, municipalities, and other organizations outsource recurring site visits, counts, readings, route work, or staffed operating tasks. Revenue is commonly per visit, meter, location, unit, crew-hour, route, or fixed contract, with renewals, service-level requirements, scheduling, field supervision, exception handling, and reporting.

records.title: Paint and Coating Manufacturing
