records.search_text: US lower-middle-market toll and contract manufacturers of paints and coatings that repeatedly formulate, blend, test, fill, private-label, warehouse, or distribute products for external brand owners and industrial customers. Industrial customers, distributors, and brand owners purchase formulation projects and recurring batches under specifications, purchase orders, private-label programs, or supply agreements; revenue is usually product-volume based with technical service bundled into the relationship.

records.title: Sales Financing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms primarily originating and servicing collateralized credit for purchases of vehicles, equipment, inventory, and other goods, either directly to purchasers or through dealers and sellers, including separable externally facing captive-finance platforms. Sales-finance firms acquire borrowers through direct, dealer, merchant, manufacturer, or vendor channels and earn recurring interest, finance charges, fees, servicing income, and sometimes residual or ancillary economics while bearing funding, credit, fraud, collateral, and compliance costs.

records.title: Commercial Bakeries
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market commercial bakeries that manufacture fresh or frozen bread, bread-type rolls, and other fresh bakery products and repeatedly supply external retail, foodservice, institutional, wholesale, vending, or contract-manufacturing customers. Recurring wholesale product supply, generally priced by item, case, shipment, or negotiated customer program and delivered through direct-store-delivery or warehouse channels; some firms also manufacture under customer brands or foodservice specifications.

records.title: Perishable Prepared Food Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. manufacturers repeatedly producing short-shelf-life prepared meals, salads, sandwiches, fresh pizza and pasta, peeled or cut vegetables, and similar refrigerated foods for external retailers, brands, meal platforms, institutions, or foodservice customers under private-label, co-packing, contract-production, or customized supply relationships. Recurring business-to-business unit sales and manufacturing programs, often customer-specific or private label, with high-frequency ordering, strict freshness and service windows, retailer or foodservice specifications, and revenue tied to cases, units, or meals delivered.

records.title: Drugs and Druggists' Sundries Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, inventory, trace, allocate, and deliver prescription drugs, biologics, vaccines, veterinary medicines, over-the-counter products, vitamins, cosmetics, and related druggists' sundries to external pharmacies, providers, institutions, retailers, or veterinary customers. Recurring product resale spread and manufacturer economics, with service embedded in licensed sourcing, inventory availability, cold-chain or controlled handling, transaction tracing, credit, delivery, returns, shortage allocation, and customer support; most revenue is transactional under negotiated supply agreements.

records.title: Office Equipment Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US lower-middle-market office document-equipment wholesalers and dealers that repeatedly supply copying and multifunction equipment, shredders, related accessories, configuration, fleet support, workflow advice, warranty coordination, and managed-print-like service to external business and institutional customers. Repeat B2B equipment placements and refreshes, often bundled or adjacent to metered, maintenance, fleet-management, document-workflow, and support relationships; product gross margin and recurring service economics pay for sales, configuration, field coordination, supplies fulfillment, and customer support.

records.title: Aluminum Foundries (except Die-Casting)
records.search_text: Independent US aluminum foundries outside die casting, in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band, repeatedly producing customer-designed sand, permanent-mold, semi-permanent-mold, investment, or related castings for external customers. Recurring engineered business-to-business casting programs priced per part or lot with separate pattern, tooling, engineering, finishing, testing, and metal-surcharge economics under quotes, releases, purchase orders, and supply agreements.

records.title: Charter Bus Industry
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market charter-bus and motorcoach operators providing repeat outsourced group transportation to schools, colleges, sports teams, tour operators, event planners, corporations, associations, hotels, and other external customers. Reserved group trips priced by itinerary, vehicle, hour, day, mileage, or combinations of those units, with repeat business arising from institutional accounts, tour and event intermediaries, teams, schools, and corporate clients rather than scheduled-route passenger fares.

records.title: Tire Retreading
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market tire retreaders and rebuilders that repeatedly inspect customer or program casings, apply new tread, test and identify finished retreads, and supply external trucking fleets, bus operators, dealers, off-road users, and tire-management programs. Recurring B2B service and product revenue per retread, casing, or tire-management program, often tied to fleet routes and replacement cycles, with pickup and delivery, casing acceptance, tread choice, warranty, and volume pricing.

records.title: Lessors of Other Real Estate Property
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market owner-operators that lease manufactured-home sites to residents who own their homes and receive recurring community access, grounds, utility, compliance, and resident-service operations. Residents typically pay recurring site rent under monthly or annual arrangements, sometimes with separately billed utilities, amenities, storage, home-sale, or other community services.

records.title: Other Gambling Industries
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market licensed distributed-gaming concession operators that own or control coin-, card-, video-, or slot-gambling devices and repeatedly place, monitor, maintain, repair, and support them in external bars, restaurants, convenience stores, truck stops, fraternal or veterans organizations, and similar host locations. External licensed host locations receive recurring equipment placement, uptime, compliance, cash-control, reporting, and field-service support. The operator commonly earns a regulated or contracted share of net terminal income under a multi-year location or use agreement rather than billing technicians by the hour.

records.title: Promoters of Performing Arts, Sports, and Similar Events without Facilities
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly organize, promote, book, produce, or manage live performing-arts, sports, concert, festival, fair, and similar events in third-party facilities for artists, rights holders, sponsors, venue partners, and event audiences. Promoters contract with artists, booking agents, rights holders, venue operators, sponsors, and production vendors. Revenue is commonly ticket-led, with possible service charges, sponsorship, concessions or merchandise participation, camping and other event fees; costs include artist guarantees or profit shares, venue rental, marketing, and production services.

records.title: Air and Gas Compressor Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of general-purpose reciprocating and centrifugal air or gas compressors, non-laboratory vacuum pumps, and nonagricultural spraying and dusting compressor or spray-gun units that repeatedly supply external industrial, commercial, distributor, OEM, and aftermarket customers. Repeat business-to-business equipment, configured-system, replacement, spare-parts, and aftermarket sales through direct, OEM, distributor, and service channels, commonly priced by unit, configuration, project, or supply agreement.

records.title: Fluid Power Cylinder and Actuator Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders and actuators, including engineered-to-order units, repeat OEM production, replacements, remanufacture, and manufacturer-provided repair or rebuild work. Revenue is primarily business-to-business: repeat OEM production orders, configured or custom units quoted by specification, replacement cylinders, and episodic but recurring repair, rebuild, and remanufacture work for industrial and mobile-equipment users.

records.title: Printed Circuit Assembly (Electronic Assembly) Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. firms in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly provide printed circuit board loading, electronic assembly, module production, testing, and closely related engineering or supply-chain services to external OEM customers. Customers outsource build-to-print or configured-to-order PCB assemblies and modules through purchase orders, forecasts, and product-lifecycle programs. Revenue is generally per assembly or production lot and can include engineering, test development, procurement, fulfillment, and aftermarket services; material costs are often contractually or commercially passed through.

records.title: Mining Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of underground mining machinery, stationary mineral-processing and beneficiating machinery, and related repeat equipment or parts programs supplied to external mining customers. Project and repeat B2B sales of engineered machines, modules, replacement units, and manufacturer-supplied parts to mines, mineral processors, contractors, and equipment integrators; revenue is usually quoted per machine, system, or order, with recurring demand tied to wear, capacity, mine plans, and installed equipment.

records.title: Laminated Plastics Plate, Sheet (except Packaging), and Shape Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying laminated plastic plate, nonpackaging sheet, rod, and other shapes made by bonding or resin-impregnating profiles and compressing them under heat. Repeat B2B sales of standard and customer-specified laminated stock under purchase orders, blanket agreements, distributor programs, and qualified supply relationships, priced by sheet, unit, area, weight, or production run.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Ambulatory Health Care Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing outsourced health screening, employee testing, remote physiologic or cardiac monitoring support, and medical case or care-management services to employers, payers, health systems, and clinician practices. Customers pay per screen or test, per monitored patient or device period, per case, per member per month, time-based service fees, fixed program fees, or payer fee-schedule amounts; providers combine technology, clinical or technical staff, records, escalation, and customer reporting.

records.title: Used Household and Office Goods Moving
records.search_text: Independent operating companies that use their own crews and vehicles to provide local or long-distance household, institutional, and office moves, including packing, loading, transport, unloading, and directly related storage or accessorial services. Consumers, employers, institutions, and offices buy project-based moves under written binding or non-binding estimates, with revenue from transportation, labor, packing, storage, and accessorial services; repeat demand is episodic rather than subscription-based.

records.title: Plastics Packaging Film and Sheet (including Laminated) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers converting plastics resins into flexible packaging film and packaging sheet for recurring or repeat external customers, including repeat converter-film, food, consumer, stretch, and shrink-film programs. Recurring and repeat B2B sales of specified film or sheet, typically by roll, weight, or production order, to converters, packagers, and consumer-product supply chains; price resets and resin-index or other contractual mechanisms can transmit input-cost changes.

records.title: Wine and Distilled Alcoholic Beverage Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers buying wine or distilled alcoholic beverages for resale and repeatedly providing portfolio sales, inventory holding, warehousing, delivery, merchandising, invoicing, supplier reporting, and alcohol-compliance services to external retailers and hospitality accounts. Recurring B2B product resale to licensed retail and on-premise accounts, with gross profit primarily from the acquisition-to-resale spread and sometimes supplier programs; portfolio rights, account relationships, case volumes, delivery density, merchandising, and compliance shape economics.

records.title: Dental Equipment and Supplies Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of dental chairs, delivery systems, hand instruments, laboratory and office equipment, impression materials, cements, restorative and preventive supplies, and related manufacturer-attributable parts, repair, training, and service. Repeat outsourced revenue from dental practices, dental service organizations, laboratories, schools, distributors, and OEMs through equipment programs, consumable replenishment, replacement instruments and parts, repair, maintenance, training, and warranties.

records.title: Drilling Oil and Gas Wells
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors repeatedly drilling oil and gas wells for external exploration and production customers on a contract or fee basis, including spudding, drilling, redrilling, and directional drilling performed with contractor-operated rigs and crews. Recurring outsourced rig-and-crew service sold primarily through daywork/dayrate, well-based, or performance-based contracts, with mobilization and lower rates for some non-operating periods; activity and pricing track customer drilling budgets and rig supply.

records.title: Clay Building Material and Refractories Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing and supplying clay and nonclay refractories, refractory cement and mortar, ceramic and structural clay tile, brick, roofing and paving clay products, flue linings, and related fired structural-clay materials to external industrial, construction, distribution, and government customers. Repeat industrial supply and relining orders for consumable refractories, plus distributor, builder, architect-specified, and project orders for brick, tile, and other building materials; revenue is usually per ton, unit, pallet, lot, or project.

records.title: Speed Changer, Industrial High-Speed Drive, and Gear Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of gears, speed changers, and non-hydrostatic industrial high-speed drives that repeatedly supply external OEM, distributor, maintenance, repair, and industrial customers. Repeat business-to-business sales of catalog, configured, engineered, replacement, and spare gearing and drives, priced by unit, drawing, program, or supply agreement, sometimes with engineering and aftermarket support bundled into the product relationship.

records.title: All Other Support Activities for Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring outsourced transportation-support operations, including transport coordination and scheduling, terminal or stockyard support, and other repeat services where an accountable operator serves external customers. Business and institutional customers buy recurring, usage-based, per-transaction, or contracted support from a third-party operator. Revenue commonly follows service volume, facility use, labor, or a negotiated fixed service fee.

records.title: All Other Rubber Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying industrial rubber tubing, reclaimed or ground-rubber inputs and products, rubber foam, mats, flooring, bumpers, pads, and similar non-tire, non-hose industrial or facility products to external business and government customers. Repeat purchase orders, distributor/catalog sales, private-label programs, and project or supply contracts for industrial inputs and durable rubber products, with revenue primarily per unit, pound, sheet, or order.

records.title: Hazardous Waste Collection
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly collect or haul hazardous waste for external customers, prepare or package it for transport, or operate hazardous-waste transfer stations. Industrial, automotive, healthcare, institutional, commercial, and public-sector generators buy scheduled pickups, containerized-waste programs, transfer services, and occasional project or emergency work; charges commonly reflect waste volume, materials, labor, transportation, and other fees under service agreements or purchase orders.

records.title: Beer and Ale Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers buying beer and ale for resale and repeatedly providing account sales, inventory holding, warehousing, route planning, delivery, merchandising, invoicing, and compliance to external retail and hospitality customers. Recurring B2B product resale to licensed retailers and hospitality accounts; gross profit is principally the spread between acquisition cost and retailer invoice price, with routes, service frequency, territory and supplier-brand rights shaping economics.

records.title: Packaging Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of wrapping, bottling, canning, labeling, filling, bagging, case-packing, palletizing, inspection, and other packaging machinery that provide recurring or repeat outsourced service through installation, maintenance, spare parts, upgrades, remote support, monitoring, software, training, performance agreements, or lifecycle optimization. Manufacturers sell standard and custom machines or complete lines to food, beverage, pharmaceutical, personal-care, chemical, consumer-product, and industrial producers. Eligible firms monetize the installed base through parts, field service, preventive and predictive maintenance, upgrades, training, digital monitoring, remote support, software, and multi-period performance or lifecycle agreements.

records.title: Meat Processed from Carcasses
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market establishments that repeatedly process purchased red meat for external customers through private-label, case-ready, co-packing, custom-formulation, cutting, curing, smoking, grinding, cooking, freezing, or related contract programs. Recurring B2B production programs for retailers, foodservice distributors, institutions, and food brands, typically invoiced per pound, case, SKU, or production run under specifications that incorporate raw-material, packaging, conversion, and logistics economics.

records.title: Mechanical Power Transmission Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly supply external customers with non-automotive, non-aircraft mechanical power-transmission equipment such as plain bearings, clutches, couplings, joints, and drive chains. Repeat business-to-business sales of catalog, configured, drawing-specific, replacement, and spare power-transmission components to OEMs, distributors, MRO buyers, and industrial end users, commonly priced by unit or supply agreement.

records.title: Sports and Recreation Instruction
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market schools, academies, and instruction camps that repeatedly provide paid athletic instruction to external customers, including swimming, gymnastics, martial arts, cheerleading, riding, golf, tennis, and team-sport skills. Primarily household-paid recurring class tuition, memberships, lesson packages, seasonal programs, and repeat camps, with some school, club, or municipal contracts; facilities and equipment may be owned or leased and instructor labor is central to delivery.

records.title: Concrete Block and Brick Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of concrete block, concrete brick, concrete pavers, and related masonry units sold repeatedly to external distributors, contractors, dealers, and projects, including production, quality, sales, order management, logistics, and administration. Business-to-business product sales by unit, pallet, or project order, generally through local or regional distribution because product weight makes freight consequential; repeat revenue follows construction activity and contractor or dealer relationships.

records.title: Automotive Oil Change and Lubrication Shops
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market quick-lube and preventive-maintenance operators whose primary work is motor-oil changes and chassis lubrication for passenger vehicles, vans, and trucks, including closely associated filter, fluid, battery, bulb, and wiper services delivered by stand-alone company-owned or franchised operating businesses. Retail drivers and commercial fleets pay per completed preventive-maintenance service, often in a drive-through or stay-in-vehicle format. Revenue combines a core oil-change ticket with recommended filters, fluids, wipers, batteries, and checks; throughput depends on bay flow, technician teams, inventory availability, trust, and repeat maintenance intervals.

records.title: Custom Compounding of Purchased Resins
records.search_text: Independent LMM businesses that repeatedly blend, mix, modify, pelletize, or add additives to purchased plastic resins for external customers, including custom formulations and recycled-content compounds. Repeat B2B toll or product sales of customer-qualified compounds and masterbatches, supported by formulation, testing, color or performance matching, quality documentation, and supply coordination.

records.title: Home Furnishing Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, stock, quote, sell, and coordinate delivery of home furnishings and housewares such as floor coverings, lamps, linens, tableware, mirrors, curtains, shades, and related products for external customers. Repeat merchandise sales to retailers, designers, hospitality operators, installers, contractors, and other business buyers; the wholesaler takes product risk and earns a resale gross margin, sometimes adding assortment, samples, credit, fulfillment, and account support.

records.title: Junior Colleges
records.search_text: Privately controlled two-year colleges that provide recurring academic or academic-technical instruction to external students, award associate degrees or equivalent credentials, and operate in the approximately $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. Students purchase term- or program-based education, commonly funded through a mix of tuition, federal or state student aid, employer support, and private payment; enrollment recurs across terms until completion or withdrawal.

records.title: Search, Detection, Navigation, Guidance, Aeronautical, and Nautical System and Instrument Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying qualified search, detection, radar, sonar, navigation, guidance, flight-instrument, mission-computer, avionics, and related sensing or control systems to external defense, aerospace, marine, government, and commercial OEM customers. Revenue arises from product deliveries, production releases, development and engineering contracts, upgrades, spares, repair, overhaul, service, and lifecycle support. Programs are often platform-specific, qualification-heavy, and sold to prime contractors, government agencies, aircraft or vessel OEMs, operators, and allied customers under fixed-price, cost-type, commercial, or negotiated purchase arrangements.

records.title: Poultry and Poultry Product Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, take title to, hold, sell, and distribute live poultry, fresh or chilled poultry, eggs, and other poultry products other than canned or packaged-frozen products to external business customers. Recurring and repeat business-to-business product sales with a wholesale gross margin compensating the distributor for sourcing, inventory risk, handling, temperature control where applicable, delivery, trade credit, account service, availability, and rapid response to perishable-product exceptions.

records.title: Commercial Screen Printing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market screen printers repeatedly serving businesses, institutions, brands, distributors, teams, schools, events, and promotional-product customers on apparel, textiles, labels, signs, stationery, invitations, components, and other purchased substrates. Job-order and recurring-program revenue priced per design, setup, color, item, run, or fulfillment program, often including artwork, screen preparation, printing, curing, finishing, packing, online storefront, and distribution services.

records.title: Ophthalmic Goods Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of prescription eyeglasses outside retail settings, ophthalmic lenses, contact lenses, frames, sunglasses, standard-power reading glasses, and protective eyewear, including manufacturer-attributable finishing, coating, parts, warranty, and service revenue. Repeat outsourced revenue from optical retailers, optometrists, ophthalmologists, laboratories, distributors, employers, safety programs, government buyers, and branded-product customers through prescription replenishment, lens and frame programs, coatings, contact-lens replacement, protective eyewear, and warranties.

records.title: Consumer Lending
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operating lenders primarily making unsecured cash loans to external consumers, including personal, student, small-dollar, and other repeat consumer loan products, with transferable underwriting, funding, compliance, servicing oversight, and customer-acquisition operations. Operators earn interest spread, origination and account fees where permitted, late or ancillary fees, and sometimes gains on loan sales; revenue depends on funded balances or originations, credit losses, funding cost, regulation, repeat borrowing, and customer acquisition.

records.title: Service Establishment Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market custodial and janitorial equipment and supplies merchant wholesalers serving external commercial cleaners, facility managers, institutions, and businesses through repeat consumables, scheduled replenishment, route delivery, equipment, technical support, and related inventory services. Product gross margin on recurring sales of cleaning tools, janitorial equipment, paper and hygiene items, liners, and related supplies, often using account-specific pricing, scheduled or route delivery, replenishment, equipment support, and occasional service fees.

records.title: Frozen Specialty Food Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of frozen dinners, entrees, side dishes, pizza, whipped topping, waffles, pancakes, French toast, and similar specialty foods for repeat retail, foodservice, institutional, private-label, co-manufacturing, and distributor customers. Repeat finished-product and co-manufacturing sales priced per unit, case, shipment, or production run through branded, private-label, foodservice, institutional, and distributor programs.

records.title: Trust, Fiduciary, and Custody Activities
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing trust administration, fiduciary, custody, safekeeping, securities settlement and reporting, or non-real-estate escrow services to external customers on a fee or contract basis, including independent trust companies and outsourced specialist administrators. Individuals, families, institutions, asset owners, financial intermediaries, and transaction parties pay recurring asset-based, account, custody, administration, safekeeping, settlement, reporting, or contract fees; non-real-estate escrow may also produce transaction-specific fees. Contracts, governing instruments, asset levels, service complexity, and regulation shape pricing and service obligations.

records.title: Other Building Finishing Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors providing repeat or recurring outsourced building-finishing work to external customers, especially commercial waterproofing, concrete coating or sealing, modular furniture and exhibit installation, and recurring shade, blind, fixture, and space-reconfiguration installation. Project and work-order revenue from property owners, facilities managers, general contractors, dealers, exhibitors, and commercial tenants; eligible firms earn repeat demand through maintenance, renovation cycles, multi-site programs, reconfigurations, or recurring events, commonly under competitively bid fixed-price or unit-price scopes.

records.title: New Multifamily Housing Construction (except For-Sale Builders)
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. general contractors, design-build firms, and construction-management firms building new multifamily apartments and condominiums for external owners and developers, including high-rise, garden, town-house, low-income, and panelized projects. Project-based outsourced construction for developers, investors, housing authorities, and institutional owners, commonly delivered under guaranteed-maximum-price, lump-sum, design-build, or construction-management arrangements; repeatability comes from a continuing pipeline of projects and repeat developer relationships rather than subscription revenue.

records.title: Nonupholstered Wood Household Furniture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US manufacturers of stock or custom nonupholstered wood household furniture and freestanding cabinets in the lower-middle-market band that provide recurring or repeat outsourced production, private-label, retailer, dealer, designer, hospitality, replacement, or support work to external customers. Customers include retailers, dealers, designers, private-label brands, hospitality buyers, distributors, and households. Revenue comes from stock collections or custom orders and can include repeat replenishment, private-label programs, finish or size options, replacement parts, warranty work, and after-sale support.

records.title: Bus and Other Motor Vehicle Transit Systems
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market private firms that repeatedly operate local or suburban fixed-route bus or other motor-vehicle transit service for external public authorities, institutions, or system owners under operating or purchased-transportation contracts. The core customer is a public transit authority or other system owner paying recurring fees under a multiyear competitively procured operating contract, with fixed-price, indexed, cost-reimbursement, or management-fee structures; the authority generally controls fares, routes, service levels, and subsidy.

records.title: Aircraft Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers and prime integrators of complete light, utility, agricultural, training, unmanned, special-mission, and niche general-aviation aircraft with repeat production, certification, customer delivery, and product-support revenue. Manufacturers earn milestone, progress, deposit, or delivery-based aircraft revenue under customer orders and government or commercial contracts, supplemented by options, spares, training, warranty, field support, and service. Certification and product configuration create long lead times; demand is lumpy by unit even where order books and installed-base support recur.

records.title: Solid Waste Landfill
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating landfills for nonhazardous solid waste, including firms that combine local collection or hauling with their own landfill operations and repeatedly serve external municipal, commercial, industrial, and construction customers. Municipalities, haulers, businesses, institutions, and contractors pay tipping or disposal fees commonly based on tons, loads, waste type, and contract terms; integrated operators may also earn recurring collection fees, environmental surcharges, and revenue from landfill-gas electricity or renewable natural gas.

records.title: Other Nonferrous Metal Foundries (except Die-Casting)
records.search_text: Independent US copper, brass, bronze, zinc, magnesium, titanium, and other nonaluminum nonferrous foundries outside die casting, in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band, repeatedly producing customer-designed castings for external customers. Recurring engineered business-to-business casting programs priced per part or lot with pattern, tooling, alloy, testing, finishing, and metal-surcharge economics under quotes, releases, purchase orders, and supply agreements.

records.title: Other Support Activities for Road Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing repeat outsourced street sweeping, road-transport inspection or weighing, truck loading and unloading, shared terminal or maintenance-facility services, and contracted toll-road, bridge, tunnel, or related road-network operations to external customers. Customers include municipalities and road agencies, commercial fleets and carriers, terminal users, and infrastructure owners. Revenue ranges from multi-year fixed-price or unit-price service contracts and scheduled recurring work to usage fees and availability-payment or concession arrangements.

records.title: Ship Building and Repairing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market shipyards with fixed facilities and fabrication equipment that build, repair, convert, alter, dismantle, or scale commercial, government, and other nonrecreational ships, barges, workboats, and floating platforms for external customers. Recurring or repeat outsourced revenue from shipowners and government agencies through maintenance availabilities, repair, overhaul, conversion, modernization, emergency work, and repeat new-build programs.

records.title: Metal Window and Door Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market metal window, door, frame, screen, trim, and curtain-wall manufacturers that repeatedly configure, engineer, fabricate, glaze, assemble, finish, and supply custom products to external dealers, contractors, architects, and building owners. Quoted per-unit, package, or project revenue through dealer, contractor, and specification channels, with configuration, engineering, fabrication, testing documentation, warranties, and delivery often bundled.

records.title: Textile and Fabric Finishing Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing dyeing, bleaching, printing, washing, preshrinking, mercerizing, napping, scouring, cleaning, and other textile or fabric finishing to external B2B customers, including converters with durable repeat finishing programs. Mostly per-yard, per-pound, per-lot, per-garment, or program-based job finishing for fabric mills, apparel manufacturers, brands, and industrial-textile customers; converter revenue may include a wholesale spread on purchased grey fabric. Specifications, approved recipes, color matching, compliance, and turnaround create repeat relationships but limited contractual subscription revenue.

records.title: Pet Care (except Veterinary) Services
records.search_text: U.S. multi-employee, facility-based pet boarding, daycare, and grooming operators, including businesses combining those services with supervised exercise, feeding, sanitation, monitoring, and non-veterinary ancillary care. Consumer-paid boarding nights, daycare visits or packages, grooming appointments, memberships, and ancillary services delivered from staffed facilities, with repeat use and local customer relationships but generally limited contractual commitment.

records.title: Polish and Other Sanitation Good Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contract, toll, and private-label manufacturers of sanitation and specialty cleaning goods, including disinfectants, sanitizers, polishes, waxes, and institutional or industrial cleaners, that repeatedly formulate, blend, fill, package, document, or fulfill products for external brands and customers. Customers are brands, retailers, distributors, and institutional or industrial product marketers. Revenue recurs through production runs and replenishment orders and is commonly quoted per batch, unit, or packaged program, with formulation, testing, regulatory support, warehousing, and fulfillment bundled or separately charged.

records.title: Railroad Rolling Stock Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers and rebuilders of locomotives, freight and passenger cars, rapid-transit equipment, railcar parts and equipment, and track-maintenance machinery sold repeatedly to external railroads, transit agencies, lessors, fleets, or OEMs. Repeat new-build, rebuild, component, replacement, and fleet-program revenue under bids and long-term contracts, supported by engineering, safety compliance, inspection, documentation, warranty, and parts service.

records.title: Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying vitreous-china plumbing fixtures and accessories, porcelain electrical insulators and parts, ceramic or ferrite magnets, chemical stoneware, and other specification-based ceramic goods to external distributors, builders, OEMs, utilities, and industrial customers. Repeat distributor and OEM purchase orders, builder/project bids, utility and industrial specifications, and catalog sales, generally priced per fixture, piece, lot, or project.

records.title: Tortilla Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market US tortilla manufacturers that repeatedly supply external foodservice, industrial, retail private-label, distributor, or customer-specific accounts with corn or flour tortillas and related flatbreads classified in NAICS 311830. Recurring per-case, per-pound, or per-shipment sales to foodservice operators, retailers, distributors, and industrial customers, often with customer specifications, route or distributor delivery, periodic price resets, and competitive account retention.

records.title: Ball and Roller Bearing Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of ball, roller, mounted, precision, and specialty bearings supplied repeatedly to external OEM, distributor, maintenance, aerospace, energy, transportation, and industrial customers. Repeat component and replacement sales through OEM programs, qualified supply agreements, distributor replenishment, maintenance demand, and standing purchase orders, generally priced per bearing or lot.

records.title: Inland Water Freight Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced cargo transportation on rivers, lakes, and intracoastal waterways outside the Great Lakes System, including crewed barge and towing operations that move bulk liquids, grain, coal, aggregates, chemicals, petroleum products, and other freight. Industrial, agricultural, energy, construction, and trading customers buy contracts of affreightment, voyage movements, or time-charter capacity under annual or multi-year term contracts and spot agreements; operators provide towboats, barges, qualified crews, dispatch, maintenance, regulatory compliance, and accountable delivery through the inland waterway network.

records.title: Automobile and Other Motor Vehicle Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, take title to, market, and distribute new or used passenger vehicles, trucks, trailers, motorcycles, recreational vehicles, and similar motor vehicles to dealers, fleets, and other business buyers. Business customers buy vehicles for resale or fleet use; the wholesaler earns an inventory spread or gross margin and may bundle inspection, reconditioning coordination, title administration, financing support, and transport coordination into repeat account relationships.

records.title: Other Services to Buildings and Dwellings
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing outsourced physical services to buildings and dwellings, including swimming-pool cleaning and maintenance, chimney cleaning and inspection, ventilation-duct cleaning, drain or gutter cleaning, pressure washing and other building-exterior cleaning, commercial kitchen exhaust cleaning, parking-lot cleaning, lighting maintenance, and stand-alone driveway or parking-lot snow removal. External homeowners, property managers, associations, businesses, institutions, and public facilities buy weekly or monthly routes, seasonal programs, annual inspections, periodic maintenance, and one-time cleaning or corrective projects. Billing may be flat monthly, per visit, per asset, hourly, or fixed by quoted scope, with chemical and repair materials either bundled or separately charged.

records.title: Satellite Telecommunications
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that provide recurring or repeat satellite signal forwarding, reception, capacity, airtime, or resale services to external telecommunications and broadcasting customers. Wholesale and enterprise customers buy recurring access, capacity, airtime, managed connectivity, and usage-based transmission services, sometimes alongside equipment or engineering work.

records.title: Blind and Shade Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying stock or custom venetian and other blinds, roller, cellular, Roman and other shades, curtain and drapery rods and poles, fixtures, and motorized window coverings to external dealers, designers, retailers, builders, commercial customers, and direct consumers. Made-to-measure dealer and designer orders, builder and commercial projects, private-label or retail programs, e-commerce direct sales, and replacement purchases, generally priced per opening, unit, room package, or project.

records.title: Other Animal Food Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent custom, toll, contract, and private-label manufacturers of livestock, poultry, equine, aquaculture, and other non-dog/cat feeds, premixes, concentrates, and supplements that repeatedly produce for external customers at roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA. Recurring B2B production priced per ton, batch, formula, package, or supply program for farmers, livestock operators, dealers, and animal-nutrition brands; services may include formulation, blending, pelleting, packaging, testing, traceability, and delivery.

records.title: Other Commercial Equipment Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent commercial-equipment distributors in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that repeatedly serve external restaurant, food-retail, institutional, or other commercial customers with equipment specification, project coordination, installation, preventive maintenance, repair, parts, or related accountable service. Customers purchase commercial machines, fixtures, shelving, scales, and related equipment at product margins, then buy repeat project, installation, maintenance, repair, parts, or annual-service work through contracts and work orders.

records.title: Plastics Bottle Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers producing plastic bottles for external food, beverage, household, personal-care, pharmaceutical, and industrial customers through recurring or repeat supply relationships. Predominantly repeat B2B sales under customer specifications, purchase orders, supply agreements, and negotiated unit pricing; revenue is volume multiplied by per-unit bottle prices, often with resin-cost adjustment mechanisms.

records.title: General Line Grocery Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US general-line grocery merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, take title to, warehouse, sell, finance, and deliver a wide grocery assortment to independent retailers, convenience stores, institutions, and other business customers, with normalized EBITDA of roughly $1-10 million. High-frequency B2B product sales earn a markup or fee over vendor cost while the distributor supplies aggregation, assortment, procurement, inventory availability, trade credit, ordering, compliant storage and transport, delivery, and recall or exception response.

records.title: Coffee and Tea Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market coffee roasters and tea blenders that repeatedly provide contract roasting, blending, grinding, private-label manufacturing, co-packing, custom packaging, and related fulfillment to external retailers, cafés, restaurants, hospitality programs, distributors, and consumer brands. Repeat B2B orders under wholesale, private-label, toll-roasting, contract-manufacturing, and co-packing arrangements, typically priced per pound, case, capsule, package, or production run, sometimes bundled with sourcing, formulation, certification, packaging, and fulfillment.

records.title: Spice and Extract Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. firms repeatedly manufacturing custom or private-label spices, table salt, seasonings, non-coffee and non-meat flavoring extracts, natural food colorings, and qualifying dry food mixes for external food manufacturers, foodservice operators, distributors, or retailers. Recurring business-to-business ingredient and finished-product sales, commonly based on customer formulas, specifications, private labels, or application requirements, with pricing and quantities set through purchase orders, master agreements, or frequently renewed annual arrangements.

records.title: Poured Concrete Foundation and Structure Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors pouring and finishing concrete foundations and structural elements for external residential, commercial, industrial, and public-project customers. Project-based subcontracting for owners and general contractors, commonly bid as fixed-price, lump-sum, or unit-price scopes with progress billing and change orders; repeat business comes from contractor and developer relationships rather than subscription revenue.

records.title: Other Metal Valve and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of industrial metal valves, flanges, unions, couplings, and pipe fittings supplied repeatedly to external process-industry, energy, water, infrastructure, OEM, distributor, and maintenance customers. Repeat product and replacement sales under OEM and distributor programs, project specifications, approved-vendor relationships, maintenance demand, and standing purchase orders, usually priced per part or lot.

records.title: Temporary Help Services
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market temporary-help and staffing firms that repeatedly recruit, employ, administer, and supply workers to external clients for limited assignments across industrial, clerical, professional, technical, education, and health-care categories. Clients buy contingent labor capacity, usually billed per worker-hour or per unit. The staffing firm pays assigned employees, payroll taxes, benefits where applicable, and workers' compensation, retaining a spread that funds recruiting, branch operations, compliance, and profit; some providers also earn managed-service or outcome-based fees.

records.title: Upholstered Household Furniture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US stock or custom upholstered household-furniture manufacturers in the lower-middle-market band that provide recurring or repeat outsourced production, private-label, retailer, designer, dealer, hospitality, warranty, or replenishment work to external customers. Customers include furniture retailers, dealers, designers, private-label brands, hospitality buyers, distributors, and households. Revenue comes from stock collections or custom orders and can include repeat retailer replenishment, private-label programs, options and fabrics, warranty replacement, and after-sale support.

records.title: All Other Converted Paper Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market converters supplying repeat business-to-business runs of molded-pulp egg cartons, food trays and containers, industrial or protective paper components, and other specification-driven die-cut or converted paper products not classified elsewhere. Repeat unit sales and production runs to food producers, distributors, industrial customers, packers, and private-label buyers under purchase orders, supply agreements, or recurring specifications.

records.title: Custom Roll Forming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market custom roll formers that repeatedly engineer, tool, and produce metal profiles for external construction, transportation, appliance, storage, energy, industrial, and component customers. Recurring production runs and release orders priced per part, foot, pound, run, or program, often with separate or amortized engineering and tooling charges and customer-owned specifications.

records.title: All Other Traveler Accommodation
records.search_text: Employer-operated cabins, guest houses, villas, cottage resorts, hostels, and similar short-term traveler accommodations in the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly sell lodging and associated operating services to external guests. Revenue is predominantly transactional room or unit accommodation sold per stay to household travelers, with some business and government customers and ancillary food, recreation, laundry, parking, and space-rental revenue. Repeat demand is recurring at the property and destination level even when individual guests are episodic.

records.title: Specialty (except Psychiatric and Substance Abuse) Hospitals
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating licensed specialty hospitals that repeatedly provide inpatient and related outpatient diagnostic, medical, long-term acute, rehabilitation, restorative, or condition-specific care to patients and payers. Hospitals receive prospective case or discharge payments, negotiated commercial payments, public-program reimbursement, and patient cost sharing for staffed episodes of inpatient care, often with related outpatient, diagnostic, therapy, and assessment services.

records.title: Hazardous Waste Treatment and Disposal
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that operate hazardous-waste treatment or disposal facilities, including firms that combine local hazardous-waste collection or hauling with their own treatment or disposal facilities, and repeatedly serve external generators under permits and service agreements. Industrial, commercial, healthcare, laboratory, and government generators purchase waste profiling, manifest administration, transportation where integrated, treatment, incineration, stabilization, storage, and disposal, commonly through recurring accounts, scheduled pickups, unit-priced waste streams, and competitively bid term contracts.

records.title: Lessors of Miniwarehouses and Self-Storage Units
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market owner-operators that repeatedly rent secure rooms, compartments, containers, lockers, or outdoor space for customers to store and retrieve their own goods. Households and businesses usually pay recurring unit rent, often month to month, with ancillary administrative fees, merchandise, and tenant-protection or insurance revenue.

records.title: Coin-Operated Laundries and Drycleaners
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators of coin-, card-, or similarly operated self-service laundry and drycleaning facilities for external customers, plus firms that supply and service self-service laundry equipment in apartments, dormitories, and other third-party premises. Ancillary wash-dry-fold, pickup, delivery, and vending are included when attached to an eligible self-service operation. Walk-in customers repeatedly buy washer and dryer cycles at posted vend prices, often supplemented by vending and attended services. Route operators receive recurring cycle revenue or contractual revenue-share from equipment placed in apartments, dormitories, or other third-party properties.

records.title: Flooring Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing, replacing, sanding, finishing, and refinishing resilient flooring, carpet, linoleum, and hardwood for property owners, general contractors, retailers, property managers, and other external customers, including residential and commercial new work, alterations, maintenance, and repair. Project revenue is earned through competitively quoted lump-sum or unit-price installation, replacement, repair, and refinishing work. Individual projects are episodic, but the service recurs through flooring wear, tenant turnover, remodeling, new construction, and repeat relationships with general contractors, retailers, property managers, and owners.

records.title: Plastics Material and Resin Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market producers that repeatedly manufacture, polymerize, mix, or blend resins and plastics materials on a custom, toll, or customer-specification basis for external customers. Industrial customers buy recurring custom resin campaigns or volumes under formulation and performance specifications, with revenue based on conversion fees, per-pound pricing, or formulas that may adjust for monomer, feedstock, and energy costs.

records.title: Ice Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. manufacturers that repeatedly produce and supply packaged ice to external retail, foodservice, airline, event, industrial, or emergency-service customers, including direct-store delivery and co-packing relationships. Recurring product sales coupled with replenishment, route delivery, merchandising, emergency supply, or co-packing; larger customers commonly procure by negotiated bid and value reliable stocking and on-time delivery.

records.title: Elevator and Moving Stairway Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of passenger and freight elevators, escalators, moving walkways, automobile lifts, and separately sold components supplied repeatedly to external building owners, contractors, integrators, distributors, and modernization projects. Project and business-to-business equipment and component sales through specifications, contractors, integrators, direct accounts, and modernization programs; repeat revenue follows parts, upgrades, safety changes, accessibility work, and installed-base renewal.

records.title: Direct Property and Casualty Insurance Carriers
records.search_text: Privately controllable, risk-bearing direct property and casualty insurers in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that underwrite and renew policies for external personal or commercial policyholders and operate policy administration, billing, regulatory, and claims workflows. Customers or their brokers pay recurring premiums for risk transfer and related underwriting, policy-administration, and claims services. Economics combine earned premiums, losses and loss-adjustment expense, commissions, operating expense, reinsurance, and investment income; personal and commercial lines differ in complexity and distribution but share the same risk-bearing carrier model.

records.title: Other Airport Operations
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring outsourced airport and fixed-base operational services, including ramp and turnaround coordination, baggage handling, ground direction, aircraft service support, de-icing, runway clearing, and contract airport administration. Airlines, airport authorities, aircraft operators, and tenants purchase contracted services through negotiated ground-handling agreements, per-turn or activity charges, recurring FBO service relationships, and airport-management contracts. Revenue generally combines volume-linked service fees with contractual operating and quality obligations.

records.title: Structural Steel and Precast Concrete Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors that erect and assemble structural steel or precast concrete and install reinforcing steel for external owners, general contractors, fabricators, and precast producers, including new work, additions, maintenance, and repairs. Project-based specialty subcontracting, generally awarded as fixed-price, lump-sum, or unit-price scopes with progress billing and change orders; repeat revenue comes through relationships with general contractors, fabricators, precast suppliers, industrial owners, and public agencies rather than subscriptions.

records.title: Offices of Chiropractors
records.search_text: Independent group and multisite chiropractic practices in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly provide diagnostic assessment, spinal or extremity manipulation, related neuromusculoskeletal treatment, rehabilitation guidance, care-plan management, and patient administration to external patients and payers. Patients are the service recipients and may also be the direct economic customers; commercial insurers, Medicare, workers' compensation programs, and other payers may fund covered visits. Revenue is commonly tied to visits, procedures, treatment episodes, or cash-pay care rather than disclosed labor hours, and repeat treatment creates recurring patient interactions without guaranteeing contractual recurrence.

records.title: Other Similar Organizations (except Business, Professional, Labor, and Political Organizations)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market organizations in NAICS 813990 that repeatedly provide member-interest, governance, administration, communications, regulatory, event, or community-association services funded by recurring dues, assessments, fees, sponsorships, or repeat programs, including property-owner, condominium, homeowner, cooperative-owner, tenant, and regulatory athletic associations. Members and represented communities pay recurring dues, assessments, initiation charges, program or certification fees, and event charges; some organizations also earn sponsorship, advertising, licensing, disciplinary, management, or service revenue. Staff deliver governance support, member records, billing and collections, communications, meetings, compliance, vendor coordination, events, and issue resolution.

records.title: Tire and Tube Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant distributors and jobbers that repeatedly source, stock, sell, and deliver new or used tires and tubes for passenger, light-truck, commercial-truck, bus, industrial, off-road, farm, and other vehicles to dealers, installers, fleets, retailers, and business users. Repeat B2B customers purchase replacement or original-equipment tires and tubes; distributors earn product margin and may provide assortment availability, fitment support, warehousing, route delivery, emergency fulfillment, credit, returns, warranty, casing, and recall administration.

records.title: Other Paperboard Container Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US manufacturers in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band repeatedly supplying custom setup paperboard boxes and specialty fiber tubes, cores, cans, drums, mailing cases, reels, and similar containers to external industrial and consumer customers. Repeat business-to-business programs for specification-defined rigid boxes and specialty fiber containers, sold through quotes, purchase orders, releases, and supply agreements with pricing based on materials, dimensions, printing or finishing, tooling, run length, and freight.

records.title: Other Spectator Sports
records.search_text: Privately controlled U.S. multi-person sports-training, coaching, scouting, athlete-performance, and competition-support firms whose recurring external service prepares or supports participants in spectator sports events or competitions. Repeat outsourced support sold to athletes, families, teams, schools, clubs, and competition organizations through monthly memberships, session packages, season or team contracts, retainers, assessments, and specialized project fees. Delivery combines recurring coaching or training with performance analysis, program design, documentation, travel, and event support.

records.title: Glass and Glazing Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing, replacing, repairing, and maintaining architectural glass, storefronts, interior glazing, and curtain-wall or related glazing systems for external customers, including firms whose recurring activity is a sequence of bid projects and service calls. Revenue is primarily project-based or service-call contracting for general contractors, building owners, property managers, retailers, institutions, and homeowners; work is commonly won through fixed-price bids, negotiated subcontracts, unit pricing, or time-and-material repair orders.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Wood Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US manufacturers of miscellaneous wood and cork products with repeat external B2B supply relationships, including standardized dowels, handles, ladders, kiln-drying output, wooden housings, and other recurring industrial or wholesale products, provided the operation is transferable and in the target band. Revenue comes from per-unit, batch, or processing sales, often under repeat purchase orders or distributor relationships; it is product revenue rather than a recurring service fee, except for limited repeat processing such as kiln drying.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Nonmetallic Mineral Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing externally sold B2B industrial and building products within 327999, including dry-mix concrete, stucco, mica products, engineered proppants, synthetic stone for industrial use, and off-site reclaimed asphalt or concrete products. Repeat sales of bagged, bulk, formulated, or specification-controlled mineral products to distributors, contractors, construction-material dealers, oilfield customers, and industrial users through purchase orders, dealer programs, and supply agreements.

records.title: Turbine and Turbine Generator Set Units Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying complete nonaircraft steam, hydraulic, gas, and wind turbines or turbine-generator set units, together with manufacturer-provided parts, upgrades, technical support, and service, to external power, industrial, and infrastructure customers. Long-cycle engineered equipment and generator-set projects supplemented by installed-base replacement parts, planned outages, controls upgrades, long-term service agreements, and technical support; sales are negotiated directly or through major project channels.

records.title: Unlaminated Plastics Film and Sheet (except Packaging) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers converting plastics resins into nonpackaging plastics film and unlaminated sheet for recurring or repeat external customers, including specification-bound industrial and specialty-film programs. Recurring and repeat B2B sales of film or sheet by roll, sheet, weight, or production order to industrial customers under product specifications; pricing reflects resin, conversion, performance, service, and qualification, with index-based or other cost pass-through arrangements in some contracts.

records.title: Fertilizer (Mixing Only) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market fertilizer blenders that repeatedly formulate and mix purchased nutrient ingredients to external customer, retailer, agronomist, or private-label specifications. Recurring seasonal or programmatic blend orders priced per ton, batch, or formulation, combining purchased straight fertilizers and micronutrients with ingredient, freight, handling, storage, and blending economics.

records.title: Fine Arts Schools
records.search_text: US fine-arts schools in the lower-middle-market band providing recurring or repeat paid instruction in music, dance, drama, visual arts, crafts, or related nondegree arts disciplines to external households and other customers. Primarily household-paid recurring tuition or lesson fees, often monthly or term-based, supplemented by camps, recitals, competitions, materials, and private lessons; some schools also receive institutional contracts or grants.

records.title: Other Nonscheduled Air Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators providing recurring or repeat outsourced specialty air transportation with general-purpose aircraft, principally helicopter air-ambulance, utility, offshore-support, search-and-rescue, and other special-mission flying for hospitals, governments, industrial customers, and other external buyers. Customers pay through multi-period availability or master-service agreements, fixed monthly standing charges plus flight-hour fees, day rates plus hourly charges, government contracts, hospital arrangements, or per-transport insurer and public-payer reimbursement.

records.title: Support Activities for Rail Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing recurring outsourced rail switching and terminal support, railcar and track inspection, repair and maintenance, and rail-freight handling services to external railroads, railcar owners, shippers, and industrial customers. Customers buy physical, often safety-critical operating, inspection, repair, maintenance, and handling services through work orders, recurring service arrangements, and sometimes long-term contracts with committed minimum values or volumes.

records.title: Passenger Car Leasing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms leasing passenger cars, light-duty passenger trucks, passenger vans, and sport utility vehicles without drivers for generally long periods, including recurring fleet-leasing and related account-management services sold to external customers. Operators earn recurring lease payments over multi-month or multi-year contracts and may also charge or embed fleet-management, maintenance, telematics, compliance, fuel, and vehicle-disposal services. Customers include businesses, governments, organizations, and some individual lessees.

records.title: Fiber Optic Cable Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers that repeatedly sell insulated fiber-optic cable, cable assemblies, and closely related manufactured connectivity products to external enterprise, telecom, data-center, industrial, and specialty customers. Repeat business-to-business product sales, commonly specification- or project-driven, with revenue from manufactured cable and assemblies sold through direct accounts and distribution channels; qualification, delivery performance, and application engineering support recurring customer relationships.

records.title: Iron and Steel Pipe and Tube Manufacturing from Purchased Steel
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers repeatedly forming, welding, drawing, finishing, testing, and selling iron and steel pipe and tube made from purchased steel to external customers. Repeat spot, program, and contract product sales to oil and gas, pipeline, mechanical, structural, automotive, energy, construction, boiler, heat-exchanger, and industrial customers, priced by ton or length with grade, dimensions, finish, testing, processing, steel input, freight, and market conditions reflected.

records.title: Sewage Treatment Facilities
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators of sewer systems and sewage treatment facilities that collect, convey, treat, and dispose of wastewater for external residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, institutional, or wholesale customers. Recurring tariff, assessment, contracted, availability, capacity, volumetric, or wholesale revenue for accountable operation of sewer collection and wastewater treatment infrastructure serving external customers.

records.title: Industrial Launderers
records.search_text: U.S. industrial laundries supplying laundered work uniforms, protective and clean-room apparel, and dust-control textiles on rental or contract service routes, including washing, finishing, inventory control, pickup, and delivery. Recurring business-to-business rental or service contracts combining textile inventory, scheduled laundering, replacement, and route delivery; ancillary revenue can include loss charges, special processing, and related facility-service items.

records.title: Heating Equipment (except Warm Air Furnaces) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of heating boilers, heating stoves, floor and wall furnaces, wall and baseboard units, and related non-warm-air heating equipment supplied repeatedly to distributors, OEMs, contractors, building owners, and institutional customers. Repeat B2B equipment and replacement-part sales through wholesale, contractor, OEM, specification, and project channels, generally priced per unit or configured system with distributor discounts, bids, warranties, and periodic material-price adjustments.

records.title: Rubber and Plastics Hoses and Belting Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying rubber or reinforced-plastics hose, belting, and purchased-hose garden-hose products to external OEM, distributor, replacement, and industrial customers. Repeat purchase orders, supply agreements, catalog and distributor sales, and OEM programs; revenue is predominantly product sales rather than time-based professional fees.

records.title: Nonwoven Fabric Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market mills repeatedly supplying external customers with nonwoven fabrics, felts, and further-fabricated nonwoven products made by mechanical, chemical, thermal, solvent, or combined bonding processes. Repeat B2B sale of engineered roll goods, sheets, felts, or converted nonwoven components by specification, volume, production run, or supply agreement to medical/PPE, wipes, filtration, construction, industrial, and other downstream manufacturers.

records.title: Farm Supplies Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market merchant wholesalers repeatedly supplying farms, ranches, agricultural retailers, or other wholesalers with animal feed, seed, fertilizer, agricultural chemicals, hay, straw, bulbs, and related consumable farm supplies, together with procurement, inventory, delivery, credit, compliance, and product-support workflows. Recurring and seasonal B2B resale of consumable agricultural inputs, usually earning a product spread and sometimes service, delivery, financing, application, or agronomy-related revenue; demand is tied to planted acreage, livestock production, input intensity, weather, and farm economics.

records.title: Seafood Product Preparation and Packaging
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market seafood processors and packagers that repeatedly provide custom, toll, contract, co-packing, freezing, filleting, cooking, smoking, canning, or packaging services to external harvesters, aquaculture producers, brands, cooperatives, importers, or distributors. Recurring or seasonal repeat processing for external product owners, generally billed per pound, per lot, per case, or under negotiated toll/co-packing agreements, with added charges for storage, packaging, labeling, certification, and specialty processes.

records.title: Electronic Connector Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers that repeatedly supply engineered electronic connectors and interconnect components to external OEMs, contract manufacturers, distributors, and industrial customers, with emphasis on custom, application-specific, or qualified programs. Revenue is primarily point-in-time product sales by unit, lot, or program to OEMs, their contract manufacturers, distributors, and industrial users. Tooling, engineering, qualification, and value-added interconnect assembly can be separately priced or embedded in product margins; repeat revenue follows platform production, replacement, and new-design wins.

records.title: Wood Preservation
records.search_text: Independent U.S. facilities that repeatedly pressure- or thermally treat lumber, railroad ties, utility poles, posts, pilings, crossarms, and similar wood for external customers. Repeat batch product and toll-treatment sales to lumber distributors, railroads, utilities, infrastructure contractors, agricultural suppliers, and builders, generally priced per unit or volume with wood and preservative inputs reflected in quotes or contracts.

records.title: Powder Metallurgy Part Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers producing pressed-and-sintered, metal-injection-molded, and related powder-metallurgy parts on repeat job or order programs for external automotive, industrial, appliance, medical, aerospace, defense, and consumer-product customers. Recurring program and release-order revenue priced per part or lot, commonly supported by customer-specific tooling, material and process specifications, qualification, inspection, and quality documentation.

records.title: Polystyrene Foam Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying expanded or extruded polystyrene foam insulation, protective packaging, cold-chain containers, food-service items where lawful, and other molded or cut foam products to external customers. Repeat B2B product sales under purchase orders, project quotes, distributor arrangements, and supply agreements, priced by unit, board, volume, weight, or run; the mix spans commodity foodware and insulation through custom protective packaging and molded components.

records.title: Drycleaning and Laundry Services (except Coin-Operated)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly clean, press, finish, receive, collect, or deliver customer-owned garments and household textiles through staffed plants, stores, routes, or drop sites. Households and small commercial accounts usually pay per item, per bag, by weight, or through recurring pickup and delivery plans for garment and textile cleaning.

records.title: Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, Land
records.search_text: Privately controlled U.S. operators of repeat, ticketed local land sightseeing experiences, including sightseeing buses and trolleys, scenic rail excursions, and horse-drawn sightseeing rides, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Primarily direct-to-consumer, prepaid per-passenger tickets or day passes for scheduled or reservable local experiences, with secondary group, private-hire, food, merchandise, and attraction-bundle revenue; repeat service is delivered across departures and seasons rather than through long-term customer contracts.

records.title: Nonferrous Metal Die-Casting Foundries
records.search_text: Independent US nonferrous die-casting foundries in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band repeatedly producing customer-designed cast components under external supply programs. Recurring business-to-business programs priced per part or lot, usually supported by tooling, qualification, quality requirements, metal surcharges, purchase orders, releases, and annual or multiyear supply agreements.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Metal Stamping
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market outsourced Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 manufacturers of recurring high-volume automotive metal stampings and welded subassemblies, including progressive, transfer, deep-draw, and related tool-and-die-supported production for body, chassis, seating, closure, safety, and powertrain-neutral applications. Suppliers receive vehicle-program awards from OEMs and larger Tier suppliers, recover tooling and launch engineering separately or through program economics, and earn recurring per-part revenue against customer releases. Quantities are not guaranteed and revenue follows platform production, with annual productivity negotiations and customer-specific quality, PPAP, and delivery obligations.

records.title: Animal (except Poultry) Slaughtering
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market slaughter and meat-processing operators that repeatedly provide custom, toll, or inspected processing services to livestock owners, farms, brands, and other external customers. Recurring or repeat business-to-business and owner-paid processing, commonly billed as a slaughter fee plus a per-pound processing charge, with optional packaging and value-added service fees.

records.title: Cheese Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly manufacture natural, processed, specialty, private-label, or substitute cheese products for external retail, foodservice, distributor, or industrial customers. Repeat product sales under distributor, retailer, foodservice, ingredient, branded, and private-label relationships; revenue is primarily unit- or weight-priced rather than explicitly billed labor.

records.title: Miscellaneous Financial Investment Activities
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms repeatedly supplying external customers with fee- or commission-based miscellaneous financial investment services classified in 523999, including securities or commodities exchange clearing services, stock quotation services, gas lease brokerage, and other qualifying financial-contract agency services. Customers include exchanges, broker-dealers, market participants, data users, energy and mineral-rights parties, and other institutions. Revenue can include clearing, membership, transaction, subscription, data, access, agency, and commission fees; economics vary sharply between regulated clearing infrastructure, scalable quotation data, and relationship-driven gas lease brokerage.

records.title: Other Engine Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of internal-combustion engines other than automotive gasoline and aircraft engines, including diesel, semidiesel, stationary, marine, nonroad, and other application engines supplied repeatedly to external OEM, distributor, fleet, power, and industrial customers. Repeat business-to-business engine and parts sales under OEM programs, dealer or distributor channels, fleet specifications, generator and equipment platforms, and replacement or service-parts relationships, generally priced by unit, configuration, or supply agreement.

records.title: Plastics Pipe and Pipe Fitting Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying plastic pipe, conduit, and fittings to distributors, utilities, contractors, agricultural users, and industrial customers.
