Repeat business-to-business product sales under purchase orders, distributor programs, project bids, and supply agreements, generally priced per length, fitting, weight, or shipment with resin and freight adjustments.

records.title: Blood and Organ Banks
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent operators providing recurring blood or source-plasma donor recruitment, collection, processing, testing coordination, storage, inventory management, and distribution services to hospitals or plasma-product manufacturers. Operators receive per-unit product or processing revenue, contracted hospital or manufacturer payments, collection and testing fees, and sometimes donor-related service revenue; customers require validated collection, traceability, quality, storage, and delivery rather than software alone.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Steering and Suspension Components (except Spring) Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers of steering and suspension components other than springs, including control arms, steering linkages, knuckles, shock absorbers, struts, stabilizer assemblies, and related machined or fabricated subassemblies. Repeat B2B sales to vehicle OEMs, tier-one integrators, distributors, and aftermarket channels, screened for firms plausibly in the $1-10 million EBITDA lower-middle-market range and serving external customers.

records.title: Wood Office Furniture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of stock or custom wood office furniture supplied repeatedly to dealers, architects, contract-furniture distributors, corporate and government buyers, hospitality or education clients, and private-label customers. Repeat B2B product and project revenue through dealer, specification, bid, OEM or private-label, renovation, and direct channels, generally priced per SKU, workstation, configured package, or project with discounts, freight, installation coordination, warranties, and line reviews.

records.title: Nonchocolate Confectionery Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US lower-middle-market contract and private-label manufacturers repeatedly formulating, producing, and packaging nonchocolate confections such as gummies, hard candy, mints, chews, caramels, lozenges, and taffy for external brands and retailers. Recurring product-development, batch-conversion, and packaging revenue from brands and retailers, generally priced per unit, case, or production run under purchase orders, fixed-term supply agreements, or periodically rebid private-label programs.

records.title: Other Foundation, Structure, and Building Exterior Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing and repairing exterior architectural-metal and structural-access systems for external customers, including metal curtain walls, fire escapes, metal balconies and stairways, and ornamental or decorative metalwork, with associated on-site welding. Project-based specialty subcontracting for general contractors and building owners, usually against drawings, specifications, bids, and progress-payment schedules; repair, rehabilitation, and code-driven replacement provide repeat work across a customer and property base.

records.title: Cotton Ginning
records.search_text: Independent, cooperative, and custom cotton-ginning establishments that repeatedly separate lint from seed and perform associated drying, cleaning, contamination control, baling, and shipment preparation for external growers or cotton owners. Seasonal business-to-business processing fees paid by growers, cooperatives, or cotton owners, generally linked to bales or volume handled, with possible ancillary revenue from seed handling, storage, and related services.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Electrical Equipment and Component Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. firms providing repeat outsourced engineering and production of specialized power-conversion, power-supply, power-quality, UPS, high-voltage, ultrasonic, and related industrial electrical apparatus for external customers. Recurring B2B programs based on customer specifications, qualification, configurable designs, repairable installed products, and repeat purchase orders; revenue comes from engineered units, production runs, spares, upgrades, and associated testing or support.

records.title: Plastics Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of plastic bathtubs, shower stalls, sinks, lavatories, toilet fixtures, and related plastic plumbing fixtures supplied repeatedly to distributors, builders, remodelers, hospitality customers, and OEM or private-label accounts. Repeat B2B product sales through distributor, wholesale, builder, OEM, and project channels, generally priced per unit or product line with periodic negotiated adjustments and meaningful freight and resin exposure.

records.title: Port and Harbor Operations
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market private firms that repeatedly operate ports, harbors, docking and pier facilities, waterfront terminals, or canals for external vessel operators, shippers, cargo owners, or public port authorities. Eligible operators earn recurring service, access, docking, storage, facility-use, concession, and terminal-operation revenue under permits, leases, concessions, minimum commitments, per-unit charges, and service contracts.

records.title: Cemeteries and Crematories
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators of cemeteries, memorial gardens, mausoleums, pet cemeteries, and stand-alone crematories that provide repeat or recurring disposition, interment, plot, memorial-property, records, and care services to external families or funeral-service customers. Customers are families, estate representatives, funeral homes, and pet owners. Revenue is primarily transaction- and contract-based through interment or cremation charges, cemetery property and memorial rights, and pre-need arrangements, with some recurring care or maintenance fees.

records.title: Iron Foundries
records.search_text: Independent US iron foundries that repeatedly engineer and pour ductile, gray, malleable, compacted-graphite, or semisteel castings to external customer specifications and have transferable operations in the target band. Revenue is earned per casting, pound, tooling program, lot, or machining/finishing step under recurring OEM, distributor, infrastructure, and industrial purchase orders, often with metal and energy surcharges.

records.title: Coastal and Great Lakes Freight Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators providing recurring or repeat outsourced cargo transportation in coastal waters, on the Great Lakes System, or between US domestic ports, including crewed coastal and Great Lakes barge and vessel charter services. Business and government shippers buy voyages, contracts of affreightment, or time charters, with a mix of annual or multi-year term contracts and market-priced spot movements; the carrier supplies vessels, qualified crews, compliance, and accountable transport execution.

records.title: Inland Water Passenger Transportation
records.search_text: Privately owned lower-middle-market operators providing recurring or repeat passenger ferry and water-taxi transportation on U.S. lakes, rivers, and intracoastal waterways outside the Great Lakes System. Riders or sponsoring organizations buy trips, passes, or contracted service; revenue is typically posted passenger and vehicle fares, multi-trip products, or fixed/unit-rate public or institutional service contracts.

records.title: Miscellaneous Intermediation
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operating firms classified in 523910 that repeatedly source, analyze, acquire, manage, and sell financial contracts or contractual rights as principals to external counterparties and have a transferable team, process, data, and control structure. The NAICS core acts as principal and puts its own capital at risk, earning realized or unrealized investment gains and spreads rather than a conventional recurring service fee. The small lens-eligible subset repeatedly originates and exits contracts with external counterparties, but its economics depend on deal selection, purchase price, holding period, financing, and exit proceeds.

records.title: Concrete Pipe Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of concrete culvert, storm-drain, sanitary-sewer, and other concrete pipe sold repeatedly to external public agencies, utilities, distributors, contractors, and infrastructure projects, including production, engineering support, quality control, sales, logistics, and administration. Business-to-business and business-to-government product sales, commonly through project specifications, distributor or contractor orders, and competitive bids; heavy products are supplied from regional plants and require engineering, testing, delivery, and quality documentation.

records.title: Asphalt Paving Mixture and Block Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market asphalt-mixture and paving-block manufacturers with repeat external customers, including independent hot-, warm-, and cold-mix plants and genuine contract or toll-mixing operations serving road contractors, governments, utilities, and commercial pavers. Predominantly per-ton or per-load sales of specified mixtures, with repeat project and account relationships; qualifying toll or contract mixing earns processing revenue for external customers.

records.title: Totalizing Fluid Meter and Counting Device Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of water and gas consumption meters, other totalizing fluid meters, parking and fare devices, vehicle gauges, and counting devices that repeatedly supply external customers, including associated communications, software, configuration, and support. Device and system sales to utilities, municipalities, transportation operators, OEMs, distributors, and facilities, with repeat revenue from fleet replacement, new endpoints, communications modules, spares, software, configuration, maintenance, and support.

records.title: Rooming and Boarding Houses, Dormitories, and Workers' Camps
records.search_text: US operators of rooming and boarding houses, off-campus dormitories, and worker accommodations that repeatedly provide managed lodging and related services to paying residents or external institutional or employer customers, with normalized EBITDA of roughly $1-10 million. Residents commonly pay recurring bed or room rent under monthly or academic-year arrangements; student housing may be leased by the bed, while externally operated worker housing may be paid by an employer or project sponsor. Housekeeping, meals, laundry, maintenance, and resident support may be bundled or sold as complementary services.

records.title: Rolled Steel Shape Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers repeatedly rolling or drawing plate, sheet, strip, rod, bar, rebar, light structural sections, rails, and other non-wire shapes from purchased steel for external customers. Repeat spot, program, and contract sales to construction, infrastructure, service-center, automotive, machinery, agricultural, energy, petrochemical, and general manufacturing customers, priced by ton, dimensions, grade, finish, processing, freight, and steel-market conditions.

records.title: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly engineering, winding, assembling, testing, repairing, and supplying power, distribution, instrument, control, isolation, welding, and other specialty transformers to external utilities, OEMs, industrial customers, contractors, and distributors. Quoted per-unit, program, or project sales, often engineered to utility or OEM specifications, with design, materials, testing, documentation, warranty, and sometimes repair or lifecycle support bundled under repeat accounts.

records.title: All Other Transportation Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US manufacturers of powered off-road and low-speed specialty transportation equipment, including all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, golf carts, similar passenger carriers, and go-carts, with repeat model, parts, accessory, warranty, fleet-support, or service revenue sold to external customers in the lower-middle-market band. Customers include dealers, rental and resort fleets, golf courses, commercial sites, recreational users, municipalities, and specialty distributors. Revenue combines vehicles with repeat model orders, replacement parts, accessories, warranty work, fleet support, and sometimes direct maintenance or refurbishment.

records.title: Framing Contractors
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market contractors performing structural framing and sheathing, including wood, light-gauge steel, post framing, and onsite truss or wood-frame component work for builders, general contractors, developers, and property owners. Project-based subcontract revenue, commonly bid as a fixed-price or lump-sum scope and billed by percentage of completion; some work uses time-and-materials or unit-price terms. Repeat business comes from recurring builder and general-contractor relationships rather than subscriptions.

records.title: Limousine Service
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators of reserved limousine and luxury-sedan transportation for repeat external customers, including corporate accounts, hospitality and airport work, and recurring relationships with event planners and institutions. Reserved trips, hourly or itinerary-based bookings, and account work paid by corporate, hospitality, institutional, event, and affluent individual customers; repeat usage may be contractual, account-based, or relationship-driven rather than scheduled-route revenue.

records.title: Other Performing Arts Companies
records.search_text: For-profit circus, magic, ice-skating, traveling-carnival, and similar live-performance companies in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly provide produced shows to venues, promoters, corporate buyers, fairs, or other external customers under booking or performance contracts. Repeat business-to-business bookings and fixed or negotiated performance fees from venues, promoters, fairs, casinos, resorts, and corporate-event buyers; some companies may also receive admissions participation or ancillary revenue.

records.title: Business and Secretarial Schools
records.search_text: Privately owned providers of repeat or recurring employer-contracted instruction in office procedures, administrative support, reception, communications, word processing, and related secretarial skills. Employer-paid cohort, seat, subscription, or fixed-fee training sold repeatedly to external organizations; learner-paid tuition without a repeat organizational customer is excluded.

records.title: Civic and Social Organizations
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market civic and social organizations that repeatedly serve unaffiliated members or participants through dues-supported membership, clubs, alumni or ethnic associations, scouting and youth programs, fraternal or veterans' organizations, recreation, events, facilities, and related food or beverage services when there is a durable external-member service model. Customers and beneficiaries are members, families, alumni, veterans, ethnic or fraternal constituencies, youth participants, and event or facility users. Revenue commonly combines recurring dues and initiation fees with program charges, events, food and beverage sales, facility use, contributions, sponsorships, and grants.

records.title: Reinsurance Carriers
records.search_text: Independently controlled commercial reinsurance carriers writing recurring treaty or facultative life, health, property-casualty, and specialty risk-transfer contracts, including underwriting, actuarial analysis, contract wording, exposure aggregation, portfolio management, claims oversight, finance, and regulatory reporting. The reinsurer earns recurring premiums from ceding insurers in exchange for assuming defined policy or portfolio risks, deploying capital and underwriting expertise across renewal cycles, and generating underwriting and investment returns net of claims, commissions, retrocession, and operating expense.

records.title: Ice Cream and Frozen Dessert Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly manufacture ice cream, frozen yogurt, frozen ices, sherbet, frozen tofu, and other frozen desserts for external retail, foodservice, distributor, private-label, or branded customers. Repeat unit- or volume-priced product sales through retail, foodservice, distributor, branded, club, and private-label channels, often with seasonal demand, promotions, slotting, and cold-chain requirements.

records.title: Wood Window and Door Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market wood window and door manufacturers that repeatedly provide outsourced custom fabrication, configuration, prehanging, finishing, or specification-controlled production to builders, dealers, distributors, or other external customers. Eligible firms receive repeat B2B orders and earn revenue through customer-specific fabrication and production services bundled into the price of physical window and door units; standard catalog manufacturing remains product revenue and is outside the lens.

records.title: Tile and Terrazzo Contractors
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contractors installing ceramic tile, interior stone, mosaic, and job-site terrazzo for external residential and commercial customers, including new construction, renovation, maintenance, and repair work supported by repeat relationships with general contractors, builders, designers, property operators, or recurring customers. Project revenue from property owners and, frequently, subcontract work for general contractors or builders; jobs are commonly bid from plans, measurements, labor, and material requirements, with repeat business driven by trade relationships rather than subscription contracts.

records.title: Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings (except Miniwarehouses)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market owner-lessors and master lessors of office, retail, industrial, medical, lodging, and other nonresidential buildings, excluding self-storage, that repeatedly lease space to external tenants and retain accountable leasing, building-operation, maintenance, compliance, or vendor-oversight functions. External business and institutional tenants pay recurring base rent and, depending on lease structure, reimburse some taxes, insurance, utilities, maintenance, or common-area costs; the lessor provides usable space and remains responsible directly or through vendors for lease administration and building stewardship.

records.title: Political Organizations
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market-scale political parties, political action committees, and multi-cycle political organizations with recurring fundraising, compliance, voter contact, communications, field, and administrative operations serving a party, candidates, donors, or supporters. Organizations raise regulated contributions and transfers and spend them on candidate or party support, voter contact, media, fundraising, field operations, compliance, staff, and administration; supporters and political principals fund mission activity rather than purchasing a conventional fee-for-service product.

records.title: Ground or Treated Mineral and Earth Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly calcine, dead-burn, grind, blend, activate, or otherwise process clays, barite, ceramic and refractory minerals, fillers, absorbents, and other nonmetallic minerals for external industrial customers. Repeat B2B sales of specification-controlled bulk or packaged mineral products under purchase orders, supply agreements, and customer formulas, with pricing influenced by grade, processing, energy, packaging, freight, and commodity competition.

records.title: All Other Business Support Services
records.search_text: Recurring outsourced address coding, barcode imprinting, mail preparation, and presort services for external customers, including the associated data cleansing, job setup, exception handling, and customer reporting. Business, nonprofit, and institutional mailers pay processing fees tied mainly to pieces, jobs, or recurring programs. Providers combine address data work, USPS-compliant preparation, physical sorting, and logistics so customers can obtain workshare discounts and reliable induction.

records.title: Steel Investment Foundries
records.search_text: Independent US steel investment foundries that repeatedly produce detailed lost-wax steel castings to external aerospace, defense, turbine, industrial, medical, automotive, fluid-handling, or other customer specifications and have transferable operations in the target band. Revenue is earned through tooling, qualification, casting, heat-treatment, finishing, inspection, and sometimes machining charges under repeat part programs and purchase orders, commonly with alloy and energy surcharges.

records.title: Nature Parks and Other Similar Institutions
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating preserved natural areas, nature centers, wildlife sanctuaries, natural-wonder attractions such as caverns and waterfalls, and similar settings that repeatedly serve paying visitors, members, schools, or program customers. Mostly posted-price admissions, tours, passes, memberships, education and events, with ancillary food and merchandise; nonprofit operators also rely on donations and grants. Customers are predominantly individuals, families, members, schools, and organized groups.

records.title: Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers and prototype-to-production developers of complete guided missiles or space vehicles that repeatedly supply external government, prime-contractor, or commercial-space customers. Long-cycle government and prime contracts plus commercial-space programs, with recurring development, production lots, testing, configuration, compliance, and sustainment work.

records.title: Household Appliances, Electric Housewares, and Consumer Electronics Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers repeatedly supplying household appliances, electric housewares, televisions, audio-video equipment, and other consumer electronics to retailers, dealers, installers, hospitality buyers, and other external business customers, including the associated ordering, merchandising, logistics, returns, warranty, credit, and product-support work. Recurring and repeat B2B product sales earned through a merchandise gross margin, sometimes supplemented by freight, handling, configuration, warranty, marketing, financing, or other value-added fees.

records.title: Repossession Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms whose principal service is recovering tangible collateral for creditors after delinquency, including assignment intake, skip tracing, field recovery, transport coordination, storage, condition reporting, compliance documentation, invoicing, and lender status communication. Creditors, auto-finance servicers, equipment lenders, and forwarding companies place repeat assignments; agencies generally earn transaction or recovery fees plus ancillary charges for field attempts, transport, storage, keys, personal-property handling, and related services.

records.title: In-Vitro Diagnostic Substance Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of in-vitro diagnostic reagents, substances, calibrators, controls, and related consumable preparations supplied repeatedly to external laboratories, device companies, distributors, or healthcare customers. Recurring or repeat consumable sales and contract-manufacturing revenue under supply agreements, standing orders, distributor arrangements, or OEM and private-label programs, generally priced per kit, vial, lot, or unit.

records.title: Current-Carrying Wiring Device Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly produce customer-specific, private-label, or qualified plugs, terminals, bus bars, receptacles, GFCIs, switches, lamp holders, and related current-carrying wiring devices for external OEM, distribution, and infrastructure customers. Customers buy recurring production programs under electrical, dimensional, material, safety, testing, certification, packaging, and delivery specifications through purchase orders or supply agreements.

records.title: Industrial Gas Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly produce and supply compressed, liquefied, or solid industrial gases to external customers through on-site, merchant/bulk, or packaged-cylinder delivery. Recurring B2B product and cylinder-use revenue under purchase orders and multi-year supply agreements; larger on-site customers may have minimum purchases and indexed pass-through, while merchant and packaged customers receive repeated deliveries or pickups.

records.title: Spring Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market spring manufacturers that repeatedly engineer and produce springs from purchased wire, strip, or rod to external-customer drawings and performance specifications. OEM and industrial customers buy repeat production runs under drawings, material, heat-treatment, tolerance, fatigue-life, testing, packaging, and delivery specifications through purchase orders or supply agreements.

records.title: Dance Companies
records.search_text: US dance companies producing recurring or repeat live dance performances and related contracted programs for ticket buyers, presenters, schools, sponsors, and community or institutional customers, within the roughly $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. A mixed model of fixed-price tickets and subscriptions, presenter or institutional performance fees, education and outreach contracts, sponsorship, and contributed or grant revenue; the recurring-service screen is met through repeated seasons, tours, residencies, and programs rather than continuous service contracts.

records.title: New Housing For-Sale Builders
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market for-sale home builders with a material pattern of repeat external-customer work, principally repeated bulk or programmatic sales to institutional, investor, or community-development buyers, including the associated planning, procurement, project administration, sales coordination, handover, and warranty workflows. The qualifying edge case earns primarily project and home-sale revenue under repeated relationships with external bulk or programmatic buyers. Prices are generally set per home or portfolio rather than as cost-plus labor reimbursement; warranty and handover obligations accompany the sale but are not assumed to be standalone recurring revenue.

records.title: Support Activities for Nonmetallic Minerals (except Fuels) Mining
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market independent contractors performing recurring or repeat drilling, blasting, exploration support, shaft or tunnel development, overburden stripping, and closely related mine-support work for nonmetallic-mineral mines and quarries on a contract or fee basis. Quarry and nonmetallic-mineral mine operators pay independent contractors through unit rates such as dollars per foot or meter drilled, per blast, or per quantity moved, plus mobilization, equipment, day-rate, and project charges. Revenue recurs through production campaigns, maintenance programs, and repeat site work, although volumes and awards can reset with mine plans, construction demand, and rebids.

records.title: Amusement and Theme Parks
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators of amusement parks, theme parks, and water parks that earn repeat admissions and in-park revenue from external guests, including season-pass and group customers. Predominantly direct-to-consumer admissions, season or annual passes, food and beverage, retail, games, parking, and premium add-ons; group sales and sponsorships are secondary.

records.title: Adhesive Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market toll, contract, and private-label adhesive manufacturers providing repeat formulation, scale-up, blending, testing, filling, packaging, and outsourced capacity to external brands and industrial customers. Brands and industrial users buy recurring batches, overflow capacity, custom formulations, private-label products, and associated QC under specifications and purchase orders; customers may own formulas in toll arrangements or rely on the manufacturer for formulation and compliance.

records.title: Amusement Arcades
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators of amusement arcades and arcade-focused parlors that earn repeat revenue from external guests through game play, stored-value cards, redemptions, parties, memberships or loyalty programs, and related food and beverage. Primarily direct-to-consumer pay-per-play or timed admission, increasingly through stored-value RFID cards, kiosks, and mobile applications; redemption prizes, parties, food and beverage, memberships, and promotions provide ancillary and repeat revenue.

records.title: Forest Nurseries and Gathering of Forest Products
records.search_text: U.S. private lower-middle-market forest nurseries growing bareroot or containerized tree seedlings for reforestation, restoration and commercial forestry, including seed and seedlot management, propagation, irrigation, cultivation, inventory and quality control, lifting, grading, packing, cold storage and shipment. Nurseries sell seedlings by unit, tray, lot or contracted growing program to timber companies, public agencies, tribes, conservation organizations, restoration contractors and private landowners. Revenue repeats with harvest replanting and restoration programs but follows multi-season seed and production planning, species and provenance requirements, customer contracts, public funding, wildfire recovery and forestry cycles.

records.title: Office Furniture (except Wood) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying stock or custom nonwood office desks, systems, seating, storage, tables, workstations, and related office-type furniture to external dealers, corporate, education, healthcare, hospitality, and government customers. Dealer and contract-channel orders, project bids, catalog sales, corporate standards programs, and repeat replacement or reconfiguration purchases, generally priced per piece, workstation, lot, or project.

records.title: Small Arms, Ordnance, and Ordnance Accessories Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market OEM, build-to-print, contract, private-label, and high-volume component manufacturers that repeatedly produce small arms, receivers, barrels, actions, suppressors, weapon components, or ordnance accessories for external firearm brands, defense primes, and government customers. Customers place recurring purchase orders and program releases for machined parts, assemblies, finished or private-label weapons, and accessories. Revenue is generally per component, unit, lot, or production program, with engineering, tooling, qualification, traceability, finishing, testing, and configuration support incorporated into quotes or separately charged.

records.title: Welding and Soldering Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of arc, resistance, gas, plasma, laser, electron-beam, ultrasonic, and non-handheld soldering equipment; welding consumables and accessories; and welding automation that provide recurring or repeat outsourced service through system integration, maintenance, field repair, monitoring, software, training, consumable programs, precision fabrication, or lifecycle technical support. Manufacturers sell equipment, automation systems, electrodes, wire, rods, accessories, and related software through industrial distributors, retailers, integrators, and direct relationships with fabricators and OEMs. Eligible firms combine repeat consumables or equipment supply with integration, maintenance, monitoring, training, field repair, software, or engineered process support.

records.title: Other Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent OEM and Tier automotive suppliers making recurring-program components captured by the residual 336390 category, including thermal and HVAC systems, fluid and air-management parts, filters, airbags and restraint components, wheels, frames, bumpers, radiators, fuel tanks, exhaust-system parts, and related assemblies. Suppliers earn per-part revenue under OEM or Tier customer platform awards and production releases, often with separate or negotiated tooling, engineering, testing, and launch economics. Awards can last a vehicle program but quantities are not guaranteed; customer quality, traceability, warranty, delivery, and annual productivity expectations govern the relationship.

records.title: Musical Instrument Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market musical-instrument manufacturers that repeatedly provide external brands, dealers, schools, institutions, professional artists, or owners with OEM and private-label production, custom instruments, institutional programs, warranty support, factory repair, parts, or technical service. Eligible firms combine physical instrument production with repeat B2B manufacturing programs or an ongoing accountable service relationship. Revenue may include contracted production runs, customized institutional orders, factory service, parts, warranties, repair, and recurring dealer or artist support.

records.title: Fiber, Yarn, and Thread Mills
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market mills that repeatedly provide custom, toll, or contract fiber preparation, spinning, twisting, texturing, winding, blending, yarn design, or related processing to external industrial, apparel, craft, technical-textile, or government-supply-chain customers. Customers supply fiber or specifications and purchase recurring lots priced by processed or finished weight, setup, complexity, finishing options, design time, or negotiated industrial conversion terms.

records.title: Other Personal and Household Goods Repair and Maintenance
records.search_text: US LMM firms repairing or servicing personal and household goods not assigned to the excluded repair industries, including garments, watches, jewelry, musical instruments, bicycles, motorcycles, and recreational boats, when supplied as repeat outsourced services to external customers. A heterogeneous mix of consumer and small-business repair revenue, usually per job or service event after inspection, with some maintenance plans, fleet or dealer relationships, storage-linked boat work, and repeat institutional accounts.

records.title: Passenger Car Rental
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly rent passenger cars, light-duty passenger trucks, passenger vans, and sport utility vehicles without drivers for short periods to unaffiliated leisure, business, insurance-replacement, dealership, and local customers. Revenue comes primarily from time- and vehicle-class-based rental charges, often dynamically priced, plus protection products, upgrades, fuel, late-return, equipment, and other ancillary charges. Service repeats across travelers and institutional referral channels while the operator owns or controls, prepares, maintains, repositions, and disposes of the fleet.

records.title: Support Activities for Metal Mining
records.search_text: Independent U.S. contractors providing repeat exploration drilling, mine development, blasting, excavation, dewatering, equipment operation and maintenance, sampling, surveying, geological field support, reclamation, and related services to metal-mine owners. Recurring or repeat project, footage-, unit-, day-rate, and time-and-materials services sold to external mine developers and operators, limited to transferable firms plausibly producing $1-10 million of normalized EBITDA.

records.title: Scenic and Sightseeing Transportation, Water
records.search_text: Privately controlled U.S. operators of repeat, ticketed local water experiences in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band, including excursion and harbor sightseeing boats, dinner cruises, airboat tours, whale and nature watches, and charter fishing boats with operators. Primarily direct-to-consumer prepaid per-passenger tickets for scheduled or reservable trips, with meaningful private-charter, group-event, food-and-beverage, and fishing-party revenue; repeat service is delivered across departures and seasons rather than mainly through long-term customer contracts.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Textile Product Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms providing repeat contract textile conversion, embellishment, cutting, binding, sewing, and custom or specification-driven industrial textile products to external commercial customers, including embroidery-for-trade and repeat-production technical-textile work. Repeat B2B job, batch, or supply-order revenue, often priced per unit or per production run under quotes, purchase orders, and recurring customer programs; customers outsource specialized labor, equipment, quality control, and fulfillment.

records.title: Audio and Video Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of professional and specialty speakers, amplifiers, microphones, mixers, public-address equipment, musical-instrument amplification, video capture and display devices, and connected audio-video systems, emphasizing proprietary software, installed systems, repeat professional users, accessories, support, and replacement cycles. Customers include professional integrators, venues, studios, broadcasters, musicians, houses of worship, education and enterprise buyers, vehicle OEMs, specialty retailers, and consumers. Revenue is predominantly equipment sales, augmented in the more attractive subset by embedded software, cloud control, licensing, accessories, service, repair, extended support, and repeat expansion of installed systems.

records.title: Printing Ink Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers of printing and inkjet inks and cartridges with repeat external sales to printers, converters, packagers, or equipment users, including formulation and technical support. Repeat B2B sales of color- and process-qualified consumables, commonly accompanied by matching, formulation, troubleshooting, inventory, and regulatory support.

records.title: Broom, Brush, and Mop Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of industrial, commercial, laboratory, medical, food-service, sanitation, and other professional brushes, brooms, mops, and related custom brushing or cleaning tools supplied repeatedly to external customers. Revenue is recurring business-to-business supply through standard catalog replenishment, wholesale and private-label programs, specification-driven custom brushes, replacement tools, prototypes, and repeat production runs for industrial, institutional, healthcare, laboratory, and food-service customers.

records.title: Livestock Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market cattle, hog, sheep, and goat merchant wholesalers, livestock auction-market merchants, and commission buyers with recurring producer, feedlot, or packer relationships and transferable operating teams. The coherent service is repeated livestock sourcing, matching, price discovery, physical handling, settlement, and compliance for producers and downstream buyers. Revenue is earned either as an inventory spread on principal transactions or as commissions and related market fees.

records.title: Nonscheduled Chartered Freight Air Transportation
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market certificated air carriers providing recurring or repeat outsourced, nonscheduled cargo-only charter, CMI, or ACMI flying to freight forwarders, integrators, government, airlines, and direct shippers. Customers buy aircraft capacity by trip or block hour under full-service charter, CMI, or ACMI arrangements; repeat programs can guarantee flying, while spot charters are transaction-based. Fuel and other direct costs may be borne by the customer under ACMI or included in a fixed charter price.

records.title: Irradiation Apparatus Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly supplying medical diagnostic and therapeutic, industrial, research, and scientific irradiation apparatus, tubes, components, upgrades, and associated configured systems to external healthcare, laboratory, industrial, OEM, and research customers. Capital-equipment, component, tube, upgrade, replacement, and configured-system revenue under purchase orders, tenders, OEM programs, service-linked installed-base relationships, and regulated product lifecycles.

records.title: Other Concrete Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market manufacturers of other concrete products that repeatedly provide external customers with engineered-to-order production, estimating, project coordination, scheduled delivery, installation support, inspection documentation, or similar accountable outsourced programs. Mostly project or purchase-order revenue from contractors, utilities, transportation agencies, developers, and industrial customers, with eligibility limited to repeat programs where engineering, coordination, logistics, installation support, or documentation is a substantive ongoing supplier responsibility.

records.title: Iron and Steel Forging
records.search_text: Independent US iron and steel open-die, impression-die, press, hammer, and seamless rolled-ring forging firms in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band repeatedly producing qualified forged components for external customers. Recurring engineered business-to-business programs priced per part, weight, or lot, with tooling, setup, heat treatment, testing, finishing, steel surcharges, purchase orders, releases, and multiyear qualification or supply agreements.

records.title: Nonferrous Metal (except Copper and Aluminum) Rolling, Drawing, and Extruding
records.search_text: Independent US toll, contract, and custom processors that repeatedly roll, draw, or extrude purchased nickel, titanium, zinc, lead, precious, refractory, or other nonferrous metals into customer-specified shapes and have transferable operations in the target band. Revenue is earned through conversion charges and per-pound or per-lot sales, commonly with metal-price pass-through, specifications, certifications, minimum lots, and repeat industrial purchase orders.

records.title: Footwear Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US footwear merchant wholesalers that repeatedly source, hold title to, market, sell, finance, and distribute footwear and related products to retailers and other business customers, with normalized EBITDA of roughly $1-10 million. Repeat B2B product sales earn the spread between merchandise acquisition and resale price, with value also supplied through assortment curation, availability, inventory risk, credit, order handling, and delivery.

records.title: Fabric Coating Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market mills repeatedly coating, laminating, varnishing, waxing, or rubberizing textile fabrics or apparel for external B2B customers under toll-coating, approved-supplier, or recurring production programs. Per-yard, per-roll, per-pound, per-lot, or program pricing for toll coating and conversion, plus product margins where mills purchase substrate or chemistry. Customers span industrial, automotive, defense, protective, outdoor, furnishings, and other technical-textile markets; approvals, performance specifications, recipes, and qualification histories support repeat orders but seldom subscription contracts.

records.title: Cookie and Cracker Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market manufacturers of cookies, crackers, ice cream cones, and similar products that repeatedly supply external supermarkets, discounters, warehouse clubs, food distributors, foodservice customers, private-label owners, or contract-manufacturing customers. Repeat wholesale product supply priced per unit, case, shipment, negotiated retail program, private-label contract, or co-manufacturing arrangement, often accompanied by discounts, rebates, volume incentives, displays, and other trade promotions.

records.title: Petroleum Bulk Stations and Terminals
records.search_text: Independent US lower-middle-market operators of bulk liquid storage stations or terminals that repeatedly receive, store, meter, document, sell, and transfer crude petroleum, refined petroleum products, biofuel blends, or liquefied petroleum gas to external customers. Recurring B2B wholesale distribution and throughput operations earning commodity spreads and, where applicable, storage, handling, throughput, blending, loading, or ancillary service fees; working capital and inventory values are large relative to labor.

records.title: Cosmetology and Barber Schools
records.search_text: US for-profit cosmetology, barbering, esthetics, nail-care, and closely related personal-appearance schools in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that repeatedly enroll external student cohorts and deliver state-recognized occupational training. Primarily cohort-based tuition and fees paid by students or funded through Title IV and other aid, with repeat intake cycles; some schools also earn clinic-service or product revenue. Tuition is generally set for a program or cohort rather than billed as labor cost-plus.

records.title: Other Waste Collection
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms providing local collection and hauling of waste other than nonhazardous solid waste and hazardous waste, including brush, rubble, and similar debris removal, where revenue comes from repeat outsourced service or a repeatable stream of jobs for external customers. Customers include households, property managers, contractors, businesses, and public entities. Revenue is generated through scheduled or on-call pickup, container or equipment rental, hauling, and charges influenced by service frequency, volume or weight, distance, and disposal cost; some demand is recurring while construction debris, storm cleanup, and household cleanouts are episodic.

records.title: Bed-and-Breakfast Inns
records.search_text: U.S. bed-and-breakfast inn operators providing short-term guest lodging, a full breakfast, personalized service, reservation handling, housekeeping, and related guest support to external travelers. Guests buy short stays, usually at a property-set room rate that includes breakfast; bookings arrive directly or through online travel agencies that use commission or net-rate models. Revenue repeats with room-night demand but is not generally contractual or subscription-based.

records.title: Steel Wire Drawing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers repeatedly drawing, annealing, coating, cleaning, spooling, inspecting, and selling steel wire made from purchased steel rod to external customers. Repeat product sales to reinforcing, prestressing, spring, fastener, cable, automotive, appliance, bedding, construction, industrial, and agricultural customers, priced by weight, diameter, grade, tensile properties, coating, packaging, processing, freight, and wire-rod market conditions.

records.title: Ammunition (except Small Arms) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market privately owned manufacturers and specialist contract producers that repeatedly make medium- or large-caliber ammunition, cartridge cases, grenades, mortar or artillery components, energetics, or load-assemble-pack deliverables for government customers and defense primes. Revenue comes from recurring lots, delivery orders, multiyear procurement, and program subcontracts for specified munitions or components. Pricing is commonly firm-fixed-price for production, with some cost-reimbursement or incentive arrangements for development and specialized work; qualification, security, testing, and traceability are integral to delivery.

records.title: Steel Foundries (except Investment)
records.search_text: Independent repeat-contract foundries producing carbon, low-alloy, and high-alloy steel castings by non-investment processes for external industrial, mining, rail, power, transportation, and defense customers. Revenue is earned from engineered castings priced by part, pound, lot, or program, often with separate pattern or tooling charges and optional heat treatment, finishing, testing, and machining. Repeat qualified programs and steel or energy surcharge mechanisms are common economic features.

records.title: Dried and Dehydrated Food Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market contract dryers, freeze-dryers, dehydrators, ingredient processors, blenders, and private-label producers supplying recurring drying, milling, blending, filling, packaging, labeling, and quality-assurance services for fruits, vegetables, soup mixes, bouillon, and related human-food ingredients. Food and beverage manufacturers, consumer brands, retailers, foodservice suppliers, and institutional customers purchase repeated batches or capacity under tolling, co-manufacturing, private-label, ingredient-supply, and packaging arrangements, priced by pound, batch, unit, or committed capacity.

records.title: Appliance Repair and Maintenance
records.search_text: Independent US firms repairing and servicing household appliances for external customers, including refrigerators, stoves, washing machines, clothes dryers, and room air conditioners. The screened population is limited to recurring or repeat outsourced-service firms in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Customers include households, landlords, property managers, warranty administrators, manufacturers, and retailers. Revenue comes from diagnostic or trip charges, hourly or flat-rate labor, parts markups, service contracts, and warranty reimbursement.

records.title: Mineral Wool Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly manufacture mineral wool and fiberglass insulation from rock, slag, glass, or combinations thereof for external residential, commercial, industrial, acoustic, fire-protection, and equipment customers. Repeat product sales through distributors, lumberyards, home centers, installers, contractors, retailers, and direct industrial channels, with revenue driven by volume, product mix, service, location, quality, building specifications, and negotiated selling prices.

records.title: Other Pressed and Blown Glass and Glassware Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly make pressed, blown, or otherwise shaped non-container glassware and glass components to industrial or commercial customer specifications, including laboratory, technical, lighting, appliance, and specialty applications. Repeat business-to-business orders for specified glassware or components, commonly priced by unit, batch, tooling program, or supply agreement, with revenue primarily from manufactured products.

records.title: Deep Sea Freight Transportation
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market vessel operators and ship-chartering businesses with crew that provide recurring deep-sea cargo transportation to or from foreign ports for external customers. Voyage charters, time charters, liner service contracts, tariffs, and spot bookings paid by shippers, charterers, or logistics intermediaries for international ocean carriage; repeated trade lanes and customer shipping programs create recurring demand despite volatile rates.

records.title: Breweries
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. production breweries with recurring external wholesale, self-distribution, private-label, contract-brewing, or alternating-proprietor relationships, including microbreweries and regional breweries with material off-site sales. Repeat beer production and supply to distributors, retailers, hospitality accounts, or brand owners through wholesale, self-distribution, contract-production, and shared-brewery arrangements, with some ancillary direct-to-consumer sales.

records.title: Knit Fabric Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market mills that repeatedly knit weft or warp fabric, make lace, or combine knitting with finishing or further nonapparel fabrication for external B2B customers under recurring programs or repeat purchase orders. Primarily per-pound, per-yard, per-roll, or per-program B2B sales and contract-manufacturing charges to apparel, industrial, medical, furnishings, and other downstream textile customers; specifications and repeat orders create recurring relationships but generally not long-duration subscriptions.

records.title: Boat Building
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of motorboats, fishing boats, pontoons, rowboats, canoes, kayaks, sailboats, yachts outside shipyards, heavy-duty inflatable boats, rigid inflatable boats, and boat-yard-built unmanned or robotic watercraft for external dealers, distributors, fleets, and end customers. Recurring or repeat outsourced revenue through dealer and distributor programs, repeat production orders, replacement models, fleet or club purchases, accessories attributable to the manufacturer, warranty, and manufacturer-provided support.

records.title: Automotive Glass Replacement Shops
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market automotive glass businesses providing repair, replacement, tinting, mobile service, and required camera or driver-assistance recalibration to consumers, fleets, and insurers through stand-alone shops or technician routes. Revenue is earned per repair, replacement, tint, or recalibration job, paid directly by vehicle owners, commercial fleets, or insurers. The model combines appointment and claims coordination, vehicle-specific glass sourcing, mobile routing or shop capacity, physical installation, cure and leak checks, and increasingly safety-system calibration and documentation.

records.title: Petroleum Lubricating Oil and Grease Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market formulators and blenders that repeatedly supply specialty lubricating oils, greases, and related technical support to external industrial, commercial-vehicle, and equipment customers. Repeat B2B product sales under customer specifications, purchase orders, distributor relationships, and some supply agreements; revenue is primarily per unit shipped, supported by formulation, testing, reliability, and technical service.

records.title: Prefabricated Wood Building Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US manufacturers of prefabricated stationary wood buildings, volumetric modules, panelized systems, and precut building packages that serve repeat builders, developers, public agencies, or commercial customers and have transferable standalone operations. Revenue is principally project, module, panel, component, or complete-building sales under bids and supply contracts; repeat business can come from builders and developers, but it is not usually subscription or recurring-service revenue.

records.title: Zoos and Botanical Gardens
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators whose recurring or repeat external-customer service is paid access, membership, educational programming, events, or related visitor services centered on live animal or plant collections. Primarily consumer admissions and memberships, supplemented by school and public programs, events, retail and food, donations, grants, and public support; prices are generally posted per visit or membership rather than cost-plus.

records.title: Barber Shops
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market barber-shop firms providing repeat haircutting, trimming, styling, shaving, beard care, and closely related grooming services directly to external customers, including multi-location and franchised operators when the screened firm operates the service locations. Customers usually purchase appointments or walk-in grooming services at posted per-service prices and return periodically; operators may also receive product sales and booth or chair rent. Revenue is transactional but highly repeat-oriented rather than contractually recurring.

records.title: Narrow Fabric Mills and Schiffli Machine Embroidery
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market mills repeatedly supplying external customers with woven or braided narrow fabrics, elastic narrow fabrics, labels, ribbons, tape, webbing, covered rubber thread, and Schiffli machine embroidery. Repeat B2B manufacture sold by specification, lot, length, production run, or contract order, often with customer-specific material, performance, design, or finishing requirements.

records.title: Scheduled Passenger Air Transportation
records.search_text: US certificated scheduled passenger airlines in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that repeatedly transport external passengers or operate scheduled regional capacity for airline customers. Passenger carriers earn primarily ticket and ancillary-fee revenue; regional carriers may instead receive recurring capacity-purchase payments from major airlines under long-term agreements.

records.title: Psychiatric and Substance Abuse Hospitals
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market licensed psychiatric and substance-abuse hospital operating firms repeatedly providing inpatient diagnostic, medical treatment, monitoring, nursing, psychiatric, psychological, social-work, and related services to external patients and payers through a transferable operation. Recurring admissions and patient days reimbursed by Medicare, Medicaid, commercial insurers, public purchasers, employers, and patients through prospective per-diem systems, negotiated daily or case rates, fee schedules, bundled arrangements, and limited cost-based or supplemental payments.

records.title: Industrial Valve Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market industrial-valve manufacturers repeatedly providing build-to-print, private-label, configured-to-order, or customer-engineered valve production programs to external industrial, water, energy, and infrastructure customers. Recurring engineered or contract production billed per valve or program, often with material surcharges, testing and documentation, drawings, pressure class, metallurgy, actuation, trim, inspection, and delivery requirements.

records.title: Commercial Air, Rail, and Water Transportation Equipment Rental and Leasing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operating firms that repeatedly rent or lease aircraft, aircraft engines, railcars, locomotives, ships, tugboats, barges, containers, or other off-highway commercial transportation equipment without operators to external customers, including associated asset management, contract administration, inspection, maintenance oversight, remarketing, and fleet services controlled by the lessor. Airlines, railroads, shippers, industrial customers, and transport operators enter operating, finance, net, or full-service leases that generate contracted rent and sometimes maintenance reserves, usage charges, insurance or tax recovery, management fees, transition services, and asset-sale gains; lessors bear capital, funding, residual-value, credit, remarketing, compliance, and varying maintenance obligations.

records.title: Flight Training
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market flight-training firms primarily delivering career-oriented initial pilot certificates, instrument and commercial ratings, instructor training, advanced ratings, and related recurrent ground, simulator, and aircraft instruction to external students or airline and institutional customers under Part 61 or Part 141. Students, employers, airlines, and institutional partners pay by flight or instructor hour, course block, fixed career-program tuition, or training contract. Revenue recurs through new cohorts, sequential certificates and ratings, proficiency work, and recurrent training, while aircraft rental, fuel, maintenance, simulators, and instructor time are major delivery inputs.

records.title: RV (Recreational Vehicle) Parks and Campgrounds
records.search_text: Private operating companies that run RV parks and campgrounds for external guests, including short-stay and seasonal campsite accommodation, reservations, guest service, site utilities, washrooms, laundry, grounds, recreation amenities, camp stores, and related add-ons. Direct-to-consumer accommodation sold by night, week, month, or season, with repeat visits and ancillary fees for site selection, amenities, rentals, retail, and activities; some parks also have longer-duration annual or membership-like arrangements.

records.title: Offices of Podiatrists
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market independent podiatry practices providing recurring or repeat diagnosis and treatment of foot, ankle, and lower-leg conditions to external patients, including examinations, imaging review, diabetic foot care, wound care, routine procedures, surgery, orthotics-related clinical work, and follow-up care in offices or facilities of others. Practices receive fee-for-service, contracted payer, Medicare or Medicaid, and patient cash payments for visits, diagnostics, procedures, surgery, supplies, and follow-up care; chronic disease and post-procedure cases create repeat utilization while reimbursement is commonly tied to coded clinical services.

records.title: Other Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Parts and Auxiliary Equipment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent LMM manufacturers and prototype-to-production suppliers of guided-missile and space-vehicle parts, subassemblies, and auxiliary equipment other than propulsion, sold repeatedly to external primes, government programs, and commercial-space customers. Recurring development, qualified production-lot, spares, and sustainment revenue with engineering, configuration control, parts assurance, inspection, traceability, and mission-support obligations.

records.title: Packing and Crating
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms that repeatedly provide outsourced packing, custom or standard crating, protective preparation, marking, weighing, documentation, and related preparation of customer goods for transportation. Customers include manufacturers, distributors, government agencies, freight forwarders, movers, exporters, art and equipment owners, and other shippers. Revenue is commonly job-, unit-, material-, labor-, or requirements-contract based, including fixed-price work, scheduled repeat services, call-off orders, and customer-specific recurring arrangements.

records.title: Rope, Cordage, Twine, Tire Cord, and Tire Fabric Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers selling rope, cordage, twine, tire cord, tire fabric, and closely related reinforcing products repeatedly to external commercial, industrial, transportation, marine, agricultural, or distribution customers. Predominantly repeat B2B product sales under purchase orders, distributor relationships, specifications, and periodic supply arrangements; revenue is tied to physical volume, mix, and negotiated unit prices rather than hourly billing.

records.title: Carpet and Upholstery Cleaning Services
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing outsourced cleaning, spotting, dyeing, maintenance, and related care for used carpets, rugs, and upholstery at residential, commercial, institutional, or plant locations. External households and organizations buy on-site or plant-based cleaning through per-room, per-square-foot, hourly, quoted-project, or scheduled maintenance pricing. Residential demand is commonly episodic but repeatable; commercial and institutional accounts can be scheduled under recurring maintenance plans or service agreements.

records.title: All Other Basic Organic Chemical Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market toll, custom, and specification-driven producers that repeatedly synthesize, ferment, purify, recover, or modify basic organic chemicals for external industrial customers. Industrial customers buy recurring batches, campaigns, or contracted volumes under defined specifications, with revenue based on conversion fees, per-unit pricing, or formulas that may adjust for feedstock and energy.

records.title: Poultry Processing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market poultry slaughter and processing firms that repeatedly provide inspected, custom, toll, co-processing, cutting, packaging, or value-added processing services to external farms, brands, cooperatives, or food companies. Recurring or repeat processing for external poultry owners and brands, billed per bird, per pound, per batch, or under negotiated toll/co-processing agreements, sometimes with packaging and specialty-process add-ons.

records.title: Synthetic Dye and Pigment Manufacturing
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly supply synthetic organic or inorganic dyes, pigments, lakes, and toners to external coatings, plastics, inks, textiles, paper, and other industrial or specialty customers. Repeat B2B product sales under customer specifications, purchase orders, distributor relationships, qualification cycles, and some supply arrangements; price per unit is supported by color performance, consistency, technical service, and reliable availability.

records.title: Lessors of Nonfinancial Intangible Assets (except Copyrighted Works)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operating licensors that repeatedly assign external rights to patents, trademarks, brand names, and franchise agreements in exchange for royalties or license fees and maintain transferable licensing, administration, compliance, audit, enforcement, or franchise-support operations. Licensees and franchisees pay fixed fees, minimum guarantees, upfront fees, per-unit or usage fees, and recurring royalties often tied to licensee product sales or franchisee system sales; operating licensors may also receive audit recoveries, renewal or territory fees, and related support or technology fees.

records.title: Biological Product (except Diagnostic) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contract biologics manufacturers and development-and-manufacturing organizations providing repeat process development, scale-up, GMP batch manufacturing, fill-finish, testing, and related outsourced production services to external biopharma customers. Biotechnology and pharmaceutical sponsors buy technical-transfer and development projects, GMP batches, reserved capacity, testing, and commercial supply under statements of work, purchase orders, and sometimes multi-year supply arrangements; repeat revenue depends on program continuation and regulatory approval.

records.title: Environment, Conservation and Wildlife Organizations
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market organizations classified in 813312 that repeatedly provide environmental advocacy, conservation and habitat programs, wildlife protection, research and monitoring, education, restoration or stewardship coordination, and related services to external constituencies or funding partners. Revenue is commonly a mix of contributions, memberships, foundation and government grants, restricted project awards, sponsorships, and endowment support, with some recurring stewardship contracts, research or monitoring agreements, admission, education, licensing, events, or program-service fees. Funders, contracting agencies, landowners, members, and beneficiaries may be different parties.

records.title: Flavoring Syrup and Concentrate Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly develop, blend, quality-control, pack, and supply custom or private-label flavoring syrups, beverage concentrates, fountain bases, and related drink compounds for external beverage brands, foodservice operators, retailers, distributors, and bottlers. Repeat B2B formulation and production programs priced per gallon, pound, bag-in-box, case, batch, or contract run, often combining product development, ingredient sourcing, blending, regulatory and label support, quality assurance, packaging, and palletized delivery.

records.title: Theater Companies and Dinner Theaters
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market theater and dinner-theater companies that repeatedly supply live theatrical productions or integrated dinner-and-show experiences to external audiences, presenters, venues, institutions, or event buyers, with transferable operations, rights, customer channels and production capability. Revenue is commonly earned from single tickets, subscriptions, contracted performances, venue or presenter guarantees, education and community programs, food and beverage at dinner theaters, sponsorship, donations and grants. The qualifying lens emphasizes repeat paid productions and transferable customer relationships rather than temporary show-level investment vehicles.

records.title: Retail Bakeries
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market retail bakeries that make bread and other bakery products on premises and have a meaningful repeat external-customer component, including recurring wholesale, hospitality, institutional, office, event-planner, or standing custom-order accounts. Product revenue from baked goods made on premises, with eligible firms deriving a meaningful share from repeat standing orders or recurring account customers; pricing is generally per item, batch, or order rather than cost-plus labor billing.

records.title: Books Printing
records.search_text: US book printers in the lower-middle-market band producing hardcover, paperback, educational, religious, professional, and other bound books for external publishers and institutional customers, including associated prepress, binding, finishing, and fulfillment performed with the print job. Repeat business-to-business job and contract revenue from publishers and institutions, generally quoted per run or per unit with paper, setup, production, binding, and finishing embedded in the price.

records.title: Other Snack Food Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market contract manufacturers, co-manufacturers, and private-label producers of potato chips, corn chips, popped popcorn, pretzels, and similar snack foods that repeatedly manufacture and package products for external brand owners, retailers, or foodservice customers. Repeat B2B production runs priced per unit, case, pound, or run under co-manufacturing, co-packing, and private-label agreements, often with additional product-development, sourcing, packaging, and logistics services.

records.title: Other Fabricated Wire Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly form, weave, strand, weld, or assemble purchased wire into customer-designed wire forms, mesh, cloth, baskets, racks, cable, fencing components, or related products for external customers. OEM, industrial, construction, and distribution customers buy repeat runs under drawings, dimensions, material, coating, strength, packaging, and delivery specifications through purchase orders or supply agreements.

records.title: Coal and Other Mineral and Ore Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: US merchant wholesalers that repeatedly take title to and supply coal, coke, metal ores, ore concentrates, and nonconstruction nonmetallic minerals to external utilities, steelmakers, processors, exporters, and industrial customers. Commodity resale spreads and logistics/service margin on physical bulk materials, under a mixture of spot, indexed, bid, fixed-price, and multi-year supply arrangements, sometimes including blending, quality assurance, storage, terminal, transportation, and working-capital support.

records.title: Convention and Visitors Bureaus
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators repeatedly providing destination marketing, convention and meeting sales support, visitor information, itinerary content, local-partner promotion, and related destination-management services to external municipalities, tourism districts, lodging stakeholders, event organizers, businesses, and travelers. The economic customers and funders are commonly municipalities, tourism improvement districts, lodging and hospitality members, and other destination stakeholders, while travelers and meeting planners receive many services without a direct charge. Revenue commonly comes from dedicated occupancy or sales taxes, district assessments, government service contracts, memberships, sponsorships, advertising, and grants rather than transaction fees from visitors.

records.title: Land Subdivision
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly providing fee-based or contracted land-subdivision and development execution for external landowners, builders, or institutional clients, including entitlement coordination, development management, budgeting, subcontractor procurement, and oversight of roads, grading, drainage, and utility servicing. Development-management fees, fixed or milestone fees, reimbursed project costs, and contracted execution revenue from repeat external landowners and builders; qualifying firms coordinate approvals and physical servicing while often subcontracting field work.

records.title: Broadwoven Fabric Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market mills that repeatedly weave broadwoven fabrics or felts for external business customers, including mills that also finish or further fabricate their woven output. Repeat B2B sale of fabric by order, specification, yardage, or production run, with some contract/jobber arrangements and customer-specific finishing or fabrication.

records.title: Motor Vehicle Brake System Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers of motor-vehicle brake systems and components, including calipers, rotors, drums, friction assemblies, hydraulic and electromechanical brake hardware, and related tested subassemblies. Repeat B2B supply to vehicle OEMs, tier-one integrators, distributors, fleets, and aftermarket channels, limited to externally serving firms plausibly in the $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band.

records.title: Nonferrous Forging
records.search_text: Independent US aluminum, titanium, nickel, copper, magnesium, and other nonferrous forging firms in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band repeatedly producing qualified forged components for external customers. Recurring engineered business-to-business programs priced per part, weight, or lot with tooling, setup, heat treatment, testing, machining or finishing, alloy surcharges, purchase orders, releases, and multiyear qualification or supply agreements.

records.title: Motion Picture Theaters (except Drive-Ins)
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators of indoor motion-picture theaters that repeatedly sell admissions and related concessions, food and beverage, advertising, loyalty, rental, and event services to external customers. Consumers pay per admission or through memberships and also buy concessions and premium experiences; operators may earn auditorium-rental, advertising, ticket-fee, loyalty, gift-card, merchandise, and alternate-content revenue. Film rental is commonly linked to admissions revenue, while venue labor, occupancy, utilities, and most technology costs remain with the exhibitor.

records.title: Dry Pasta, Dough, and Flour Mixes Manufacturing from Purchased Flour
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market manufacturers in NAICS 311824 that repeatedly supply dry pasta, prepared flour mixes, or refrigerated or frozen dough to external retail, foodservice, institutional, or food-manufacturer customers, including private-label and customer-specific production. Recurring product sales under private-label, institutional, ingredient, foodservice, or customer-specific arrangements; revenue is typically per unit or shipment, with some contracts adjusting prices for input costs.

records.title: Other Farm Product Raw Material Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market merchants of storable, nonliving farm raw materials such as raw cotton, hemp and other fibers, hides and pelts, leaf tobacco, raw nuts, oil kernels, raw spices, and raw sugar, where recurring sourcing, aggregation, grading, market access, documentation, and logistics are provided to external counterparties by a transferable team. Merchants generally buy raw materials on their own account from farms or assemblers and resell to processors, manufacturers, exporters, or other wholesalers. The recurring service embedded in the spread is aggregation, quality matching, inventory and price-risk management, documentation, and delivery coordination rather than a separately billed service fee.

records.title: Tobacco Product and Electronic Cigarette Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Materially compliant independent merchant wholesalers repeatedly sourcing, holding, selling, delivering, and documenting authorized cigarettes, cigars, smokeless tobacco, nicotine pouches, electronic cigarettes, and related products for external convenience, tobacco-specialty, grocery, fuel, hospitality, and other licensed retail customers. High-frequency B2B product sales earning thin wholesale spreads and manufacturer promotional allowances, with value added through assortment, credit, tax and regulatory compliance, inventory availability, route delivery, retailer service, and transaction records.

records.title: Industrial Sand Mining
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent operators that mine, dredge, wash, dry, screen, size, blend, and repeatedly sell industrial-grade sand to external oilfield, glass, foundry, filtration, building-products, chemical, ceramic, recreational, and other industrial customers. Repeat business-to-business product sales by ton and specification through spot, short-term, or take-or-pay contracts, often combined with rail, transload, storage, or delivery arrangements; price and volume depend on sand quality, customer qualification, proximity, logistics, end-market cycles, and competing capacity.

records.title: Hydroelectric Power Generation
records.search_text: U.S. private lower-middle-market owners and operators of operating conventional or pumped-storage hydroelectric generation facilities that convert water power into electricity and provide it to transmission or distribution systems. The lens includes plant operations, water and generation scheduling, dam and equipment monitoring, maintenance, compliance, and commercial management attached to those facilities. Operators sell electricity and renewable attributes to utilities, transmission or distribution systems, or wholesale markets through long-term power-purchase agreements, regulated recovery, bilateral contracts, or merchant energy sales. Reservoir and pumped-storage assets may also earn capacity and ancillary-service revenue.
