records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers repeatedly producing or texturizing artificial and synthetic fibers or filaments to external-customer specifications under contract, tolling, or dedicated-program arrangements. Recurring industrial programs priced per pound, lot, or contracted capacity, with customer specifications for polymer, denier, color, finish, tow, staple, yarn, or filament performance and periodic raw-material or energy repricing.

records.title: Casinos (except Casino Hotels)
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly manage and staff a regulated stand-alone, floating, riverboat, or racetrack casino for an external property, tribal, or governmental license holder. The qualifying customer is an external casino property or license holder paying a recurring management or operating fee; the casino itself earns gross gaming revenue from repeat patron wagering and may also earn food-and-beverage revenue.

records.title: Petrochemical Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market petrochemical processors that repeatedly provide toll, contract, or customer-specific production of acyclic or aromatic hydrocarbons to external industrial customers. Repeat B2B sales and contract-processing revenue under term supply agreements, formula or index-adjusted pricing, and customer specifications; customers are downstream chemical, resin, materials, and industrial manufacturers.

records.title: Consumer Electronics and Appliances Rental
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms renting consumer electronics and household appliances to external customers, including recurring rental and lease-to-own operators using stores, e-commerce, or retail-partner channels. Operators own or acquire durable merchandise and earn repeat weekly, biweekly, semimonthly, or monthly rental payments; customers may return merchandise or obtain ownership through renewal or early-purchase options. Revenue depends on approvals, collections, merchandise utilization and loss control, delivery, installation, repair, and resale or rerental.

records.title: Reupholstery and Furniture Repair
records.search_text: Independent US firms providing reupholstery, refinishing, repair, and restoration of furniture for external household and commercial customers. The screened population is limited to recurring or repeat outsourced-service firms in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Customers include households, interior designers, hospitality and commercial facilities, furniture dealers, and institutional owners. Revenue comes mainly from custom job quotes covering skilled labor, fabric and other materials, refinishing, frame repair, pickup and delivery, and sometimes repeat commercial refurbishment programs.

records.title: Beet Sugar Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US beet-sugar processors in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that repeatedly provide toll refining, contract processing, or a comparable outsourced manufacturing service to external customers. Eligible revenue would arise from repeated fees or conversion margins for processing customer-owned or customer-directed beet inputs into refined sugar and by-products; ordinary sales of processor-owned sugar are not eligible service revenue.

records.title: Metal Can Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent manufacturers supplying metal cans, lids, and ends on recurring or repeat contracts to external food, beverage, pet-food, aerosol, and general-line customers. High-volume cans and ends are supplied repeatedly under annual or multi-year agreements, frequently from plants near customer filling operations; specialty and general-line cans may use shorter quoted runs, but revenue still depends on recurring packaging demand and customer qualification.

records.title: Pipeline Transportation of Refined Petroleum Products
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that transport refined petroleum products by pipeline for external refiners, marketers, airports, terminals, distributors, or other shippers on a recurring basis. Recurring tariffed or contracted transportation and related terminal-interface services, generally priced by barrel, distance, product, batch, or committed capacity, with interstate rates commonly subject to FERC ceilings and intrastate service subject to state or contractual regimes.

records.title: Printing and Writing Paper Merchant Wholesalers
records.search_text: Independent US merchant wholesalers in the EBITDA band that repeatedly source, stock, sell, and coordinate delivery of bulk printing or writing paper to printers, converters, publishers, and other external business customers. Business customers purchase bulk paper repeatedly, generally by roll or other production quantity. The outsourced service combines sourcing, credit, inventory availability, specification matching, order coordination, and delivery, with revenue captured mainly in product gross margin.

records.title: Home Centers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market home-center firms that repeatedly serve external homeowners, contractors, property managers, and institutions through broad-line building-material retail plus material advice, quoting, account service, project fulfillment, delivery, rental, returns, and installation coordination. Predominantly transaction-based product revenue with repeat contractor and homeowner demand; services such as delivery, rental, project quoting, credit, and installation coordination support merchandise sales and may be charged directly or embedded in product margin.

records.title: Gypsum Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of gypsum wallboard, plaster, molding, statuary, architectural plasterwork, and other gypsum products that make repeat sales to external distributors, contractors, builders, or industrial customers, including recurring specification, custom-production, inventory, and delivery work embedded in those relationships. Revenue is principally product sales to building-material dealers, specialty distributors, lumber yards, home-center chains, contractors, and other construction customers. Wallboard is commonly priced on a delivered basis and shipped locally by truck; eligible specialty producers may also earn repeat revenue through custom fabrication, specification support, scheduled replenishment, and job-linked delivery.

records.title: Chocolate and Confectionery Manufacturing from Cacao Beans
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that shell, roast, or grind cacao beans and make chocolate, cacao products, or chocolate confectionery for repeat wholesale, private-label, foodservice, or retail-channel customers. Repeat product sales under wholesale, distributor, private-label, foodservice, and retailer relationships; revenue is primarily per unit or shipment rather than a cost-plus service fee.

records.title: Shellfish Fishing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operating firms that control commercial vessels, fishing permits, crews, and recurring wild-capture shellfish harvesting for sales to external dealers, processors, auctions, or other wholesale buyers. Operators earn predominantly variable ex-vessel revenue from repeated landings of crab, lobster, shrimp, scallops, and other wild shellfish; dealers or auctions typically pay by landed weight, species, grade, and prevailing dockside price rather than under a recurring service fee.

records.title: Apparel Accessories and Other Apparel Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly provide OEM, private-label, custom, uniform, corporate, promotional, or program-based production of hats, caps, belts, neckties, non-medical/non-safety/non-sport gloves, and other apparel accessories to external business or institutional customers. Repeat business-to-business custom or private-label manufacturing, typically quoted by design, quantity tier, and unit, with sampling, decoration, labels, packaging, quality control, and recurring reorders or replenishment programs.

records.title: Synthetic Rubber Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market synthetic-rubber manufacturers that repeatedly produce customer-specified elastomers or latex under contract or toll-manufacturing relationships for external customers. Recurring batch or continuous production for industrial customers, generally priced per pound or shipment under specifications and purchase agreements, with feedstock and energy clauses negotiated contractually.

records.title: Grantmaking Foundations
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market grantmaking foundations and charitable trusts that repeatedly solicit, evaluate, award, administer, and monitor grants for external charitable beneficiaries and have institutional operations separable from a single donor or manager. Foundations deploy endowed, donated, or trust assets through grants rather than selling services to grantees. Operating resources come from investment income, contributions, and endowment draw, while grants and administration are governed by charitable purpose, payout requirements, boards, donors, and tax rules.

records.title: Offices of Real Estate Agents and Brokers
records.search_text: United States lower-middle-market real estate brokerages and offices of licensed agents that repeatedly represent external buyers, sellers, landlords, or tenants in residential or commercial real estate transactions. Customers engage the firm through listing, buyer-representation, sales, or leasing agreements. Revenue is principally transaction-linked brokerage commissions or fees, with some recurring franchise, agent, referral, or ancillary-service economics at the firm level.

records.title: Cyclic Crude, Intermediate, and Gum and Wood Chemical Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market producers that repeatedly provide toll, custom, or specification-driven distillation, fractionation, synthesis, recovery, or modification of cyclic intermediates and gum or wood chemicals for external industrial customers. Industrial customers purchase recurring batches, campaigns, or volumes under product specifications, with revenue based on conversion fees, unit prices, or formulas that can pass through feedstock and energy costs.

records.title: Timber Tract Operations
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operators managing timber tracts to grow and sell standing timber, including inventory, silvicultural planning, road and access coordination, reforestation, fire and pest management, harvest-sale administration, and landowner reporting. Repeated timber-sale and tract-management activity serving external mills, loggers, landowners, or timber buyers, screened for transferable operating firms plausibly generating $1-10 million normalized EBITDA.

records.title: Pipeline Transportation of Natural Gas
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators transporting natural gas by pipeline, including transmission-network operation and storage performed as part of the pipeline network, for external shippers or customers. Recurring capacity-reservation and commodity-transport services sold to producers, utilities, marketers, industrial users, and export-linked shippers under regulated tariffs or negotiated contracts; revenue is tied to reserved capacity, throughput, storage, and ancillary services.

records.title: Copper Rolling, Drawing, Extruding, and Alloying
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market firms that alloy, roll, draw, or extrude copper and copper alloys into tube, rod, bar, sheet, strip, foil, wire, or custom shapes, emphasizing toll conversion, specialty alloys, custom profiles, difficult short runs, and repeat specification programs. Customers include plumbing and HVAC suppliers, electrical-equipment and data-center infrastructure manufacturers, utilities, transportation and aerospace suppliers, industrial OEMs, distributors, and other mills. Revenue generally combines benchmark-linked copper cost with a conversion or fabrication margin; repeat custom drawings, alloy specifications, tooling, qualification, stocking, and just-in-time releases create recurring economics, with a smaller toll-processing subset handling customer material.

records.title: Rice Milling
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent rice millers that repeatedly mill customer-owned paddy or manufacture and package rice to external customers' specifications, including toll and private-label production where physical milling is a material part of the service. Customers are food brands, retailers, foodservice operators, industrial ingredient users, and growers that pay per-volume milling, processing, packaging, or negotiated private-label supply prices under repeat orders or supply arrangements.

records.title: Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. direct carriers repeatedly underwriting health and medical insurance risk for external individuals, employers, associations, and government-program members, including risk-bearing group and individual plans, Medicare and Medicaid products, supplemental coverage, dental and vision coverage, and HMOs that provide insurance without directly providing health care. Individuals, employers, associations, and government programs pay recurring premiums or capitation for the carrier to assume medical-cost risk, maintain networks and benefits, enroll and serve members, adjudicate claims, manage utilization, meet regulatory obligations, and pay covered providers. Premium and reimbursement rates are renewed or reset periodically, medical claims consume most revenue, and some products operate under federal or state program rules and risk adjustment.

records.title: Local Messengers and Local Delivery
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market employer firms providing recurring or repeat outsourced local courier, scheduled route, same-day, medical, legal, retail, grocery, meal, and small-parcel delivery services to external customers, where commercial accounts, dispatch capability, and operations can transfer with control. Per-delivery, per-stop, route-day, mileage, subscription, or negotiated account fees paid by merchants, institutions, platforms, or end consumers; repeat revenue ranges from contracted dedicated routes to on-demand orders.

records.title: Electric Power Distribution
records.search_text: Privately controlled lower-middle-market operators of physical electric distribution systems that repeatedly deliver electricity and related distribution service to external customers. Recurring regulated or contract-based charges to residential, commercial, and industrial customers for electricity delivery and associated distribution service, commonly recovered through approved tariffs and usage-linked charges.

records.title: Glass Container Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of glass packaging, bottling, and canning containers that repeatedly supply external beverage, food, pharmaceutical, cosmetic, or other packaging customers. Repeat business-to-business container sales under volume programs, forecasts, purchase orders, or supply contracts, priced primarily by unit and specification with energy, freight, and raw-material economics influencing negotiations.

records.title: Bottled Water Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US bottled-water plants in NAICS 312112 that repeatedly purify, bottle, label, package, or prepare water for external brands, retailers, exchange programs, home-and-office-delivery systems, hospitality customers, or other beverage companies, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Recurring B2B co-packing, private-label, and independent bottler programs priced per case, bottle, gallon, or production run; agreements may include minimum volumes, take-or-pay commitments, packaging and resin indexation, customer-supplied materials, quality specifications, and periodic repricing.

records.title: Fossil Fuel Electric Power Generation
records.search_text: Independent LMM operators of coal-, petroleum-, or natural-gas-fired generation facilities that repeatedly sell electricity, capacity, ancillary services, or contracted output to external utilities, markets, cooperatives, or large customers. Recurring wholesale market, power-purchase agreement, capacity, tolling, ancillary-service, or regulated generation revenue from dispatchable physical plants.

records.title: Community Food Services
records.search_text: U.S. operators classified in community food services that repeatedly collect, prepare, distribute, or deliver food to external recipients and that can plausibly support lower-middle-market normalized operating earnings. Recipients often pay little or nothing; operating resources commonly come from contributions, donated food, grants, member-network support, public programs, or service contracts. The narrower investable subset may have recurring fixed-fee or reimbursement arrangements with governments, health plans, institutions, or sponsors.

records.title: Emergency and Other Relief Services
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market firms supplying recurring or repeat outsourced disaster relief, emergency food or shelter, material-aid logistics, survivor counseling, case management, or refugee and immigrant resettlement services to governments, institutions, nonprofit prime contractors, or affected populations. Revenue commonly comes from government grants, cooperative agreements, cost-reimbursement programs, competitively or urgently awarded service contracts, nonprofit subcontracts, donations, and restricted program funding. Delivery spans preparedness and grant administration, emergency deployment, shelter and commodity operations, case management, resettlement, and long-tail recovery support.

records.title: Malt Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent malt houses manufacturing barley, rye, or other-grain malt for repeat sale to external breweries, distillers, and food customers. Recurring business-to-business sales of specification-grade malt, typically by shipment or supply agreement, with revenue tied to physical volume and negotiated product specifications.

records.title: Credit Unions
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market credit unions providing recurring deposit, payment, consumer-lending, mortgage, member-service, and related financial services to eligible external members. Members place insured share deposits and use loans, payments, and account services; the credit union earns interest spread and fees to cover operating costs, losses, and required net worth, while residual economic value belongs to members through retained capital, dividends, rates, fees, and services.

records.title: Soybean and Other Oilseed Processing
records.search_text: Independent merchant processors that crush soybeans, cottonseed, canola, sunflower seed, peanuts, flaxseed, or other oilseeds and tree nuts into oils, meal, hulls, cakes, protein concentrates, and related co-products for repeat sale to external feed, food, biofuel, and industrial customers. Recurring B2B sales of commodity or specification-based meal, oil, hulls, protein, and co-products, with economics driven by throughput, local origination and logistics, crush spreads, quality, and product mix.

records.title: Commuter Rail Systems
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market private firms that repeatedly operate scheduled commuter rail service for external public transit authorities or other system owners under operations, maintenance, or integrated operating contracts. The screened operator primarily earns recurring contract payments from a public authority or system owner under a multiyear, competitively procured operating arrangement; passenger fares and public subsidy generally accrue to or are controlled by the authority rather than functioning as unconstrained retail pricing for the contractor.

records.title: Secondary Smelting and Alloying of Aluminum
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market operators that repeatedly melt, refine, blend, and alloy customer-owned aluminum scrap, dross, or purchased secondary feedstock into specified billet, ingot, sow, or molten-metal products, including toll processors and regional secondary smelters. Customers are automotive, casting, rolling, extrusion, packaging, and other aluminum users. Revenue is earned through per-pound or per-ton toll conversion charges when the customer retains metal ownership, or through the spread between purchased scrap and sold specification alloy in buy/sell operations; energy and metal-price adjustments are often passed through contractually.

records.title: Asphalt Shingle and Coating Materials Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of asphalt shingles, roll roofing, saturated felts, roof and waterproofing coatings, and related asphalt materials that serve recurring external distributors, contractors, private-label customers, or genuine contract-manufacturing accounts. Primarily per-unit, bundle, roll, gallon, or truckload product sales through distributors and contractor channels; qualifying outsourced-service revenue is limited to private-label or contract manufacturing with repeat external customers.

records.title: Office Supplies (except Paper) Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of nonpaper office, writing, marking, and art supplies that also provide recurring or repeat outsourced customer work such as private-label production, contract manufacturing, custom marking programs, or replenishment-linked fulfillment. Eligible firms principally earn repeat B2B revenue from production runs, customization, packaging, replenishment, and related fulfillment for brands, schools, offices, dealers, and institutional buyers; product units remain the economic basis even when the relationship is recurring.

records.title: Ethyl Alcohol Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market plants that repeatedly provide contract fermentation, distillation, purification, denaturing, recovery, or byproduct-processing capacity to external customers under customer specifications. Customers purchase contracted processing campaigns or recurring volumes, with revenue based on throughput, finished gallons, conversion fees, or product prices that may include feedstock and energy adjustments.

records.title: Carpet and Rug Mills
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market carpet and rug mills providing repeat commission coating, finishing, printing, or contract/custom production to external manufacturers, dealers, hospitality groups, institutions, and other commercial customers. Business customers place repeat purchase orders or production runs priced per yard, unit, lot, or specification; the qualifying subset earns manufacturing-service revenue through commission finishing/printing or recurring contract production rather than a subscription.

records.title: Aluminum Sheet, Plate, and Foil Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers that hot-roll or cold-roll aluminum into sheet, plate, strip, or foil, with emphasis on specialty short runs, customer-qualified products, and toll or conversion work using customer-supplied metal. Customers include aerospace and defense fabricators, beverage and flexible-packaging converters, automotive suppliers, industrial OEMs, and distributors. Most revenue is product sales priced as metal cost plus conversion margin; a narrower recurring-service subset earns toll rolling or finishing fees on customer-owned material and repeat specification orders.

records.title: Air Traffic Control
records.search_text: US private operators providing recurring air traffic control tower staffing and associated operational support to the FAA or airport sponsors, with emphasis on businesses plausibly in the $1-10M normalized EBITDA band. The core customer is the FAA under multi-area, multi-year service contracts; some airport sponsors share costs. Revenue is predominantly contracted tower staffing and operating support, with approved staffing plans and service-level oversight.

records.title: Electric Bulk Power Transmission and Control
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms operating high-voltage electric transmission lines, transformer stations, control centers, or related bulk-power control systems and providing recurring transmission or control service to generators, distribution utilities, load-serving entities, market participants, or other external customers. Recurring regulated or contracted transmission and control revenue through FERC-approved tariffs and formula rates, state-regulated arrangements where applicable, network or point-to-point service, interconnection and congestion-related services, or long-term contracts; revenue commonly recovers operating costs, depreciation, taxes, and an allowed return on invested capital.

records.title: Reconstituted Wood Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of reconstituted wood sheets and boards that repeatedly provide custom, toll, specification-controlled, or otherwise service-like production to external customers. Eligible firms earn recurring B2B revenue by manufacturing particleboard, MDF, fiberboard, OSB, or related panels to customer specifications; the broader code predominantly earns point-in-time product revenue through purchase orders.

records.title: Confectionery and Nut Retailers
records.search_text: US confectionery and nut retailers in the lower-middle-market EBITDA band that provide recurring or repeat outsourced supply, curation, gifting, replenishment, or fulfillment to external business, institutional, hospitality, event, or subscription customers. Eligible firms earn fixed-price product and program revenue from repeat orders, standing accounts, subscriptions, or scheduled gifting and replenishment, with sourcing, assortment, packaging, fulfillment, and account service embedded in product margins.

records.title: Breakfast Cereal Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US breakfast-cereal co-manufacturers and private-label manufacturers repeatedly producing cereal for external brand and retail customers, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Recurring batch production, formulation, packaging, and private-label supply for food brands and retailers, generally priced per case or production run under fixed, indexed, or periodically renewed commercial terms.

records.title: Drive-In Motion Picture Theaters
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operators of drive-in motion-picture theaters that repeatedly sell vehicle or individual admission, concessions, rentals, and related outdoor entertainment services to external customers. Consumers generally buy admission to an outdoor, vehicle-oriented screening experience and may also buy concessions, rentals, advertising, merchandise, or special-event access. The operator controls the site experience and local pricing while paying film-rental, labor, utilities, insurance, technology, maintenance, and often land or lease costs.

records.title: Alumina Refining and Primary Aluminum Production
records.search_text: Independent U.S. businesses repeatedly refining alumina from bauxite or producing primary aluminum and aluminum alloys from alumina for sale to external customers, including integrated primary forming where it is part of the same operating business. High-volume sales of alumina, primary aluminum, billet, slab, ingot, and related alloy forms to smelters, rolling mills, extruders, foundries, fabricators, and industrial customers, priced from commodity benchmarks, regional and product premiums, alloy and shape specifications, energy economics, freight, tariffs, and contractual terms.

records.title: Nitrogenous Fertilizer Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing or formulating nitrogenous fertilizer for external customers under toll, private-label, waste-conversion, or customer-specific supply programs. Recurring industrial or agricultural supply programs priced per ton, batch, or contracted capacity, usually with natural-gas, ammonia, freight, or nutrient-market repricing and customer specifications for nutrient content and physical form.

records.title: Soft Drink Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US beverage co-packers and contract bottlers in NAICS 312111 that repeatedly blend, carbonate, fill, label, package, or warehouse soft drinks for external beverage brands, retailers, or other beverage companies, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Recurring B2B production programs and purchase orders priced per case, unit, batch, or run; contracts can combine conversion margin with direct ingredient and packaging costs, volume tiers, minimum quantities, equipment support, and negotiated cost pass-throughs.

records.title: Natural Gas Distribution
records.search_text: Privately controlled lower-middle-market operators of physical local natural-gas distribution systems that repeatedly deliver gas and related distribution service to external residential, commercial, and industrial customers. Recurring regulated or contract-based delivery charges, commonly paired with pass-through recovery of purchased gas, for operating mains, meters, pressure controls, service lines, and customer connections.

records.title: Curtain and Linen Mills
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market U.S. curtain and linen mills providing repeat private-label, contract cut-and-sew, custom institutional, or made-to-specification production to external retailers, hospitality operators, healthcare customers, designers, and distributors. Customers place recurring purchase orders or production programs for sheets, towels, curtains, draperies, table linens, shower curtains, and related household textiles, priced per unit, lot, style, or contract specification rather than by subscription.

records.title: Baked Goods Retailers
records.search_text: US baked-goods retailers selling products made off premises through recurring or repeat supply relationships, such as standing business, institutional, hospitality, or event accounts, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. The eligible model earns predominantly fixed-price product revenue from repeated orders or scheduled deliveries to external customers, with merchandising, ordering, fulfillment, and account service embedded in the product margin.

records.title: Video Tape and Disc Rental
records.search_text: US businesses whose recurring external-customer service is renting physical prerecorded video media through staffed locations, kiosks, or delivery channels and whose normalized EBITDA falls within the specified lower-middle-market band. Consumer rental fees, memberships, late or extension fees, and ancillary physical-media revenue tied to repeat use of an owned disc inventory.

records.title: Small Electrical Appliance Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of small electric appliances, electric housewares, household fans, vacuums, floor-care machines, air cleaners, portable heaters, food-preparation devices, and similar products that repeatedly supply external private-label, OEM, distributor, and retail customers. Predominantly per-unit branded or private-label product sales to retailers, distributors, and OEMs; qualifying outsourced-service revenue is concentrated in recurring contract manufacturing, product configuration, testing, and lifecycle support.

records.title: Photofinishing Laboratories (except One-Hour)
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market laboratories serving external consumer, professional, and commercial customers by developing still film or producing photographic prints, slides, enlargements, and related physical photo products on other than a one-hour basis, including repeat online and mail-order fulfillment. Customers submit film or digital image files and pay per processing order, print, enlargement, album, or other photo product, with additional charges sometimes applied for correction, retouching, premium materials, mounting, or expedited fulfillment. Professional accounts can generate repeat orders, but most revenue is transaction based rather than contracted recurring revenue.

records.title: Other Community Housing Services
records.search_text: Privately controllable operators providing recurring or repeat transitional-housing, low-income home-repair, housing-stabilization, or closely associated support services to governments, nonprofit prime contractors, institutional partners, or households, within the lower-middle-market EBITDA band. Revenue is typically a blend of competitively awarded government grants or subcontracts, reimbursable program funding, institutional service agreements, donations, and limited participant or homeowner payments. Work may combine housing operations, case management, compliance reporting, referral coordination, and field repair delivery.

records.title: Nuclear Electric Power Generation
records.search_text: Independent LMM operators of commercially licensed nuclear generation facilities that repeatedly provide electricity and capacity to external transmission systems, distribution utilities, markets, or contracted customers. Recurring regulated, merchant, capacity, or long-term contracted revenue from licensed baseload nuclear generation, with extensive operations, maintenance, security, radiation-protection, fuel, and compliance obligations.

records.title: Pulp Mills
records.search_text: Independent US market-pulp mills that repeatedly supply external paper, paperboard, tissue, specialty-cellulose, or other fiber customers and could transfer as an operating control investment in the target EBITDA band. Revenue is tonnage-based commodity or specification-grade pulp sales under spot, formula, and recurring supply arrangements; it is repeat B2B product supply rather than an outsourced service fee.

records.title: Major Household Appliance Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market manufacturers of household cooking, laundry, refrigeration, dishwashing, water-heating, disposal, and other major appliances that repeatedly supply external private-label, OEM, distributor, builder, and retail customers. Predominantly per-unit branded or private-label product sales through retailers, distributors, builders, and OEM programs; qualifying outsourced-service revenue is limited to recurring contract manufacturing, configuration, testing, and lifecycle support.

records.title: Phosphatic Fertilizer Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms repeatedly manufacturing or reacting phosphatic fertilizer materials for external customers under toll, private-label, or customer-specific nutrient programs. Recurring industrial or agricultural supply priced per ton, batch, or contracted capacity, with specifications for phosphate content and product form and periodic phosphate rock, sulfur, ammonia, energy, or freight repricing.

records.title: Petroleum Refineries
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market petroleum-refining firms that operate accountable processing assets and earn recurring external-customer revenue, including merchant or specialty refiners and any genuine toll-processing arrangements. Primarily commodity and specialty petroleum-product sales to distributors, marketers, industrial users, airlines, and other external buyers; qualifying tolling or processing fees are included where present.

records.title: Monetary Authorities-Central Bank
records.search_text: US establishments classified as monetary authorities-central bank that repeatedly provide monetary-policy, reserve-account, supervision, currency, settlement, payment, and fiscal-agent services to depository institutions and the federal government, assessed against the default lower-middle-market control lens. The Federal Reserve System performs statutory public functions, earns interest on central-bank assets, receives reimbursement for fiscal-agent work, and charges depository institutions for certain payment services under a long-run cost-recovery framework; it is not a conventional profit-seeking outsourced-services market.

records.title: Iron Ore Mining
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operating companies that develop and run iron ore mines and associated concentrating, beneficiating, pelletizing, sintering, or agglomerating facilities and repeatedly sell iron ore products to external steelmaking customers. Revenue is generated by bulk sales of ore, concentrate, pellets, or agglomerates under spot, index-linked, surcharge, or multi-year supply arrangements. Economics depend on shipped tonnage, grade and pellet specifications, benchmark and negotiated prices, freight, energy, mine life, and customer or steel-cycle demand.

records.title: Soybean Farming
records.search_text: Independent U.S. farm operating businesses repeatedly planting, managing, harvesting, storing, marketing, and selling soybeans to external elevators, processors, exporters, cooperatives, or other buyers, within the specified EBITDA band. Recurring seasonal commodity sales by bushel through cash markets, cooperatives, forward or marketing contracts, futures-linked arrangements, and specialty or identity-preserved contracts, with prices shaped by national and global supply, demand, quality, basis, delivery, and storage.

records.title: Oilseed (except Soybean) Farming
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market farms growing canola or rapeseed, sunflower, flaxseed, mustard seed, safflower, and mixed non-soy oilseeds, emphasizing scaled field-crop operations, specialty varieties, identity-preserved or certified production, seed production, and repeat processor or buyer programs. Customers include crushers, vegetable-oil and biofuel processors, seed companies, food and bird-feed businesses, exporters, and commodity merchants. Revenue is primarily seasonal crop sales; the service-like subset uses pre-harvest marketing or production contracts, verified identity preservation, specified varieties and qualities, delivery schedules, and repeat processor relationships.

records.title: Dry Pea and Bean Farming
records.search_text: Independently managed US commercial farms primarily growing dry peas, dry beans, lentils, or closely classified pulse crops and repeatedly selling harvested crops to external elevators, processors, packers, exporters, food manufacturers, or other buyers. Revenue is predominantly seasonal crop sales priced per hundredweight, bushel, pound, or contracted acreage/output, with economics driven by planted acres, yield, grade and quality, commodity or contract pricing, and crop rotation. Customers recur, but the product is an agricultural commodity rather than an outsourced service.

records.title: Wheat Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market wheat-growing or wheat-seed operations that repeatedly provide externally contracted, customer-dedicated production or managed growing services. Potentially eligible firms earn repeat revenue through production contracts, managed growing, or customer-specific seed and grain arrangements; ordinary merchant grain sales at cash or futures-linked prices are not by themselves outsourced services.

records.title: Corn Farming
records.search_text: Independent, professionally operated corn farms and seed-corn producers whose principal activity is growing field corn, silage corn, or corn seed, with sufficient controlled acreage, equipment, storage, staff, leases, and customer relationships to be evaluated as transferable operating businesses. Seasonal commodity and contract revenue from grain elevators, ethanol plants, feed mills, livestock operations, processors, exporters, and seed companies. Economics depend on acreage, yield, basis and futures prices, crop mix, land and equipment cost, input purchasing, crop insurance and government programs, storage, logistics, and weather.

records.title: Rice Farming
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market owner-operated, tenant-operated, and sharecropped farms primarily growing rice other than wild rice or producing rice seed, with recurring third-party crop sales and any genuinely contracted cultivation or water-management services identified at the firm level. Revenue is predominantly seasonal sales of an agricultural commodity or seed to mills, merchandisers, cooperatives, and other buyers; contracted farming or management revenue may occur but is not the typical model.

records.title: Oilseed and Grain Combination Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent commercial farms that grow a combination of oilseeds and grains with no single crop family representing at least half of production value and repeatedly sell harvested crops or seed to external elevators, processors, merchandisers, cooperatives, or other customers. Recurring annual commodity and seed sales, usually priced against transparent regional and global markets and delivered under spot, forward, hedged, cooperative, or production contracts; revenue varies with acres, yields, crop mix, quality, basis, and commodity prices.

records.title: All Other Grain Farming
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms, if any, providing repeat outsourced operation and management of barley, oat, rye, sorghum, wild-rice, or other 111199 grain production for external landowners or program customers while materially operating the farm. The qualifying model would use recurring farm-management, crop-share, acreage, or production agreements with accountability for a full growing program. Public sources describe commodity production but do not establish a defensible population or contract model for outsourced operators inside 111199.

records.title: Potato Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market farms repeatedly growing ware and/or seed potatoes for external processors, packers, wholesalers, retailers, or other growers, including recurring seasonal delivery under marketing or production arrangements. Annual crop sales through processor and buyer contracts, cooperatives, negotiated marketing arrangements, or spot channels, with payment based on quantity, grade, variety, delivery timing, and market or formula pricing; seed-potato growers sell recurring certified inputs to farms.

records.title: Other Vegetable (except Potato) and Melon Farming
records.search_text: Independent U.S. farms primarily growing open-field vegetables other than potatoes and melons for repeat sale to packers, processors, wholesalers, retailers, foodservice buyers, or direct-market customers. Repeat seasonal and program sales of harvested vegetables or melons to external commercial customers, including contracted processing acreage and recurring fresh-market relationships, screened for transferable operations plausibly generating $1-10 million normalized EBITDA.

records.title: Orange Groves
records.search_text: U.S. private lower-middle-market owner-operators of commercial orange groves producing fruit for fresh sale or processing through recurring annual crop cycles, including grove scouting, irrigation, nutrition, pest and disease management, pruning, mowing, harvest coordination, and farm administration. Growers sell fresh or processing oranges by box, weight, grade, or contracted crop to packing houses, marketers, cooperatives, and juice processors. Revenue recurs with each harvest but varies sharply with yield, fruit quality, disease, storms, water, labor, market channel, imports, and realized grower prices.

records.title: Citrus (except Orange) Groves
records.search_text: Independent LMM growers and grove operators of grapefruit, lemons, limes, mandarins, tangerines, and other non-orange citrus that repeatedly sell fruit to external packers, processors, distributors, retailers, or contracted buyers. Seasonal but recurring crop sales under spot, marketing-cooperative, processor, packinghouse, retail, or supply-contract arrangements, supported by grove management, harvesting, compliance, and quality grading.

records.title: Apple Orchards
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operating companies whose primary establishment activity is growing apples and selling the harvested crop to external packers, processors, wholesalers, retailers, or direct customers, including orchard-level harvest and farm-gate preparation performed by the grower. Seasonal crop revenue from fresh-market and processing apples sold through contracts, spot channels, cooperatives, wholesalers, retailers, and direct-to-consumer channels. Buyer relationships may repeat annually, but the economic output is a physical crop rather than a billed outsourced service.

records.title: Grape Vineyards
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market commercial vineyard operators that grow grapes and repeatedly sell crops to external wineries, packers, processors, distributors, or retailers while retaining control of vineyard operations. Recurring seasonal grape sales under annual or multi-year grower, winery, packing, processing, or wholesale relationships, with price determined by contract, variety and quality terms, or spot conditions.

records.title: Strawberry Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market farms primarily growing strawberries for recurring external sale to shippers, packers, wholesalers, retailers, processors, foodservice distributors, branded marketers, or direct commercial customers, including farm-level planning, cultivation, harvest, cooling coordination, food-safety records, and sales administration. Seasonal but repeat product revenue from fresh and processing strawberries sold through contracts, marketer or shipper programs, spot channels, cooperatives, and direct accounts, generally priced per flat, tray, pound, or hundredweight with quality, grade, timing, cooling, and rejection risk.

records.title: Berry (except Strawberry) Farming
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market berry farms operating under recurring production contracts in which an external contractor owns or directs the commodity and compensates the grower for cultivation services. Recurring or repeat production-service fees paid by processors, marketers, or other contractors for growing specified berries under contractor production requirements; ordinary farm-gate commodity sales are outside the fixed service lens.

records.title: Tree Nut Farming
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market commercial farms primarily growing almonds, pistachios, walnuts, pecans, hazelnuts, macadamias, and other tree nuts for repeated sale to external handlers, processors, cooperatives, wholesalers, exporters, retailers, or food manufacturers. Recurring seasonal crop sales under spot, pool, cooperative, handler, processor, export, or contracted arrangements, with yield, grade, variety, and commodity price determining revenue.

records.title: Fruit and Tree Nut Combination Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market farm operators that grow a combination of fruits and tree nuts, with no one fruit family or tree-nut family accounting for half of agricultural production value, and repeatedly supply external packers, processors, cooperatives, wholesalers, retailers, foodservice buyers, or consumers while retaining accountable responsibility for land, crops, labor, compliance, and delivery. Revenue comes from repeated seasonal crop sales through spot markets, cooperatives, marketing contracts, processors, packing houses, wholesale channels, retailers, foodservice buyers, and direct-to-consumer outlets. Price depends on crop, grade, timing, yield, contract terms, storage, certification, and market conditions; diversified farms can sell several crops across different harvest windows.

records.title: Other Noncitrus Fruit Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market growers of peaches, cherries, pears, avocados, bananas, coffee, dates, figs, pineapples, prunes, and other noncitrus fruits that repeatedly provide external customers with contract-growing, customer-specific crop programs, or another substantive outsourced production service. Eligible farms earn repeat B2B revenue through contracted production or customer-specific growing programs for packers, processors, brands, retailers, foodservice buyers, or institutions; fruit delivery remains the output, but the relationship must include substantive outsourced production responsibility rather than simple repeat sales.

records.title: Mushroom Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market establishments growing edible mushrooms under cover in mines, underground structures, or other controlled environments, including cultivation, climate monitoring, harvesting, grading, packing, food-safety compliance, sales, and distribution coordination. Growers earn primarily per-pound crop revenue through recurring wholesale sales of fresh or processed mushrooms to distributors, retailers, foodservice buyers, and processors; specialty growers may add direct restaurant or consumer channels.

records.title: Other Food Crops Grown Under Cover
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operating businesses repeatedly growing and selling vegetables, fruits, melons, sprouts, hydroponic crops, and other food crops except mushrooms under greenhouses, vertical farms, high tunnels, or other protective cover to external customers. Repeat wholesale and direct sales to grocers, foodservice distributors, processors, institutions, produce markets, and consumers, priced by crop, grade, package, season, contract, delivery, freshness, local attributes, and prevailing fresh-produce markets.

records.title: Nursery and Tree Production
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market wholesale and mixed-channel growers of nursery stock, ornamental shrubs and trees, fruit and nut plants, Christmas trees, sod, and short-rotation woody crops, emphasizing propagation, contract growing, broad inventories, climate-suitable varieties, delivery coordination, and repeat landscaper, retailer, municipal, and institutional accounts. Customers include landscape contractors, garden centers, home-improvement retailers, wholesalers, municipalities, institutions, orchards, forestry programs, and direct consumers. Revenue is earned per plant, tree, pallet, acreage unit, or crop lot; the service-like subset combines forward growing plans, reserved inventory, propagation, specifications, tagging, staged releases, logistics, and repeat seasonal replenishment.

records.title: Floriculture Production
records.search_text: Independently managed US commercial growers primarily producing cut flowers and roses, florist greens, potted flowering and foliage plants, bedding and garden plants, and floriculture propagative material under cover or in open fields, with repeat sales to external wholesalers, retailers, landscapers, florists, institutions, or consumers. Revenue comes from seasonal and recurring wholesale or retail sales of live plants, flowers, greens, and unfinished propagation material. Pricing reflects species and cultivar, format, quality, timing, availability, customer channel, and contract or spot terms; biological inventory is perishable and demand peaks around planting and gift occasions.

records.title: Tobacco Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market tobacco-growing operations that repeatedly provide customer-directed production services under contracts where the buyer meaningfully controls specifications, inputs, practices, or crop ownership. Eligible firms are paid repeat growing or production fees by tobacco companies or other external contractors with meaningful production involvement; common marketing-contract sales of grower-owned leaf are not treated as outsourced services merely because they are contracted before harvest.

records.title: Cotton Farming
records.search_text: Independent, professionally operated farms primarily growing Upland or Pima cotton, including businesses with transferable land access, irrigation systems, harvesting equipment, labor arrangements, and established ginning and marketing relationships. Revenue comes mainly from selling baled cotton lint and cottonseed into commodity and contract channels, with realized prices affected by fiber grade, staple characteristics, basis, marketing arrangements, and agricultural support programs.

records.title: Sugarcane Farming
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market farms primarily growing sugarcane for third-party mills or processors, including owner-operated and tenant-operated acreage and any genuinely contracted cultivation or crop-management services at the firm level. Revenue is mainly seasonal sale of harvested cane under processor or mill arrangements, with value linked to tonnage and sugar recovery; contract farming or management can occur but is not the standard industry output.

records.title: Hay Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent commercial farms primarily growing hay, alfalfa, clover, or mixed hay and repeatedly selling baled, chopped, or otherwise harvested forage to external dairy, beef, equine, feed, export, or other customers. Recurring seasonal and annual forage sales by ton or bale, differentiated by species, cutting, moisture, protein, digestibility, bale form, certification, location, and delivery; transactions range from repeat direct relationships to brokered or spot sales.

records.title: Sugar Beet Farming
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms, if any, providing repeat outsourced operation and management of sugar-beet production for external landowners or program customers while materially operating the farm. The qualifying model would use recurring farm-operation, crop-share, acreage, or management agreements with responsibility for a full beet-growing program. Sugar-beet production commonly uses processor-linked crop sales contracts, but those contracts coordinate commodity sale and are not evidence of outsourced operating-service revenue.

records.title: Peanut Farming
records.search_text: U.S. peanut-growing establishments in the lower-middle-market band that repeatedly provide accountable crop-production services to an external contractor, especially arrangements in which the contractor owns the commodity during production. Recurring seasonal production fees or economically equivalent grower compensation paid by an external contractor for field execution, quality compliance, and delivery; this is distinct from a farm simply selling its own commodity.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous Crop Farming
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market 111998 growers that repeatedly provide crop-production services under production contracts in which an external contractor owns the crop or key inputs, directs production, and pays the grower a fee; the most plausible included cases are contract seed growers and similar specialty-crop operators. Recurring production fees paid by seed companies, processors, or other crop owners under agreements specifying production practices, quantity or acreage, quality, and delivery; ordinary crop-sale revenue is excluded.

records.title: Beef Cattle Ranching and Farming
records.search_text: U.S. private lower-middle-market commercial cow-calf, seedstock, stocker and backgrounding ranches primarily engaged in raising beef cattle, including grazing and herd management, breeding, calving, animal health, water and fence management, forage planning and recurring cattle sales. Ranches sell calves, breeding stock, cull animals and stocker cattle by head or weight through auctions, dealers, video or direct contracts to other producers and feedlots. Revenue recurs with biological production cycles but varies with calving rates, weights, herd retention, drought and forage, animal health, commodity prices, input costs and the timing of sales.

records.title: Cattle Feedlots
records.search_text: Independent LMM cattle feedlots that repeatedly provide custom finishing and related feeding, animal-care, recordkeeping, and marketing-support services for cattle owned by external ranchers, producers, or investors. Recurring production contracts with cattle owners, typically combining per-head or per-day yardage, feed and input charges or markups, animal-health charges, and performance reporting; cattle may be marketed to packers for the owner.

records.title: Dairy Cattle and Milk Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market operating companies primarily engaged in milking dairy cattle and repeatedly selling raw milk to external processors, handlers, cooperatives, or direct customers, including on-farm herd care, feeding, milking, manure management, and crop activity integral to milk production. Recurring milk shipments generate product revenue, commonly through cooperative or processor relationships and regulated or formula-linked pricing, with secondary revenue from cattle, calves, manure, crops, and premiums for components, quality, organic status, or other attributes.

records.title: Dual-Purpose Cattle Ranching and Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market operators that raise cattle for both milking and meat production and repeatedly sell milk, calves, cull cows, or finished cattle to external cooperatives, processors, feeders, auctions, or packers. Recurring milk sales and periodic cattle sales through cooperatives, processors, livestock auctions, dealers, feeders, or packers, with the farm retaining animal-care, land, production, food-safety, and environmental accountability.

records.title: Hog and Pig Farming
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market commercial hog and pig farms with recurring external revenue from breeding, farrowing, nursery, feeder-pig, finishing, or farrow-to-finish production sold to integrators, processors, livestock buyers, or other farms, including independent and production-contract growers. Repeat livestock revenue through formula or negotiated hog sales, marketing contracts, packer programs, and production contracts that pay growers for housing and caring for pigs owned by an integrator, with economics affected by feed, productivity, mortality, weight, quality, facility terms, and environmental compliance.

records.title: Chicken Egg Production
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market chicken egg farms operating under recurring production contracts in which an integrator or contractor owns the flock or output and pays the grower to provide housing, equipment, labor, utilities, husbandry, and production execution. Recurring production-service fees from an egg contractor or integrator, potentially adjusted for flock performance, quality, mortality, efficiency, housing standards, and contract compliance.

records.title: Broilers and Other Meat Type Chicken Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market independent contract growers raising broilers, fryers, roasters, and other meat-type chickens for external poultry integrators, with growers providing housing, equipment, utilities, labor, and daily husbandry. Repeated flock-placement production contracts, commonly with one local integrator, paying grower fees based on delivered live weight, contract base rates, and relative flock performance while the integrator owns birds and supplies feed, veterinary services, and transportation.

records.title: Turkey Production
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent turkey growers and contract growers that repeatedly raise turkeys for meat or egg production for external integrators, processors, cooperatives, or buyers while retaining accountable responsibility for houses, equipment, labor, utilities, daily husbandry, welfare, biosecurity, environmental compliance, and flock performance. Contract growers receive recurring per-flock or performance-linked fees from integrators that commonly own the birds and provide feed, veterinary support, transport, and technical standards, while growers provide houses, equipment, labor, utilities, and husbandry. Independent growers instead own production inputs and sell live birds or eggs under marketing arrangements or spot conditions.

records.title: Poultry Hatcheries
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market poultry hatchery operators that repeatedly provide incubation, hatching, chick processing, vaccination, sexing, quality control, and related chick-delivery services to external poultry breeders, growers, farms, and other commercial customers. Customers are poultry breeders, layer and broiler producers, independent growers, specialty farms, and distributors; revenue is earned through repeat hatching and chick-supply orders, often priced per egg set, chick delivered, vaccination or processing service, or contracted batch.

records.title: Other Poultry Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market contract growers that repeatedly raise non-chicken, non-turkey poultry such as ducks, geese, quail, pheasants, emus, and ostriches for external integrators or processors, providing housing, equipment, utilities, husbandry, biosecurity, records, and labor for a grow-out fee. The coherent outsourced-service model is recurring production-contract revenue from an integrator or processor, commonly linked to delivered bird or live-weight performance and sometimes adjusted for mortality, feed conversion, or relative results. Independent spot and product sales exist in the NAICS code but fall outside the fixed service lens.

records.title: Sheep Farming
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operating farms and ranches repeatedly raising sheep and lambs or feeding lambs for external sale, including wool production, within the specified EBITDA band. Recurring sales of feeder and slaughter lambs, breeding stock, cull animals, wool, and related byproducts to auctions, feedlots, processors, marketers, cooperatives, textile buyers, restaurants, and direct customers, priced by live or carcass weight, grade, timing, wool quality, contracts, and market conditions.

records.title: Goat Farming
records.search_text: US goat-raising establishments in the lower-middle-market band that earn recurring or repeat external-customer revenue through production-style contracts, recurring commercial offtake, or comparable repeat relationships for milk, meat, breeding stock, or fiber. Eligible operators receive production fees or recurring proceeds from processors, distributors, dairies, breeders, or other commercial buyers; the typical NAICS establishment instead sells livestock or animal products and therefore may not satisfy the outsourced-service lens.

records.title: Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries
records.search_text: Privately owned US commercial operations primarily raising finfish or hatching fish and repeatedly selling food fish, fingerlings, eggs, baitfish, sport fish, ornamental fish, or stocking fish to external processors, distributors, retailers, restaurants, other farms, pond owners, or agencies. Revenue comes from repeated sales of live or harvested fish and eggs, generally priced per pound, head, thousand fish, lot, or supply contract. Food-fish economics depend on biomass growth, survival, feed conversion, harvest size, commodity prices, and processor demand, while hatchery, sport, bait, and ornamental products can earn species, genetics, size, health, timing, or service premiums.

records.title: Shellfish Farming
records.search_text: US shellfish-farming businesses that repeatedly supply farmed oysters, clams, mussels, shrimp, crayfish, or similar shellfish to external wholesale, processor, distributor, restaurant, retail, restoration, or contract customers and otherwise satisfy the screen's ownership and size conditions. Revenue is mainly per-pound, per-bushel, per-count, or lot pricing for live or processed-at-harvest shellfish, sometimes supplemented by seed, nursery, restoration, or contract-growing revenue. Relationships may repeat, but prices and volumes remain exposed to biological yield, seasonality, commodity competition, and buyer mix.

records.title: Other Aquaculture
records.search_text: Independent U.S. lower-middle-market establishments in NAICS 112519 that repeatedly provide contracted cultivation, growing, stocking, or related aquaculture production for external customers while raising aquatic plants or aquatic animals other than finfish and shellfish. Eligible firms earn recurring contract-production or cultivation fees and may also sell algae, seaweed, alligators, frogs, turtles, or other biological output under repeat supply agreements. Contracts may be fixed-price, unit-priced, cost-plus, or tied to survival, weight, quality, or delivery specifications.

records.title: Apiculture
records.search_text: Lower-middle-market commercial beekeeping operations raising and managing colonies, especially recurring pollination-service providers to external crop producers, together with associated honey, queen, package-bee, nucleus-colony, beeswax, propolis, and other bee-product revenue. Qualifying operators contract colonies to growers for seasonal but repeat pollination services, often moving hives among crop regions, while also earning product revenue from honey, bees, queens, nucleus colonies, wax, and related outputs.

records.title: Horses and Other Equine Production
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent commercial establishments primarily breeding and raising horses, ponies, mules, donkeys, or other equines for repeated sales of foals, breeding stock, performance or working animals, semen, stud services, or other equine products. Sales through private treaty, agents, breed and discipline networks, online listings, and auctions, supplemented by stud fees, semen, and related breeding products; prices are highly differentiated by pedigree, conformation, health, age, training, performance, reputation, and buyer segment.

records.title: Fur-Bearing Animal and Rabbit Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms, if any, providing repeat outsourced breeding, raising, husbandry, or production of rabbits or other fur-bearing animals for external animal owners, research programs, processors, or brands. The qualifying model would use recurring per-animal, capacity, husbandry, breeding, or production agreements with accountability for animal care, biosecurity, records, and delivery. Public sources document animal and pelt production but do not establish an outsourced-service population or representative contracts inside 112930.

records.title: All Other Animal Production
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market laboratory-animal producers that repeatedly manage customer-dedicated breeding colonies or provide contract breeding and cohort-production services for external biomedical-research customers. Recurring or repeat project fees for customer-directed colony establishment, breeding, husbandry, genotyping coordination, health-status control, and delivery of specified research cohorts, typically under a statement of work or dedicated-supply arrangement.

records.title: Other Marine Fishing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market commercial operators catching or taking marine animals other than finfish and shellfish from natural habitat for recurring external sale to dealers, processors, wholesalers, foodservice, research, bait, or specialty-product buyers, including vessels, crews, permits, catch handling, and reporting. Trip- and season-based repeat revenue from landed catch sold by weight, grade, species, auction, dealer settlement, processor contract, or specialty order, constrained by permits, seasons, areas, quotas, protected-species rules, weather, stock availability, and vessel economics.

records.title: Surface Coal Mining
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market surface coal operators and beneficiators, emphasizing mines serving adjacent power or industrial facilities under long-term cost-reimbursement, management-fee, requirements, or indexed supply agreements, together with specialized high-quality coal programs where reliability, consistent specifications, and mine-to-customer logistics matter. Customers include electric generators, industrial facilities, and steel producers. Revenue is typically recognized per ton delivered under spot or term supply contracts; the service-like subset earns inflation-linked management fees or agreed profit per ton under long-duration, dedicated-mine arrangements that may reimburse operating, capital, and reclamation costs.

records.title: Underground Coal Mining
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market underground bituminous and anthracite coal operators and integrated beneficiators, emphasizing high-quality metallurgical, high-calorific-value thermal, and industrial coal sold through repeat domestic or export programs, multi-year contracts, exacting quality specifications, and coordinated rail, barge, or terminal logistics. Customers include domestic and foreign steel and coke producers, electric generators, and industrial users. Revenue is earned per ton under spot, annual, or multi-year agreements, with coal quality, blending, reliable delivery, technical coordination, and transport execution creating repeat program economics rather than pure service revenue.

records.title: Gold Ore and Silver Ore Mining
records.search_text: Independent U.S. operating companies that develop and run gold or silver mines and associated crushing, grinding, beneficiation, concentration, leaching, recovery, and mine-linked doré production, with recurring sales to external smelters, refiners, traders, or offtake customers. Revenue comes from deliveries of doré, concentrate, carbon material, or payable metal under spot-linked, assay-settled, hedged, streaming, royalty-burdened, or offtake arrangements. Economics depend on recoverable ounces, grade, recovery, payability, treatment and refining charges, metal prices, mine life, sustaining capital, and reclamation obligations.

records.title: Other Metal Ore Mining
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market firms classified in development, extraction, or beneficiation of metal ores other than iron, gold, silver, copper, nickel, lead, and zinc that repeatedly provide externally contracted or customer-dedicated mining, custom milling, or concentrate-supply operations. Potentially eligible revenue comes from repeat contract operation, custom beneficiation, toll milling, customer-dedicated processing, or service-like long-term supply arrangements involving ores such as uranium-vanadium, molybdenum, tungsten, tantalum, antimony, ilmenite, and related specialty metals; ordinary merchant sales of owned ore are not by themselves outsourced services.

records.title: Kaolin, Clay, and Ceramic and Refractory Minerals Mining
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms, if any, providing repeat outsourced mine-site development, extraction, or basic beneficiation of kaolin, ball clay, common clay, bentonite, fuller's earth, fire clay, and ceramic or refractory minerals for external mineral owners or industrial customers. Multi-period B2B operating or toll-beneficiation arrangements priced by ton, campaign, capacity, or fixed operating fee, with repeat extraction, washing, screening, grinding, sizing, quality-control, and load-out obligations.

records.title: Other Nonmetallic Mineral Mining and Quarrying
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market firms, if any, supplying repeat outsourced mine-site development, extraction, milling, or beneficiation of nonmetallic minerals such as barite, natural borates, phosphate rock, potash, peat, rock salt, and dry-lake brines for external mineral owners or industrial customers. The required service model would consist of repeat operating or toll-beneficiation contracts priced by ton, campaign, capacity, or fixed operating fee. Public classification evidence indicates that contract or fee-based mining support ordinarily belongs outside 212390, so no in-code revenue model was verified.

records.title: Cane Sugar Manufacturing
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market independent cane mills or refineries repeatedly providing toll milling, toll refining, or comparable outsourced conversion services to external cane growers, raw-sugar owners, or food customers. Recurring conversion fees or processing margins for transforming customer-directed cane or raw sugar into raw or refined sugar; the eligible operator is paid for accountable manufacturing rather than merely selling its own sugar inventory.

records.title: Apparel Knitting Mills
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market apparel knitting mills that repeatedly manufacture knit garments or knit fabric-and-garment combinations for external brands, designers, uniform programs, institutions, and private-label customers under contract, replenishment, or recurring production arrangements. Repeat B2B contract, private-label, replenishment, small-batch, and on-demand production, generally quoted per unit or production run with customer specifications, samples, purchase orders, quality requirements, and delivery commitments.

records.title: Cut and Sew Apparel Manufacturing (except Contractors)
records.search_text: US lower-middle-market full-package, OEM, and private-label cut-and-sew manufacturers that buy fabric and repeatedly supply custom or customer-branded apparel to external brands, uniform programs, distributors, institutions, or other business customers. Repeat business-to-business private-label or OEM supply, commonly quoted per style and unit as an all-in price covering purchased materials, construction, decoration, labels, packaging, and delivery, with sampling and repeat production runs.

records.title: Other Leather and Allied Product Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent US contract cut-and-sew and assembly manufacturers that repeatedly produce customer-designed bags, carrying cases, pouches, straps, harnesses, and related soft goods from leather, textiles, plastics, or substitutes for external brands, OEMs, distributors, medical, industrial, outdoor, defense, or government customers. Repeat B2B contract, OEM, or private-label programs priced per unit, batch, or purchase order, often bundling design-for-manufacture, prototyping, sourcing, cutting, sewing, assembly, quality control, branding, packaging, and fulfillment.

records.title: Engineered Wood Member Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent U.S. manufacturers repeatedly supplying wood roof and floor trusses, I-joists, laminated veneer lumber, parallel strand lumber, glulam arches and beams, finger-jointed lumber, and related fabricated or laminated structural members to external customers. Repeat standardized and project-specific member sales to builders, component dealers, distributors, lumberyards, developers, fabricators, and commercial construction teams, often combining product pricing with design tables, shop drawings, takeoffs, and delivery coordination.

records.title: Paper Mills
records.search_text: Independent US paper mills that make paper from purchased or internally produced pulp and repeatedly supply external tissue, packaging-paper, printing-writing, technical, or specialty-paper customers, with transferable standalone operations in the target band. Revenue is primarily tonnage, roll, sheet, or grade-based product sales under spot, formula, and recurring supply arrangements; it is repeat B2B supply rather than an outsourced service fee.

records.title: Compost Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market compost producers that repeatedly accept organic feedstock from external customers and sell finished compost or soil amendments. Recurring tipping or processing fees from waste generators and haulers, supplemented by sales of compost to agriculture, landscaping, construction, and soil-restoration customers.

records.title: All Other Industrial Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of otherwise-unclassified industrial machinery that repeatedly supply external factories through standard or engineered equipment, replacement parts, tooling, controls upgrades, rebuilds, and technical support. Quoted and purchase-order sales of engineered capital equipment across chemical, plastics, rubber, textile, printing, additive, surface-treatment, kiln, and numerous other processes, with recurring revenue from repeat models, spares, wear parts, rebuilds, retrofits, controls, and support.

records.title: Commercial and Service Industry Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: US manufacturers in this code with repeat equipment programs, installed-base parts, upgrades, maintenance, diagnostics, or support sold to external commercial and service customers, where a transferable operating company can plausibly fall in the lower-middle-market band. Customers include laundries, food-service operators, retailers, offices, automotive service businesses, entertainment venues, and other commercial users. Revenue combines equipment sales with repeat model orders and, for some firms, aftermarket parts, upgrades, field service, software, and support tied to an installed base.

records.title: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing
records.search_text: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of industrial separation, screening, sifting, centrifuge, and filtration machinery that provide repeat aftermarket parts, replacement elements or screens, rebuilds, or ongoing service to external industrial and municipal customers. The coherent sub-lens sells engineered capital equipment through quotes and applications work, followed by recurring replacement screens, filter elements, wear parts, maintenance, field service, rebuilds, and emergency support over a long installed-base life.

records.title: Manufacturing and Reproducing Magnetic and Optical Media
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market establishments principally manufacturing blank magnetic or optical media or mass duplicating and packaging audio, video, software, and other data on magnetic, optical, and similar media. The lens includes enterprise archival tape media as well as prerecorded physical-media replication. Revenue comes mainly from manufactured media units and contract duplication or packaging runs sold to enterprise-storage customers, publishers, labels, studios, distributors, government and archival buyers, and specialty physical-media customers. Revenue is generally order- or volume-based, with repeat demand tied to releases, data growth, replacement, and archival policies.

records.title: Residential Electric Lighting Fixture Manufacturing
records.search_text: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of fixed and portable residential electric lighting fixtures and metal, paper, or textile lamp shades, including indoor and outdoor ceiling, wall, pendant, recessed, track, table, floor, low-voltage, solar, decorative, and residential grow-light fixtures. Product revenue from homebuilders, electrical and lighting distributors, retailers, e-commerce channels, designers, remodelers, and homeowners. Repeat demand comes from new construction, renovation, style refreshes, replacement, and product-line introductions; branded and specified products can earn better margins than private-label or commodity fixtures.

records.title: Commercial, Industrial, and Institutional Electric Lighting Fixture Manufacturing
